"campaign_trail.answer_score_state", "pk": 88940, "fields": { "answer": 85129, "state": 1101, "candidate": 301, "affected_candidate": 50001, "state_multiplier": 0.1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_score_state", "pk": 88941, "fields": { "answer": 85129, "state": 1104, "candidate": 301, "affected_candidate": 50001, "state_multiplier": 0.1 } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_score_state", "pk": 88942, "fields": { "answer": 85129, "state": 1110, "candidate": 301, "affected_candidate": 50001, "state_multiplier": 0.06 } } ]; campaignTrail_temp.answer_feedback_json = [ { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3004, "fields": { "answer": 6204, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "History is written by the victors. Good luck, Mr President." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3005, "fields": { "answer": 6205, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "History is written by the victors. Good luck, Mr President." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3006, "fields": { "answer": 6206, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "History is written by the victors. Good luck, Mr President." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3007, "fields": { "answer": 6207, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "History is written by the victors. Good luck, Mr President." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3058, "fields": { "answer": 6109, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "He nods along. \"I agree with you Bernard!\" It feels like you're already making headway in such a chaotic administration. \"You and I will make a great team, possibly one of the greatest!\" He smirks at you. You know even agreeing with this man will jeopardize your career. You don't care, you want what's best for America, even if it goes against what you desire." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3059, "fields": { "answer": 6110, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Donald perks up, \"Sure, sure, our workers, terrific people by the way, they need work and we’re gonna give it to them!\" His words gnaw into you, you feel like he's playing you, and he probably is, but if it gets you what you want, then so be it. Hopefully when this is all said and done you can retire to your quiet camp on Grand Isle...\n" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3060, "fields": { "answer": 6111, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "The President goes on and on about the 'MILITARY INDUSTRIAL COMPLEX' and how America is indebted beyond repair. You agree on the premise, yet something feels off. This is the same Republican Party which brought us into Iraq and Afghanistan, and you worry Trump will do the same. Hopefully, your instinct is wrong." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3061, "fields": { "answer": 6112, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "\"Bernie, I want you to visit the European Union, they'll love you!\" Donald looks eager. Too eager. Is there some reason he wants you out of the White House? You’ll do as he asks, but it’ll take a miracle before you trust him." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3062, "fields": { "answer": 6113, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie gives you a cold, almost dead stare. You smirk. You know you hold the cards. As the votes are counted, it looks to be another tie, but Manchin, that so-called 'Democrat' -- a DINO, really-- casts the deciding vote. You feel a sigh of relief. Bernie is fuming with rage, but after all, you’re the President and he’s not." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3063, "fields": { "answer": 6115, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Disbelief. He couldn't believe his ears. Bernie almost jumped up with such enthusiasm. She’s from Vermont as well, what a coincidence…you both know you’re throwing a bone to Bernie, and he bites. \"Thank you, Mr President.\" Why is he always so formal? \"Call me Donald, Bernard.\" You think, maybe, just maybe, this can work. It all comes crumbling down when the New Democrats vote her down. You're secretly glad." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3064, "fields": { "answer": 6116, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You and Bernie both nod in agreement, thinking that compromise is the best solution to this. 10 year term limits, 2 new justices. You presume that you and Bernie will both get one to choose, or maybe that socialist nut will take both for himself. Regardless, these are presented to the Senate, and in a stunning display, they're attacked by both Democrats and Republicans. The 'UNIPARTY' strikes again." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3065, "fields": { "answer": 6117, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Although Bernie protests, he still goes. He hates it every second, having to sit down with the Russian oligarchs. You’re almost gleeful at his embarrassment, although a part of you wishes he did well. He tries to catch Sergey Lavrov off guard. He doesn't bite. Bernie continues to hold firm on America's stance of anti-expansionism and that Russia can't ignore international law. His words feel empty, as you contradict him in so many ways, and the Russians know this to be true." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3066, "fields": { "answer": 6119, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie agrees to stabilize the homefront. You almost seem at home when you're abroad. You enjoy this rather hectic trip, 'manning the ship' as you make grandiose claims of American revival across the Pacific. Fortunately for you, your meeting with Xi went well, no major hiccups. Then off to the Korean DMZ. You did it, you crossed over the line. Your heart stops for a brief moment, but all is well. It goes smoothly. Bernie's impressed you managed to keep it sane for a few short weeks." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3067, "fields": { "answer": 6120, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You love every moment, gloating over Theresa May and the 'success' America is having, while Britain struggles with Brexit. You almost feel pity for her, you know all about deadlocked elections. So much so you offer a generous trade deal with the UK, and she gladly accepts. You know you've won, and Britain is back in lockstep with the USA." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3068, "fields": { "answer": 6121, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie isn't happy, to say the least. His flagship policy is gone, just like that. As much as he fights and fights, you easily overpower him at every turn. You argue affordability, about how 'socialism' cant infect this country. Right now, he has nothing but contempt for you, but you see it as your moment of glory against 'COMMUNISM', even if you don't necessarily believe it..." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3069, "fields": { "answer": 6124, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": " It works - the people begin to rise up demanding their representatives give them what they want. Congress wants the mob controlled. You watch on Twitter, countless videos of protests erupting around the country. It makes you smile, while Bernie is worried about the implications. \"Don't worry\" you tell him, \"These are our people, we'll get what we want.\" and sure enough, a bill is presented for heavily subsidized healthcare. You won. Now, whether to call it Trumpcare, or Berniecare…" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3070, "fields": { "answer": 6125, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "The President's face lights up. \"Let's get this on the road Bernie, MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN!\" and with a stroke on a pen, he enacts tariffs across the board on hostile Regimes. China. Iran. Russia. All hit, and seemingly wanting to come back to the table. You see this as a victory for you and your own cause." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3071, "fields": { "answer": 6126, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Donald seems dismayed. \"Bernie, you want this just as much as I do, we've been exploited beyond belief!\", however, after much convincing, he allows you to draft your 'fair-trade plan'. It's a shambles. The machine in Washington is against this. The status quo, for now, remains unchanged. Congress continues to deadlock your agenda." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3072, "fields": { "answer": 6128, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "President Trump is ecstatic, and immediately starts getting the votes from Congress to whip it up. It works, and 'Liberation Day' is born. The UK, EU, Canada, and most of Asia are startled by this development. The effects begin to seep in immediately. You feel like you've been sucker punched. Costs are growing as countries begin to retaliate. It's over." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3074, "fields": { "answer": 6133, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "He gives you a disappointed look. You realise at this moment he really is powerless to really affect it, as finally Congress can back you on keeping the war machine alive. You feel a moment of victory, albeit bittersweet, as you're seen propping up a collapsing establishment." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3075, "fields": { "answer": 6136, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "The hawks in the cabinet almost jump for joy as they hear this. Bernie is despondent. How could you do this, he probably wonders. Doesn't matter. You're in charge. More money to the military, feeding the beast. A small win for you and the conservative base, but Bernie doesn't look at you the same. He's almost resentful." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3076, "fields": { "answer": 6138, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie shakes his head, but can't really refute your claim. \"Donald, I- we need to represent all Americans...\", his words go through one ear and out the other. It doesn't matter. You go to the South, lapping up the crowd. It feels bittersweet, not having your counterpart there with you. Almost feels...odd?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3077, "fields": { "answer": 6142, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You grin from ear to ear. You feel powerful at this moment, sticking it to those puppets in Washington. Bernie is tacit in his support as well, although not as...enthusiastic as you are. Some of the people you and Bernard endorsed made it into Congress, enough to deadlock the house, but you win nothing in the Senate. Tough luck, I suppose." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3078, "fields": { "answer": 6143, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie softens his tone, \"I suppose that is the best action...alright then.\" His head slightly down, you wonder why. Doesn't matter either way, as the endorsements get you nowhere. More deadlock and delay within your presidency, seems like when you anger the 'Uniparty', they don't want to work with you." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3079, "fields": { "answer": 6145, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "He listens to your every word, nodding along. \"Well Bernie, to be honest, I've never understood the need, but fine, fine, we'll do it your way.\" You did it...somehow. The next day, in a televised speech, Donald proudly claims he's for the 'working-class' and proudly 'stands behind Unions', its a miracle, and you made it happen. You feel overwhelming pride." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3080, "fields": { "answer": 6146, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "The President seems anxious to agree, citing that small businesses might be hurt by this. \"Donald, we'll help them through the process, trust me.\" He relents. \"Fine, Bernie, I'll agree, in the spirit of COMPROMISE.\" Soon, a bill is drafted and ultimately defeated by the narrowest of margins. You feel a pit in your stomach, and so does Mr Trump." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3081, "fields": { "answer": 6147, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "\"I will go - myself.\" He says in a firm tone. You look uneasy, but agree. The President travels to the Teamsters Union and heads up a meeting and to the surprise of many, it went smoothly. The press eat it up, and soon a bill is drafted by you. The \"Worker's Enhancement Act\" is passed, granting what you fought for. You feel proud, and Donald feels the same way." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3083, "fields": { "answer": 6152, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "$2000 is shovelled into the 'Covid Relief Act'. You gleam with excitement as it is pushed onto the Senate floor, WITHOUT student debt relief. Bernie opted out, he had to. There was no way it would have passed with it in, and you know that to be true. Regardless, it still fails. Deficit hawks shoot it down. A smaller bill, worth only $1000 in relief, is passed instead." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3084, "fields": { "answer": 6159, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Disaster struck. What happened? You went from gaffe to gaffe, and ended up making yourself an idiot and Donald even more so. He was fuming. He didn't speak to you for a few days after that, and the 'MEDIA' was all over it. This will dominate your campaign for a few days." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3085, "fields": { "answer": 6160, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernard allows you to choose the next Supreme Court Justice. You give out a small smile, \"Thank you Bernie, it means a lot, truly it does. We'll make her proud.\" You go with a woman, Barbara Lagoa. Bernie keeps his promise, albeit reluctantly, and votes her in for the tie-breaking vote once more." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3087, "fields": { "answer": 6164, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You go for your weekly check-up, turns out, you have COVID-19. You don't panic, you think to yourself, should it ever happen, the country is in safe hands with Bernard Sanders. No matter, you said yourself, you're the 'toughest', you weathered through it and pulled back stronger." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3088, "fields": { "answer": 6166, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie does as commanded. He goes from state to state, campaigning on what a journey it has been for the both of you. It's sort of sweet in a way, and you feel like you can call him a good friend. You stay at the White House for a few days after getting COVID-19, but you feel fine." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3090, "fields": { "answer": 6170, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie objects to the 'wording', but agrees in principle. In an instant, China is locked away from America. You feel safe knowing that the disease won't make it to America. But it does, and it terrifies the Administration. No one has any idea how to handle this. Bernie and Fauci work hand-in-hand to keep the people calm, but chaos roams." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3091, "fields": { "answer": 6172, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "For once, you feel like it might be best if you don't have the spotlight. You stay at the White House, as Bernie Sanders shakes Boris Johnson's hand (you bet he would've loved that radical Corbyn). You can tell he doesn't like him, he doesn't like any of them, bar Governor Trudeau. But he does his merry little duty, for you, of course. It goes as planned." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3092, "fields": { "answer": 6175, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You've blindsided NATO. Bernie is shocked also. He can't believe you've left them out in the dust. You say to him \"They'll come begging, believe me!\", and sure enough, Johnson's on the phone. He feels snubbed, and ultimately, begins the slow separation between the US and UK. So do many NATO allies, feeling betrayed." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3093, "fields": { "answer": 6176, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Donald nods. \"I agree, totally, these phonies will pay.\" He almost sounds resolute in the face of the so-called 'UNIPARTY'. However, it is all for naught. Trump, as much as he resonates with the idea, sticks to being a Republican, fearing his chances for 2020. Regardless, this sends a ripple throughout Washington, not since Washington has there been an independent in the Executive, and there's a reason for that. You and Donald are now stuck in a precarious situation." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3094, "fields": { "answer": 6180, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You allow Bernard to push this issue across America, and petitions happen across America. A slight smile appears, hopefully allowing you to break through a bit more after your performance at the Midterms. Illinois and Louisiana fold, but that's about it. You take it as a small win." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3095, "fields": { "answer": 6181, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "The \"Federal Department for Gerrymandering\" is born. You and Bernie obviously share different views on the matter, however you decide you both need to choose an outsider. Thomas Massie and newly elected Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez fill the role, and push for redistricting. It doesn't work, and the plan falls apart. The south remains divided, for now." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3096, "fields": { "answer": 6182, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You never thought it'd work, while Bernie was optimistic. Why? Is he dumb? Regardless, it falls through. Independent committees, while a thoughtful idea, prove to be useless as Republican majorities within these states shoot them down. Their power triumphs over fairness and justice." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3097, "fields": { "answer": 6183, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie doesn't appreciate this, as you both know the south won't do it. But you both try regardless, and surprisingly, the south is warm enough to you that they agree. Soon enough, states like Georgia and Florida follow through, and allow for a national movement based on fairness in representation. You both smile gleefully." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3098, "fields": { "answer": 6184, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Donald agrees, albeit with limited enthusiasm. The \"Tax Cuts & Jobs Act\" is born, and is passed with what little political capital you have left. You feel the need to celebrate, but knowing that the 1% still dominate politics worries you still. Hell, you're even working with one, but at least he feels authentic. Your people, they cheer your names, so you have that to be proud of." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3100, "fields": { "answer": 6188, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Pundits are unanimous that you seemed, by far, the less presidential of the two candidates. Many are skeptical, though, that it will have any effect in this environment." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3101, "fields": { "answer": 6189, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Pundits are unanimous that you seemed, by far, the less presidential of the two candidates. Many are skeptical, though, that it will have any effect in this environment." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3102, "fields": { "answer": 6190, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Pundits are unanimous that you seemed, by far, the less presidential of the two candidates. Many are skeptical, though, that it will have any effect in this environment." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3103, "fields": { "answer": 6191, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Maybe these debates have outlived their usefulness anyway. Still, there are some whispers that you may have made a mistake." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3104, "fields": { "answer": 6192, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Donald hates that word. Compromise. Despises it. Why would he want to meet with so-called 'thugs', according to him? You'll have to go yourself, and talk to them, as part of the administration. He couldn't stomach going. Your promises of justice and fairness go over well enough, but they want something more concrete. You don't have it. It falls apart." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3105, "fields": { "answer": 6196, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "We will win. Fight like hell." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3106, "fields": { "answer": 6199, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "We will win. Fight like hell." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3107, "fields": { "answer": 6200, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "He brought something alright. Kennedy started off well enough, talking of inequality, how Rebirth can make it better, and how our populist ticket can soar to new heights. Then he went onto his 'science'. Vaccines causing autism, causing illness, causing pretty much everything under the sun. \"Why did we go with him again?\" You continue to ask yourself that." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3108, "fields": { "answer": 6201, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "He brought so much energy, a certain charisma to his speech. He took on wrestlers, he took on the Predator, and now he's taking on the Establishment. You and Donald give him a standing ovation, and millions of Americans pitch in to listen to a Vietnam veteran, turned wrestler, turned actor and former Governor. It was beautiful." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3109, "fields": { "answer": 6202, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Donald brought him on, and he electrified the crowd. Rand stuck his neck out for the President, and now he's aiding us in our time of need. So much so, after this, the Libertarians would endorse our ticket. You are the true populists, and the people of this country saw that when Rand Paul spoke. We're so back." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 3110, "fields": { "answer": 6203, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "A nice 'cute' choice, but pretty boring. Donald looks at you, bored as anything, as Junior over there speaks in a plain tone. You wish the real Perot, the one who almost put the 2 party system down, would be here. That wouldn't be the case. It's a nice speech, just uninspiring. Trump isn't impressed, he wanted a spectacle." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 42523, "fields": { "answer": 6114, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "He's shocked that you even suggested this, but he agrees. You wonder if maybe, bridges will start to mend again after this. It's difficult to tell, but the votes are counted in through the Senate, and it appears to be a tie. Bernie breaks it, obviously favouring Garland. Conservatives see this as a betrayal and leave the Republican Party in droves." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 42534, "fields": { "answer": 42533, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Enter feedback text here" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 42537, "fields": { "answer": 42536, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You feel like you could solve it here and now, and it certainly feels good, on paper. Alas, it wasn't to be. Both the Israeli and Palestinian representatives were at each other's throats, one trying to out-demand the other. It was utter chaos. You could only sit back and watch as it unfolded. To their credit, the Turkish representatives and Saudi representatives try to calm it down and bring a deal, but to no avail. No deal, but hey, at least you tried." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 42539, "fields": { "answer": 42538, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Smart decision. You and Donald talk about defending democracy in the region and working together with all nations to secure a lasting peace. It’s all hot air, all for a show, but it works. Praise is heaped on by the media and Donald thinks he deserves a Nobel. You laugh to yourself, 'what an idiot', but it feels like you're warming up to him." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 42540, "fields": { "answer": 6140, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "A show. A spectacle. This is what you really wanted. You and Bernie travel together, and give a passionate speech about what work you're trying to do, trying to stop the \"UNIPARTY, ELITES AND THUGS.\" Bernie chuckles, but he knows you're right, and for once, you both have a good time. Maybe the tide is turning?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 42541, "fields": { "answer": 6139, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie feels back at home, traveling to Vermont to his adoring state. He feels free. You feel free. The west, while not your typical base of support, still gathers to hear you talk. They eat up all your words, and chant \"USA! USA!\", you feel proud. Finally - a break." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 42542, "fields": { "answer": 6178, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You and the President give each other a look. You both know what needs to be done. You formally register a new party. The 'Rebirth' Party, one that stands for justice, workers and a policy of putting American industry first. Donald follows you soon after, and in a televised address to the nation, announces his decision. A third party now runs the most powerful country on Earth. Ross Perot must be smiling down at his former Reform protégé, you chuckle to yourself." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 42543, "fields": { "answer": 6177, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "He thinks it through, you hate it. Your job depended on this one man's decision. You wait, and wait...and wait. Until he gives you a call. \"Bernie, you can stay. But don't undermine me again, otherwise, well, you'll see!\" You hang up. It sends a shiver down your spine, but what else could you do?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 42544, "fields": { "answer": 6179, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You're both content with this, what needs to change? While congressional relations are poor, you both represent wings of the parties which can get some minor legislative achievements under your belts. You also have 2 out of 3 branches of government somewhat under control. Well, not Trump, but that's okay." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79074, "fields": { "answer": 79073, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Donald sneers, silently appreciating the mutual plight of despising congress. After enough coin flips and capitulations, you two workshop the list hard enough to send it through. Some of the more \"radical\" picks, such as Ron Dellums at USTR and Alan Wilson as AG get shot down, but the happy medium narrowly shoots through. A few standouts include Sheldon Whitehouse at EPA, Stanley McChrystal at DOD, and Elaine Chao at DOT. Still, the cabinet is now filled with moderates and second-choices, a problem that could very well bite the two of you in the ass later." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79076, "fields": { "answer": 79075, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Donnie, ever the trailblazer, smirks as his peach-toned hand reaches in for a shake. He openly admits to the deal the next day, citing it as an example of \"never-before-seen collaboration\". Hopefully, getting some leverage on this end will help faith among weary progressives, but you still worry about the kind of madmen Trump's appointed to the cabinet." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79078, "fields": { "answer": 79077, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You sniffle a bit thinking about how your domestic hopes might be stifled by the penny-pinching conservatives Trump plans to send through, but you rest easy at night knowing there's sensibility across the rest of the cabinet. It's better this than let Trump appoint some jackass like Michael Flynn or something, right?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79135, "fields": { "answer": 6127, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "He agrees with you, saying he will travel to both Mexico and Canada to renegotiate. The framework has been set for a complete rework of NAFTA. You begin to celebrate as TPP is also scrapped and ridden from the agenda. Both you and Trump start to feel a sense of calmness in a once ungovernable administration" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79143, "fields": { "answer": 6118, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie agrees with you, traveling the next week. \"It was extraordinary,\" he tells you, visiting all these Latin American countries. Mexico was first, Bernie was sweet-talking his way for better trade links. He continued across the jungles of Central America, meeting with people from all walks of life. This is what you had wanted. All this talk of peace and preservation of culture, it made you somewhat...content, to be a part of this administration. Nothing Nobel worthy though, unfortunately for you." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79172, "fields": { "answer": 6130, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "He gives you a smile. He knows you've folded under the pressure, but what can you do? You both shake hands and begin the work of mending bridges again after several nights of negotiations. You think you can bargain something out of this arrangement, and so does Donald." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79173, "fields": { "answer": 6129, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Donald looks at you with a cold-hard stare. This isn't what he had in mind. He scowls and, in what you see as a temper tantrum, orders you out. He can't handle the criticism you think, nor can you stomach another moment with him. You ask for a press conference the next day, shunning the President's wounded ego." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79174, "fields": { "answer": 6131, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "The President shrugs at you, questioning whether the \"CROOKED DEMS\" have it in them to compromise. You tell him that you can call in a few favors and bargain a way for a compromise. He doesn't believe you. But you do manage to make headway, albeit at a tepid pace." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79410, "fields": { "answer": 6122, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You win some, you lose some. While Bernie wanted more, he understands Congress won't budge, and neither will you. Your order goes into effect, with limited success. State governments try to fight this, saying federal overreach, while others accept it with open arms. Soon, it'll go all the way to the Supreme Court, but you and Bernie will be long gone to see those consequences, maybe." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79411, "fields": { "answer": 6123, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "His face turns pale. He wants to accept it so bad, and yet everything is telling him not to. You have to talk for a few hours, but he finally breaks. He agrees. You watch as Bernie squirms in the Senate, just to be blocked time and time again by Congress. The Dems, having 'sympathy', give him a lifeline. It fails. Back to square one, and he fully owns the consequences." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79417, "fields": { "answer": 6208, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Trump does it, citing Netanyahu being 'incredibly difficult to work with'. Your supporters are beyond grateful, while his base foam at the mouth, calling this move an 'antisemitic attack on Jews'. For once, you feel as if you are truly making a difference for the better. The establishment does everything to discredit you two, and Trump adamantly defends his decision and surprisingly, you, on Twitter." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79418, "fields": { "answer": 6209, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "The President moves at astonishing speed, You wonder how he does it. One by one, American troops begin leaving the Middle East, and returning home, where they belong. This has its consequences however. Israel no longer views America as its guarantor, and both the Russians and Turkish begin to make heavy advancements within Syria and Iraq, much to the disarray of the Kurds. There's anger back at home, and you wonder if you made the right decision." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79446, "fields": { "answer": 6135, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You smirk as you say this, and hopefully Bernie sees your point. Luckily for you, he does, and within a few weeks the new bill is presented to you. You officially sign it. You feel a sense of gratitude as more homes for veterans are being built, more money for ICE and a nice boost to your popularity." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79526, "fields": { "answer": 6134, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernard stands up and shakes your hand, \"We're going to reshape America, Mr President.\", you almost gleam with excitement as the bill is put forward. Then it's shot down. Congress hates it, and only the so-called 'independents' support it. It feels like a disaster waiting to happen, and it was." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79537, "fields": { "answer": 6144, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Donald protests, saying it's way out of line and 'BUSINESS WILL FLEE', you don't listen. You go to a picket line, and you breathe in the atmosphere. Mr Trump would never understand this. You're in your moment of glory, relishing every moment of it." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79562, "fields": { "answer": 6137, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Your first joint rally together. The atmosphere is electric, you feel the presence of the fans. One patriot has a sign, \"MAKE AMERICA FEEL THE BERN.\" It makes you happy. You feel free from Washington's chokehold over politics. This is where the real people are, and they adore you, and Bernie, of course." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79615, "fields": { "answer": 6141, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernard gives you a perplexed look. \"You want us to jointly endorse certain people in both parties?\" You nod. \"Y'know Bernie, they'll love it, our people, I mean.\" Bernie reluctantly agrees, and you both endorse people from both the Republican and Democrat side. Bar some from 'The Squad' and Senators Hawley and Rand, they all reject it. You feel disappointed knowing it was a disaster." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79629, "fields": { "answer": 6185, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You continue to push for a public health option, but it is shot down. \"We already did that and tried it Bernard, no more.\" His words sting you, maybe your dream has to be let go, for the time being. Trump's own plan, which effectively bankrupts the country in the long run, is passed. You feel helpless, but feel compelled to stay with this Administration." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79630, "fields": { "answer": 6186, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Donald looks at you blankly. \"Abolish the debt limit? Are you insane?\" That clearly didn't get through to him. You urged him to at least put more funds into solving the debt, not exacerbating it. He agrees. Soon, the debt starts to come down. A small victory, but one nevertheless." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79658, "fields": { "answer": 6173, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You go to the Summit, feeling powerful against these so called 'world' leaders. You know you're the only one who matters, and they go and follow off your word. That poodle of yours, Johnson, loves to imitate you. You enjoy it. They all continue to pledge 2%, and yet none of them follow through, except your loyal subject, Britain." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79659, "fields": { "answer": 6174, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You both go and certainly make an impression. Your fans at home aren't impressed, saying how the US piggybanks NATO, and you know they're right. Yet, you still need your allies, and you're not quite so sure why. \"We could quite easily crush Russia, China etc\" Regardless, you put on a show, and NATO firmly has your back." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79672, "fields": { "answer": 79671, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You feel taken aback. How could he do this to you? You worked closely together over the years, being the independents of the Senate, and now, you feel betrayed. Donald gives you the time to gather yourself back together, and take the fight to this ticket. Just remember, revenge is a dish best served cold..." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79800, "fields": { "answer": 79779, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "“And we love law and order, of course, but you have to do it the right way,” you say, and Bernie agrees. This is a glaring weakness for Bloomberg, and you two won’t be shy about bringing it up." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79884, "fields": { "answer": 6168, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Daily press conferences begin, lights flicker on, your face turning a glowing orange. You feel the intensity of the media, the speculation of what this disease entails. You push for masks to be worn, lockdown-lite policies, and screening and tests to be made ASAP. Bernie is glad you're listening to him. He's...happy, and you're…happy too." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79885, "fields": { "answer": 6169, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You call Xi. You tell him to stop this 'madman shit' otherwise there will be hell to pay. You don't care to understand him, and now the public is increasingly worried and frustrated over your lack of action. Bernie is beyond furious with you, but it's too late for him to leave. It has backfired on you massively." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79886, "fields": { "answer": 6171, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie nods in agreement, leading the charge against COVID-19. People seem to agree with your decision also, knowing Bernie has been fighting for healthcare for most of his political life. It was his moment, and you feel it as both you and him begin to see positive approval ratings. You, Bernie and Fauci enjoy some good press, for once." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79902, "fields": { "answer": 79901, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You did not, in fact, 'crush them'. You'd like to think you did well, but the public think you're disingenuous, unpresidential and downright arrogant. They can't stand it, and you can't stand not being liked. It feels weird, and makes you want to rant on twitter. Luckily for you, Bernie has your back." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79905, "fields": { "answer": 79903, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Meme after meme is born from your debate. You blame the so-called 'Republicrats' on everything, and people love the sound bites on Facebook. That's what matters to you, nothing else. Those who actually watched the debate thought you lost miserably, though." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79909, "fields": { "answer": 79908, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "And wing it you did. Saying whatever you can to get votes. It works. People love it, your authenticity, your anti-establishment talk, it charms America. They had no idea what was coming to them, and you knocked it out of the park. Bernie, sat in the audience, couldn't help but smile." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79913, "fields": { "answer": 79911, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Try not to get muddled up so much on the facts, okay? You thought you had them, a victory for the ages, but in reality, it was stumble after stumble. How could this happen? It was demoralizing. Never again, you can't do it, you just can't." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79929, "fields": { "answer": 6193, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You both go out in front of the White House, calling a press conference. You call for unity in these dark times, and to sympathize with everyone who is suffering. Donald talks of 'Rallying behind the flag' and making it a symbol of patriotism again. You both feel closer together by this, and ultimately it goes smoothly. The people warm up to you and see you in a new light." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79944, "fields": { "answer": 6194, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You call for justice reform, and Donald reluctantly backs you. The BLM protesters begin to listen, and as such, begin to calm down. Moderates, who you and Donald have been trying to court for a while, also listen, and ultimately want to see justice too. It goes over extremely well, and you begin to see a boost in personal approval." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79945, "fields": { "answer": 6195, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "This goes as expected. Donald manages to piss off every section of America, calling BLM protesters 'thugs' and calling for 'real law and order'. You tremble as he continues to dig himself a deeper hole, but again, you need to focus on the virus, lives are at stake. You feel powerless to stop him and his rhetoric." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79967, "fields": { "answer": 6197, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "We will win. Fight like hell." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79968, "fields": { "answer": 6198, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "We will win. Fight like hell." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79982, "fields": { "answer": 6153, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "The 'Protection Against Covid Act' is born. You and Bernie think this is ultimately a smart idea, because relief money can only last so long, while this has more long-term consequences. It easily goes through the House and Senate, and you pass it the following day. It will take time, however, to see the effects." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79984, "fields": { "answer": 6154, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie begins negotiations. Negotiations which feel like they would last a lifetime. However, they come to an agreement. $900 in relief, and partial debt relief. Bernie is exhausted, but finally, he's achieved what he thought couldn't be done. You sign it later that day. Voter registration soars soon after. You've won." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79986, "fields": { "answer": 6155, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie will have to fight debt relief another day, but for now, COVID-19 is the more pressing issue. You both are in agreement, target COVID first, then go onto more domestic issues. One battle at a time. The $1200 relief is easily passed by both the House and Senate, and you celebrate a small win for your base." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79990, "fields": { "answer": 78488, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie is flabbergasted. What happened to his friend? Why would you do this? He threatens to quit, but you know he can't. You promise to cool down the rhetoric come January, when you hope to win, but you know that means nothing. Neither of you are particularly happy with this." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79991, "fields": { "answer": 78489, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You go there, but everyone knows you don't want to. It's that look on your face, being unbothered, not a care in the world. They hate you for it, they see you as the epitome of racism itself. You knew your gut was right, but you went for your friend. Nothing happens." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79992, "fields": { "answer": 78490, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "The Justice Reform Act of 2020 is born. You finally feel some sort of 'surge' in your campaign, a sense of activity, liveliness. You don't have to rely on empty words anymore. Bernie is ecstatic, you both give each other a handshake. A much needed break for your administration." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79993, "fields": { "answer": 78491, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Enter feedback text here" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79994, "fields": { "answer": 6161, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You and Donald have a talk. \"We should wait until after the election, Donald, I can't do it in good conscience.\" He looks confused. Why? \"Alright, alright, Bernie, we will wait until after the election, but I think we're throwing away something HUGE here.\" It stings you, knowing he's right, but your conscience won't allow it." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79997, "fields": { "answer": 6156, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Trump's words echo within your head. You have to win this. You prepare like hell, and you spend a few nights away from the Campaign Trail to recuperate and face against (VP Name Here). It pays off. You win, supposedly 'BIG LEAGUE' according to Trump. The tables are beginning to turn." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 79999, "fields": { "answer": 6157, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Falter you did. It felt weak, your performance against (VP Name Here) was disastrous. You still have time to recuperate, but Trump isn't pleased. \"You win some, you lose some, I suppose, not me though, I always win BIG!\" You feel detached after he said that. Still, you push on like the proud progressive you always have been." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80001, "fields": { "answer": 6158, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "It worked. (VP Name Here) faltered, and ultimately, cost them the debate, but not by much. You had many of your own gaffes, including suggesting a more softer approach to Cuba. Donald didn't like that, but what could he do? It was your debate. This won't shift the balance of power much, unfortunately." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80012, "fields": { "answer": 80011, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You're lucky this doesn't kill you, but it weakens you. They question you, the media, about your health. You can't handle it. Bernie tells them that you're fit to be President, it's nice of him to back you up. You hate the disingenuous media though, 'Crooks', you think to yourself." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80015, "fields": { "answer": 80014, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You continue to post on Twitter, Facebook, anything to get your ratings up. Your people, they miss you, they want to see you, but trolling on social media is the most you can do, at the moment. Still, it keeps your campaign active, and all the so-called 'resist libs' despise you evermore." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80045, "fields": { "answer": 80044, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You pour fuel onto the flames. Your people, they love it, they love you. Bernie is irrelevant in this, while you use his messaging, it doesn't matter. You attack, attack and attack, feeling glee after polls show you it's working. You control the messaging, and they can't do anything about it." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80072, "fields": { "answer": 80071, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie does as commanded, and he secures some minor wins with Marcy Kaptur and Tim Ryan endorsing your candidacy, with even Patrick Leahy endorsing Bernie. However, some of the bigger heavyweights, like Warren, and 'The Squad', despite liking Bernie, despise you, and sit out. You win some, you lose some." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80074, "fields": { "answer": 79105, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You're right in saying that he is an establishment sort of figure, however it remains to be seen whether Democrats will flock to your new populist cause. Nevertheless, you paint him as a crony to the establishment, acting so called 'independent' while also shilling for the Democrats." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80075, "fields": { "answer": 80073, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You go out to the coal mining communities, the ones ravaged by NAFTA. All you feel there is suffering, and you can't help but feel bad for these people. You describe Angus as a job-taker, not a job-giver, and they love it. The Midwest begin to give him a new look. One of anger." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80084, "fields": { "answer": 80083, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "While what you say isn't exactly true, it sticks. The neo-cons begin defending him like Jesus H. Christ. What are they thinking? Completely suicidal of them. Regardless, it's a win-win, and you begin to mount pressure on the Uniparty. For once in your political life, you feel free." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80091, "fields": { "answer": 80090, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Donald agrees, he doesn’t care what state he goes to, as long as the crowd sizes are ‘big’. You work as hard as you can, traveling from New Jersey down to Florida, and before that Colorado and the Rockies. It’s exhausting, it’s breaking, but you do it, for the country." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80093, "fields": { "answer": 80092, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You begin to ramble on and on about Illegals flooding the border. Bernie can't help but cringe at some moments, and it feels flat on your end. Your base eat it up, but to moderates and independents, you seem desperate." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80095, "fields": { "answer": 80094, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You call him 'ANGRY ANGUS' on Twitter, and it becomes a hit. Soon, your base, your people, start taunting the man mercilessly. He gets more irate, and Mitt? Poor guy can't catch a break. You smirk to Bernie, \"This is almost too easy!\", he seems disappointed. Why? We're winning." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80097, "fields": { "answer": 80096, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "The hypocrisy isn't lost on anyone, but people start to notice. Where's King's energy? His Charisma? You know full well you'll be the oldest president inaugurated, but you have that charm. You have that passion, and so does Bernie. Finally, a breakthrough in the campaign. Victory." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80099, "fields": { "answer": 80098, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You felt great telling your staffers that line, \"NO KINGS IN AMERICA!\", the people loved it. The history, the irony, the humour, it was all falling into place. You plaster images of yourself as George Washington, 'kicking out' Angus King, portrayed as King George III. Angus is fuming, he's lost the advertising war." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80102, "fields": { "answer": 80101, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You're right in saying he's a fraud. Donald knows it too, hence why he was so hesitant to endorse him in 2012. You go after his multiple business ventures, his job layoffs, his corporate scheming. The Midwest loves it, and so does most of the country. No one likes an out-of-touch candidate." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80104, "fields": { "answer": 80103, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Romney, poor soul, can't deny this. He is a literal electoral loser, and has to consistently defend not only his record, but his performance. You gleam with excitement, at least you got to be VP. King has to defend him as well, and he looks tired of doing it." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80106, "fields": { "answer": 80105, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Democrats en masse begin to criticise this new ticket, why would they side with someone who was a Republican? You're right, and cracks begin to form. Marcy Kaptur and Tim Ryan are the first to defect, then come the bigger ones. 'The Squad' joins soon after. You feel a new era is about to begin." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80108, "fields": { "answer": 80107, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "It feels like he's running to win back his former dignity. You're going to show him the front door, again. He can't, and won't, become Vice President. You can't let it happen. You fought against these sorts of people all your life, and you continue, even with the media onslaught. You're ready to bring America back." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80114, "fields": { "answer": 80110, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Everyone knows you’re right, they know Bloomberg, they know his antics. He can’t get a crowd of 50 going, let alone the country. You smile as one by one, your fellow New York businessmen trash his name and his ideals. It fills you with glee, you finally take the spotlight once again." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80115, "fields": { "answer": 80111, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie can’t help but let out a smirk. The hypocrisy he thinks, it’s astronomical. Regardless, those so called ‘misogynistic’ comments don’t matter when you say them, all that matters is that Bloomberg knows who he’s facing. He can’t handle it, woman after woman, they all lambast him. Thank God you had your turn 4 years ago." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80116, "fields": { "answer": 80112, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie gives you a smile, knowing that this attack stings Bloomberg. “You’re right Bernie, honestly, we need to get rid of that fraud, Citizens United really saved his ass, can you believe it?”. You believe it, Bernie believes it, the country, they see it with their eyes wide open." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80117, "fields": { "answer": 80113, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You and Bernie, both the fittest people for office, maybe ever, have to face up against this cretin? You feel a slight amount of disgust, how come the Establishment falters so badly? You feel insulted, and yet, victory is within your grasp. Just remember, the people love your authenticity." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80120, "fields": { "answer": 80119, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You aren’t exactly wrong, however you already know he’s going to fire back with your history as well. It doesn’t matter, you know that’s in the past, everyone knows he’s doing it just for power. So greedy, he’s like you, just 4 years behind. Your people, they still cheer your name, they cheer on Rebirth, their savior." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80122, "fields": { "answer": 80121, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie chuckles, he feels like it works ‘for your personality’ or whatever that means. Moderates seem to find this petty and almost unbearable, but since when have you needed them to win? Your base, they eat it up, they can’t get enough. You’re so eager to hear them, your people, cheer your name. It’s all you’ve ever wanted." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80124, "fields": { "answer": 80123, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You know it, and your ‘friends’ in congress know it as well. So many wins, and yet, Congress still has the brass neck to try and block your agenda. It infuriates you, every twist and turn, they want to sabotage you, but they can’t stop it. Mike, such a sore loser, tries to control them, the master at the strings, you just cut them off." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80126, "fields": { "answer": 80125, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie tells you that “you’re missing an opportunity” and that “Mike will outspend us 10 to 1” but it doesn’t matter. Why should it? You’re the President, you control the narrative. Nobody is going to listen to what Mike has to say, when the President’s mic is ten times louder. Isn’t that obvious? Why can’t anyone see it?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80129, "fields": { "answer": 80128, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "“Do John Kasich and Mike Bloomberg stand for YOU?” - You meticulously plan out each ad, hoping that they start to do some real damage against this ‘oligarchy’. It works like a treat, your progressive base already hates them, but it gets them fired up like never before. You finally feel a bit free from the chaos." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80131, "fields": { "answer": 80130, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Kasich tries to squirm his way out of it, calling you ‘hellbent on destroying America’s foundation’ or whatever that means. You just smirk, you know he’s desperate, but why should you bother helping him? He’ll tear you apart at the first chance, just like he did with his business partners. Trump loves it, he starts to see a companion in you, a new heir." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80133, "fields": { "answer": 80132, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "He nods. “You’re so right Bernie, so right, I mean, we have personality, a charm. They have nothing”. You nod. Finally, the wind starts blowing your way, and soon after, independents start to be wooed over by your charisma. Finally, something good is coming into your campaign, a sense of ease." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80135, "fields": { "answer": 80134, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You hit him where it hurts, he stumbles over his record, he can’t help it, what did he even do? He didn’t exactly help the blue-collar workers who lost their jobs due to his business dealings. It leaves you fuming, it makes you mad as hell, and you won’t take anymore of it. They finally see him for who he is, a FRAUD." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80138, "fields": { "answer": 80137, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You feel like you're right, but there's a 'vibe shift' going on within the country. They want new people. It's why they elected you in 2016. This attack feels more hypocritical of you more than anyone else, but alas, you do begin to make headway." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80140, "fields": { "answer": 80139, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You call him all sorts of names. 'LITTLE PETE', 'Alfred E. Neuman', some you leave out, like 'PETE BOOTYGAY', maybe that one's a bit too much. Regardless, you come off as childish, but it has its intended effect. Meme after meme after meme is born. Your branding works, and social media is more important than ever." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80142, "fields": { "answer": 80141, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "The black vote begins its slow slipaway from the Uniparty, and Latinos, in droves, begin to abandon this ticket. Social Conservatism is a hell of a kicker, and you love it. Your coalition becomes more diverse than ever, and while *some* hate that idea, anything you can do to win, needs to happen." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80144, "fields": { "answer": 80143, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Newscum, Fat Man Pritzker, Low IQ Whitmer, they all fall in line behind Pete. This is your opportunity. You lambast him as a 'closeted Elite'. You know what you *really* mean, but it sticks. The base, and independents, listen in, and you feel more determined than ever to defeat this man." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80147, "fields": { "answer": 80146, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Her record is indeed...spotty, to say the least. You feel overwhelming power as you tear Haley's record piece by piece. You feel excited at the prospect of a second term already. Twitter mocks her with memes endlessly, and you share them every chance you get." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80149, "fields": { "answer": 80148, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You feel like this attack is slightly misplaced. You ran on a platform of being an outsider in 2016, and it worked in your favour. Now you attack her for doing the same thing. It doesn't really add up, and the electorate knows it." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80151, "fields": { "answer": 80150, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie is boiling with rage with this, but it's too late to leave the ticket. Conservatives, scared of a 'minority' being in the Oval Office, come to you in droves. People notice your subtle attacks on a minority member of society, and obviously lambast you for it. You quietly take down the ads." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80153, "fields": { "answer": 80152, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "A relatively restrained persona for you, let's see if it pays off. You and Bernie paint a positive vision for the country, sprinkling in the 'ESTABLISHMENT' and the 'MILITARY INDUSTRIAL COMPLEX' only sweetens the deal. It feels good, but can it last?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80156, "fields": { "answer": 80155, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "This does wonders in ‘Democrat’ territory, especially considering Buttigieg is either unwilling or unable to defend his running mate regarding this. Edwards fights back, arguing that his constituents wanted it. It doesn’t matter, you’ve caught him, and he begins to slowly sink back into his Louisianan swamp." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80161, "fields": { "answer": 80157, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You call it out as it is. Donald is surprised, he didn't think you had it in you, and yet you're out there, challenging Kemp. It feels freeing to finally be on the offense rather than defense. You talk about 'an establishment coup' taking place within the 2 parties, and it reaches all across America." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80162, "fields": { "answer": 80158, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "This attack sticks for a while, but you begin to wear yourself out as well. You need to take a break, and Donald thinks so too. \"You're doing great Bernie, maybe the best ever, we have to keep going, for our movement!” You think how can Donald be so energetic sometimes? He’s 70+ years old, and still thinks he’s 30. It's a mystery." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80163, "fields": { "answer": 80159, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "It feels like Kemp’s running to win back his former dignity from Trump. It looks like Edwards is trying to find himself some glory. You're going to show them the front door. They won't become Vice President. You can't let it happen. You fought against these sorts of people all your life, and you continue, even with the media onslaught. You're ready to bring America back." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80166, "fields": { "answer": 80165, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie looks at you concerningly, almost as if to suggest you are going insane. Idiot, doesn’t he realise that we’re the only ones who can stop her? Scott is a loser, a total deadbeat, it's just up to you and Sanders, and you’re not going to waste an opportunity like this. She can’t hide it, and you know you’re right. When aren’t you?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80168, "fields": { "answer": 80167, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "How isn’t this obvious to everyone? She’s just in it for her own self-gain, and yet, Americans continue to support her. You don’t care, you know that if this is what she wants, she’ll have it. Rally to rally, state to state, you speak to your followers, and they cheer your name. She’s such a bore." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80170, "fields": { "answer": 80169, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Every rally is filled to the brim with MAGA hats and MAFA shirts. The mix of red and blue, you feel like a purple tsunami is going to hit America. The energy radiating each stadium fills Bernie full of optimism and you with ego. Hillary can’t beat this, what a loser." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80172, "fields": { "answer": 80171, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "It worked 4 years ago, it’ll work now, and this time, you have her dead to rights. You should have investigated her once you were in power, but Sanders blocked it; however, it's all fair game now. Soon, the investigations begin, and she calls foul play. Too bad." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80176, "fields": { "answer": 80174, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You're right, he isn't known well nationally, probably only in New England. But news spreads quickly, and so, all his accomplishments and records go out with him. You do manage to get a few swings in though, enough to switch the narrative for a while. Take what you can get." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80177, "fields": { "answer": 80175, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie says you can't label everything 'establishment' and 'frauds', but you do it anyway. The base, as you rightly point out, love it, and begin to taunt him at every rally possible. You feel glee as he's mocked endlessly over being a RINO, and it sets you free." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80179, "fields": { "answer": 80178, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Ventura, RFK Jr, Rand Paul, they all line up to endorse you. It isn't much in the grand scheme of things, but it's something. You begin to gnaw at any chance to get an endorsement, willing to bargain political power for it. It pays off, and you starve Scott of any outsider endorsement." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80181, "fields": { "answer": 80180, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "This isn't you. It can't be you. You feel the need to attack, but for some reason, you tell yourself not to. Then the media steps in. \"He's certainly become more restrained over these past few weeks...\". It's working, people start talking about you again. Glee." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80184, "fields": { "answer": 80183, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "The same-old attacks on the establishment begin to wear off, as you actually are in government, plus he is a pretty popular Senator. You know this to be true, and ultimately, you falter in the face of Booker, who gets the momentum needed to surpass you." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80186, "fields": { "answer": 80185, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "This absolutely stings him. You've hit him where it hurts, and you continue blow after blow to knock him down. You feel triumphant as your poll numbers begin to ascend. Another victory for the populist cause, and one which resonates with Americans across the country." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80188, "fields": { "answer": 80187, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "While it doesn't hit as hard nationally, the locals in Massachusetts and New Jersey begin to see this in a new light. Why isn't he working for them, they ask. Why isn't he doing his job? You feel like you've hit the nail on the head, and you pounce at every chance to knock him down. You need to win." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80190, "fields": { "answer": 80189, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "'A house divided against itself cannot stand', you think to yourself. What would Lincoln do? What would Washington do? You need inspiration to drag this campaign out the gutter, and it's lost on you. Neither Baker nor Booker cares about bi-partisanship, they know they have Congress." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80193, "fields": { "answer": 80192, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You and Donnie spent the rest of the day pacing back and forth in the Oval Office, your heels burning a hole in the carpet as you argue every which way. The podcast eventually goes off without a hitch, save from a few gaffes. You hammer in about labor, immigration, bipartisanship, and how this administration will \"cooperate like hell\". His viewers claim it's refreshing to see 2 massive egos balanced out by each other." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80195, "fields": { "answer": 80194, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Rogan's team sighs seeing a list of Do's-And-Dont's longer than the fucking Torah, but CC's it to him nonetheless. Despite this, Joe seems more focused on bears and martians than how you plan to facilitate congressional relationships. A particularly thorny moment comes when Rogan, pupils dilated, asks your thoughts on the JFK conspiracies. Trump pats your back before words can escape your gaping mouth and claims he's got \"boys looking into it.\"" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80197, "fields": { "answer": 80196, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Trump does his little happy \"dance\" on his way out, prancing through the White House before taking the trip out West to Austin. Despite his general chutzpah, he still finds a way to knock you down as an \"idealist, not a realist\" when Joe inquires about you. Nevertheless, the two seem to have a grand old time, while you toil away giving a \"rousing\" speech on the Senate floor before a GQ shoot. Why do all the photos of you make you look like a deeply traumatized raisin?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80202, "fields": { "answer": 80200, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie relents, trying to convince you, but you’re not stupid. Is he trying to sabotage you? What a moron. Regardless, he goes instead, and it goes rather smoothly, for better or for worse. Maybe it’s best if you both stick to your strengths, but you can’t help but feel like he’s trying to steal your spotlight." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80203, "fields": { "answer": 80201, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You went into the studio, their eyes fixated on you. You listen intently. Are they trying to trap you with these questions? You go on to call them ‘FAKE NEWS’ and a ‘national disgrace’. Clearly their emotions got the better of them, and you remained calm. These idiots, they have no idea." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80205, "fields": { "answer": 80204, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Bernie agrees, he’s wanted to do an interview for a while now. You both sit inside Maddow’s studio, her eyes fixed upon both of you. What is her problem, I mean, what a loser. It goes off without a hitch, you even use your charm to make them both laugh a little. Was it really that easy?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80207, "fields": { "answer": 80206, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You go, anticipating the wrath that is Scarborough, but he remembers, he remembers your age old friendship. You’ve always liked the guy, but he soured, God knows why. But regardless, you both actually have a good conversation, from how you’ve changed certain views, to Bernie, who you call a ‘sincere guy’." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80212, "fields": { "answer": 80209, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You go to the FOX studio, you reach out to shake Tucker’s hand and he waits a few seconds before accepting it. You feel uneasy, but prepared, as you rattle off each one of your points, much to Tucker’s disappointment. It goes off just fine, he never really managed to trap you, and yet you wonder if anything really changed." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80213, "fields": { "answer": 80210, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You’re worried as ever. This is FOX you’re talking about, and they’re always prone to try and shame you at every turn. Trump must be gloating to his staff right now, knowing you ‘fell’ for his ‘trap’. Regardless, in a rare moment, you and Carlson actually share some wholesome moments together, and agree to do another interview in the future. Has FOX gone woke?" } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80214, "fields": { "answer": 80211, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Trump said you went in ‘guns blazing’ and he isn’t exactly wrong. You know what you were going up against. Tucker wasn’t playing ball, and frankly, you were tired of it. You managed to get in some zingers, but at the expense of coming off as immature for most of it. You win some, you lose some." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80216, "fields": { "answer": 80215, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Trump calls you a coward, but does that really matter all things considered? You think you missed out on a bloodbath, while FOX taunts you endlessly for dodging. In your eyes, working in Washington is more important than being media savvy. You just have to hope the American people see it that way as well." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80219, "fields": { "answer": 80218, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Really? After all I’ve tried to help you with, after all the compromises, you go out and call me a communist? You really are that narcissistic man as seen on TV, one who doesn’t give a shit about who they hurt, now I see why you’ve had 2 divorces. You truly have no shame." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80224, "fields": { "answer": 80223, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Joe Biden? That old fossil? You don’t dislike him, but in order to win over support from the moderates, this might be your best choice. It also helps you court back the Obama Coalition, albeit at a slower pace than you’d like. Hopefully the DNC won’t crucify you." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80226, "fields": { "answer": 80225, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Everyone expected this pick from you, but still, it’s a good one, she’ll hopefully energise the Progressive base, along with boosting your support among women and minority supporters, who you typically lack." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80228, "fields": { "answer": 80227, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "She’s a good pick for the Southwest, and a pick to energise the Latino vote, you hope this will be worth it in the long run. However, that being said, she doesn’t have the best name recognition and she’s seen as an Establishment figure by your base. Hopefully they stand by you come November." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80230, "fields": { "answer": 80229, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You think Klobuchar is a decent choice, but not exactly the most…energising pick for your base. While you can make inroads with the ‘moderate’ vote, you don’t want to leave your fellow BernieBros behind, it doesn’t feel right." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80239, "fields": { "answer": 80238, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "The people truly open their eyes, and they see it, the horror of Trump’s America, and they are livid, with every sane politician (who has a spine) calling out Trump's tyrannical tirade. You’ve successfully inspired America and triumphed over Donald’s ego." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80240, "fields": { "answer": 80237, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "The people truly open their eyes, and they see it, the horror of Trump’s America, and they are livid, with every sane politician (who has a spine) calling out Trump's tyrannical tirade. Heat can’t, and won’t, stop your campaign. You even go to Texas and have a rally with Senator Beto O’Rourke. It goes great, you think nothing can stop you." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80323, "fields": { "answer": 80316, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "You tour Nevada and Arizona aggressively, it feels like the heat is getting to you, but you carry on forward. Heat can’t, and won’t, stop your campaign. You even go to Texas and have a rally with Senator Beto O’Rourke. It goes great, you think nothing can stop you." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80328, "fields": { "answer": 80327, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Staff give you a confused look. Why do this? Everyone knows you held onto the Plains by considerable margins for the Democratic Nomination, but will it translate All The Way to November? It’s definitely uncertain, but you feel like you have to prove yourself." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80329, "fields": { "answer": 80326, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Staff give you a confused look. Why do this? Everyone knows you held onto the Plains by considerable margins for the Democratic Nomination, but will it translate All The Way to November? The people see you for who you are, a champion of the working class. That’s what your staffers say to you, you wonder if they're just trying to sugarcoat it." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80352, "fields": { "answer": 80350, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "Well, this style keeps the campaign all fine and going, but this won’t help you with building any sorts of bridges with donors or ‘special interests’, but since when have you ever relied on them? The people see you for who you are, a champion of the working class. That’s what your staffers say to you, you wonder if they're just trying to sugarcoat it." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80380, "fields": { "answer": 80355, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "It seems like this campaign was somewhat successful, bringing you enough money to hold you over for a good while, so no cuts for anything is needed really, so that’s a relief for your campaign, and hey, you have a small surplus too! You use what little extra funds you have to produce a hit piece of Trump. Safe to say, he doesn’t want to talk anymore." } }, { "model": "campaign_trail.answer_feedback", "pk": 80389, "fields": { "answer": 80356, "candidate": 301, "answer_feedback": "It seems like this campaign was somewhat successful, bringing you enough money to hold you over for a good while, so no cuts for anything is needed really, so that’s a relief for your campaign, and hey, you have a small surplus too!
