(8)`, }; let savedX = null; let savedY = null; const THEME = { primary: '#CA394A', secondary: '#2B9CD9', bg: '#161c22', bgSoft: '#1f2730', bgHard: '#10151a', edgeLight: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.04)', edgeDark: 'rgba(0,0,0,0.6)', text: '#d6d9dd', textMuted: '#9aa4af', border: '#2b3440', shadowOuter: '0 10px 25px rgba(0,0,0,0.65)', shadowInner: 'inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.05), inset 0 -1px 0 rgba(0,0,0,0.7)', radius: '6px' }; let popOutMenu = null; let overviewContent = null; let observedGameWindow = null; let gameWindowObserver = null; let bootstrapObserver = null; let refreshScheduled = false; let attachScheduled = false; let activeTooltip = null; function applyStyles(el, styles) { Object.assign(el.style, styles); return el; } function buildOverviewHtml() { const trumpLeftValue = typeof TrumpLeft !== 'undefined' ? TrumpLeft : undefined; const BernieRightValue = typeof BernieRight !== 'undefined' ? BernieRight : undefined; const WinsValue = typeof Wins !== 'undefined' ? Wins : undefined; const UnityValue = typeof Unity !== 'undefined' ? Unity : undefined; return [ `• ${TrumpLeftMap[String(trumpLeftValue)]}`, `• ${BernieRightMap[String(BernieRightValue)]}`, `• ${WinsMap[String(WinsValue)]}`, `• ${UnityMap[String(UnityValue)]}`, ].join('
'); } function updateOverview() { if (!overviewContent) return; const nextHtml = buildOverviewHtml(); if (overviewContent.innerHTML !== nextHtml) { overviewContent.innerHTML = nextHtml; } } function addHeadquarterButton(root = observedGameWindow || document) { if (document.getElementById('headquarter_button')) return; const refButton = root?.querySelector ? root.querySelector('#view_electoral_map') : document.getElementById('view_electoral_map'); if (!refButton) return; const newButton = document.createElement('button'); newButton.id = 'headquarter_button'; newButton.textContent = 'HQ'; newButton.style.marginLeft = '1.5em'; newButton.addEventListener('click', openSurrogates); refButton.insertAdjacentElement('afterend', newButton); } function createMenuList(items, maxHeight, hasTooltip = false) { const list = document.createElement('div'); applyStyles(list, { maxHeight: `${maxHeight}px`, overflowY: 'auto', display: 'flex', flexDirection: 'column', gap: '6px' }); const fragment = document.createDocumentFragment(); items.forEach(item => fragment.appendChild(createCard(item, hasTooltip))); list.appendChild(fragment); return list; } function ensurePopOutMenu() { if (popOutMenu && document.body.contains(popOutMenu)) { return popOutMenu; } popOutMenu = document.createElement('div'); popOutMenu.id = 'popOutMenu'; applyStyles(popOutMenu, { position: 'absolute', width: '430px', padding: '16px', color: THEME.text, borderRadius: THEME.radius, zIndex: '1000', fontFamily: 'Inter, Bahnschrift, sans-serif', background: `linear-gradient( 180deg, ${THEME.bgSoft} 0%, ${THEME.bg} 40%, ${THEME.bgHard} 100% )`, boxShadow: `${THEME.shadowOuter}, inset 0 1px 0 ${THEME.edgeLight}` }); const overviewHeader = document.createElement('h2'); overviewHeader.innerText = 'Campaign File'; applyStyles(overviewHeader, { color: THEME.primary, fontSize: '22px', margin: '8px 0' }); popOutMenu.appendChild(overviewHeader); overviewContent = document.createElement('div'); applyStyles(overviewContent, { background: THEME.bgSoft, borderRadius: '8px', padding: '10px', fontSize: '13px', lineHeight: '1.4' }); popOutMenu.appendChild(overviewContent); const closeButton = document.createElement('button'); closeButton.innerText = 'Close'; closeButton.addEventListener('click', closeSurrogates); applyStyles(closeButton, { marginTop: '12px', padding: '8px', width: '100%', borderRadius: '8px', border: 'none', cursor: 'pointer', background: THEME.primary, color: '#fff', fontWeight: '600' }); closeButton.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => { closeButton.style.background = '#b12f3f'; }); closeButton.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => { closeButton.style.background = THEME.primary; }); popOutMenu.appendChild(closeButton); document.body.appendChild(popOutMenu); makeDraggable(popOutMenu); updateOverview(); return popOutMenu; } function openSurrogates(evt) { const menu = ensurePopOutMenu(); updateOverview(); if (savedX !== null && savedY !== null) { menu.style.left = `${savedX}px`; menu.style.top = `${savedY}px`; } else if (evt?.currentTarget) { const buttonRect = evt.currentTarget.getBoundingClientRect(); menu.style.left = `${buttonRect.right + 20}px`; menu.style.top = `${buttonRect.top}px`; } else { menu.style.left = '40px'; menu.style.top = '40px'; } menu.style.display = 'block'; } function closeSurrogates() { if (!popOutMenu) return; hideTooltip(); const rect = popOutMenu.getBoundingClientRect(); savedX = rect.left; savedY = rect.top; popOutMenu.style.display = 'none'; } function showTooltip(evt, html) { hideTooltip(); activeTooltip = document.createElement('div'); activeTooltip.id = 'tooltip'; activeTooltip.innerHTML = html; applyStyles(activeTooltip, { position: 'fixed', background: THEME.secondary, color: '#fff', padding: '8px', borderRadius: '8px', boxShadow: THEME.shadowOuter, zIndex: '9999', maxWidth: '240px', pointerEvents: 'none' }); document.body.appendChild(activeTooltip); moveTooltip(evt); } function moveTooltip(evt) { if (!activeTooltip || !evt) return; activeTooltip.style.left = `${evt.clientX + 12}px`; activeTooltip.style.top = `${evt.clientY + 12}px`; } function hideTooltip() { if (activeTooltip) { activeTooltip.remove(); activeTooltip = null; } } function createCard(item, hasTooltip = false) { const baseBoxShadow = ` inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.06), inset 0 -1px 0 rgba(0,0,0,0.6) `; const card = document.createElement('div'); applyStyles(card, { display: 'flex', gap: '10px', padding: '8px', borderRadius: '10px', transition: '0.15s ease', background: `linear-gradient( 180deg, rgba(255,255,255,0.03), rgba(0,0,0,0.35) ), ${THEME.bgSoft}`, boxShadow: baseBoxShadow }); card.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => { card.style.transform = 'translateY(-2px)'; card.style.filter = 'brightness(1.05)'; card.style.boxShadow = '0 8px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.12)'; }); card.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => { card.style.transform = 'none'; card.style.filter = 'brightness(1)'; card.style.boxShadow = baseBoxShadow; }); const img = document.createElement('img'); img.src = item.photo; applyStyles(img, { width: '100px', height: '100px', objectFit: 'cover', borderRadius: '8px', border: `2px solid ${THEME.secondary}` }); const text = document.createElement('div'); text.innerHTML = item.text; text.style.fontSize = '13px'; card.appendChild(img); card.appendChild(text); if (hasTooltip && item.tooltip) { card.style.position = 'relative'; const tip = document.createElement('div'); tip.innerText = '?'; applyStyles(tip, { position: 'absolute', top: '6px', right: '10px', color: THEME.secondary, cursor: 'pointer', fontWeight: '700' }); tip.addEventListener('mouseenter', evt => showTooltip(evt, item.tooltip)); tip.addEventListener('mousemove', moveTooltip); tip.addEventListener('mouseleave', hideTooltip); card.appendChild(tip); } return card; } function makeDraggable(element) { let dragging = false; let offsetX = 0; let offsetY = 0; const onMouseMove = e => { if (!dragging) return; const nextX = e.clientX - offsetX; const nextY = e.clientY - offsetY; element.style.left = `${nextX}px`; element.style.top = `${nextY}px`; savedX = nextX; savedY = nextY; }; const onMouseUp = () => { dragging = false; document.removeEventListener('mousemove', onMouseMove); document.removeEventListener('mouseup', onMouseUp); }; element.addEventListener('mousedown', e => { dragging = true; offsetX = e.clientX - element.offsetLeft; offsetY = e.clientY - element.offsetTop; document.addEventListener('mousemove', onMouseMove); document.addEventListener('mouseup', onMouseUp); }); } function scheduleRefresh() { if (refreshScheduled) return; refreshScheduled = true; requestAnimationFrame(() => { refreshScheduled = false; addHeadquarterButton(observedGameWindow || document); if (popOutMenu && popOutMenu.style.display !== 'none') { updateOverview(); } }); } function attachGameWindowObserver(target) { if (target === observedGameWindow) return; if (!gameWindowObserver) { gameWindowObserver = new MutationObserver(scheduleRefresh); } else { gameWindowObserver.disconnect(); } observedGameWindow = target || null; if (observedGameWindow) { gameWindowObserver.observe(observedGameWindow, { childList: true, subtree: true }); } scheduleRefresh(); } function nodeMatchesOrContains(node, selector) { return ( node && node.nodeType === 1 && (node.matches?.(selector) || node.querySelector?.(selector)) ); } function handleBootstrapMutations(mutations) { for (const mutation of mutations) { for (const node of mutation.addedNodes) { if ( nodeMatchesOrContains(node, '#game_window') || nodeMatchesOrContains(node, '#view_electoral_map') ) { scheduleAttach(); return; } } for (const node of mutation.removedNodes) { if ( node === observedGameWindow || (node.nodeType === 1 && observedGameWindow && node.contains?.(observedGameWindow)) ) { scheduleAttach(); return; } } } } function scheduleAttach() { if (attachScheduled) return; attachScheduled = true; requestAnimationFrame(() => { attachScheduled = false; attachGameWindowObserver(document.getElementById('game_window')); }); } function initCampaignFile() { if (document.getElementById('controlElement')) return; const controlElement = document.createElement('div'); controlElement.style.display = 'none'; controlElement.id = 'controlElement'; document.body.appendChild(controlElement); bootstrapObserver = new MutationObserver(handleBootstrapMutations); bootstrapObserver.observe(document.body, { childList: true, subtree: true }); scheduleAttach(); } if (document.body) { initCampaignFile(); } else { window.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', initCampaignFile, { once: true }); } // Change candidate pics each question campaignTrail_temp.candidate_image_url = 'https://images2.imgbox.com/e2/63/cASCSxQr_o.png'; (ignore that i stole this from a lifetime of this let unlockedAchievements = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("unlockedAch")) || {}; const achievementQueue = []; let playthroughAchievements = {}; let isAchievementShowing = false; const achievementSoundURL = ""; function unlockAchievement(title) { if (typeof cheatsActive === 'undefined' || typeof campaignTrail_temp === 'undefined' || !campaignTrail_temp.CTS) { // not on CTS? then do nothing return; } if (unlockedAchievements[title]) { // has been unlocked? then do nothing return; } if (playthroughAchievements[title]) { // has been unlocked in this playthrough? then do nothing return; } // look up the achievement data using the title const achData = campaignTrail_temp.achievements[title]; // if the achievement doesn't exist, log an error and exit if (!achData) { console.error(`Achievement with title "${title}" not found. Check for typos?`); return; } // check conditions before awarding if (achData.cannotBeCheated && cheatsActive) { console.log(`Cannot unlock "${title}" because cheats are active.`); return; } if (playthroughAchievements[title]) { return; // already earned } // mark as earned for this playthrough playthroughAchievements[title] = true; // add the achievement to the display queue queueAchievementPopup(title, achData.image, achievementSoundURL, achData.description); // save to local storage if it's a new unlock and difficulty is valid if (campaignTrail_temp.difficulty_level_multiplier <= 1 && campaignTrail_temp.CTS) { // only queue the popup for a brand new unlock queueAchievementPopup(title, achData.image, achievementSoundURL, achData.description); // mark as permanently unlocked and save unlockedAchievements[title] = true; localStorage.setItem("unlockedAch", JSON.stringify(unlockedAchievements)); addAllAchievements(); } } function queueAchievementPopup(title, imageUrl, soundUrl, description) { if (achievementQueue.some(ach => ach.title === title)) { return; } achievementQueue.push({ title, imageUrl, soundUrl, description }); processAchievementQueue(); } function processAchievementQueue() { if (isAchievementShowing || achievementQueue.length === 0) return; isAchievementShowing = true; const { title, imageUrl, soundUrl, description } = achievementQueue.shift(); const achievementOverlay = document.createElement('div'); achievementOverlay.className = 'achievement-popup'; achievementOverlay.innerHTML = `

${title}

${description}

`; document.body.appendChild(achievementOverlay); requestAnimationFrame(() => { achievementOverlay.classList.add('show'); }); // sound (we don't currently use this) // const achievementSound = new Audio(soundUrl); // ... setTimeout(() => { closeOverlay(achievementOverlay); }, 7000); } function closeOverlay(overlay) { overlay.classList.remove('show'); overlay.addEventListener('transitionend', () => { overlay.remove(); isAchievementShowing = false; processAchievementQueue(); }, { once: true }); } // Note to future coders here, make sure to put achievement unlocks BELOW this code because otherwise the code breaks -themccain (i'll just call myself tmc in the code from now on) function updateHistoricalData(newVpTable, newHexColors, newNames, newEvs, newPvs, newPvps) { vpTableHistorical = newVpTable; HistHexcolour = newHexColors; HistName = newNames; HistEV = newEvs; HistPV = newPvs; HistPVP = newPvps; } let vpName = "Bernard Sanders"; let bernieVP = "Amy Klobuchar"; vpTable = { "Donald J. Trump" : vpName, // The Republicans "Angus S. King" : "Mitt Romney", "Michael R. Bloomberg" : "John Kasich", "Bernie Sanders" : "Amy Klobuchar", "Peter P. M. Buttigieg" : "John Bel Edwards", "Nimarata N. R. Haley" : "Brian Kemp", "Hillary R. Clinton" : "Cory Booker", "Philip B. Scott" : "Charlie Baker", "Donald Trump" : "Ted Cruz", "Bernard Sanders" : bernieVP, // Secret Rep "Ted Cruz" : "Marco Rubio", "Sarah Palin" : "Mike Huckabee", "Marco Rubio" : "Scott Walker", "Brian Kemp" : "Phil Scott", // The Libertarians "Jo Jorgensen" : "Spike Cohen", "Gary Johnson" : "Larry Sharpe", "Mark Cuban" : "Lincoln Chafee", "Rick Santorum" : "Mike DeWine", // The Greens "Jill Stein" : "Ian Schlakman", "Cynthia McKinney" : "David Duke", "Mike Gravel" : "Tulsi Gabbard", "Howie Hawkins" : "Angela Walker", "Mr Popular Front" : "KKKillary Klinton", "Mr Losertarians" : "Gary Johnson", "Mr Plants" : "Vladimir Putin", } vpTableHistorical = { "???" : "???", "Donald J. Trump" : "Bernard Sanders", "Gary Johnson" : "Larry Sharpe", "Jill Stein" : "Ian Schlakman", } HistHexcolour=["#32362f","#661659","#c9b34c","#148022"]; HistName=["???","Donald J. Trump","Gary Johnson","Jill Stein"]; HistEV=[450,88,0,0]; HistPV=["76,349,206","49,534,833","3,117,218","2,041,414"]; HistPVP=["58.3%","37.8%","2.4%","1.5%"]; newVpTableHistorical1 = { //Angus "Angus S. M Buttigieg" : "John Bel Edwards", "Donald J. Trump" : "Bernard Sanders", "Nimarata N. R. Haley" : "Brian Kemp", "Cynthia McKinney" : "David Duke", }; newHistHexcolour5 = ["#345592","#771c6b","#8c2626","#3e5742" ]; newHistName5 = ["Peter P. M Buttigieg","Donald J. Trump","Nimarata N. R Haley","Cynthia McKinney"]; newHistEV5 = [210,171,157,0]; newHistPV5 = ["55,592,958", "50,760,854", "52,075,819", "1"]; newHistPVP5 = ["35.0%", "31.0%", "31.8%", "0%"]; function onGameWindowChangeCandidates() { const resultsTableHolder = document.getElementById("overall_details_container"); if (!resultsTableHolder || resultsTableHolder.classList.contains("done")) { return; } const tables = [ { el: resultsTableHolder.childNodes[3]?.childNodes[3]?.children[0], data: typeof vpTable !== 'undefined' ? vpTable : {} }, { el: resultsTableHolder.childNodes[5]?.childNodes[3]?.children[0], data: typeof vpTableHistorical !== 'undefined' ? vpTableHistorical : {} } ]; tables.forEach(({ el, data }) => { if (!el || !el.children) return; for (let i = 0; i < el.children.length; i++) { const row = el.children[i]; if (i === 0) { const th = document.createElement('th'); th.innerHTML = 'Running Mate'; row.insertBefore(th, row.children[1]); } else { const name = row.cells[0]?.innerText?.replaceAll("-", "").trim(); const cell = row.insertCell(1); if (name && data[name]) { cell.innerHTML = data[name]; } } } }); resultsTableHolder.classList.add("done"); } const gameWindowNode = document.getElementById("game_window"); if (gameWindowNode) { if (window.candidateObserver) { window.candidateObserver.disconnect(); } window.candidateObserver = new MutationObserver((mutations) => { if (document.getElementById("overall_details_container")) { onGameWindowChangeCandidates(); } }); window.candidateObserver.observe(gameWindowNode, { childList: true, subtree: true }); } construct = (a = 1) => { e.page = e.page + a < e.pages.length ? e.page + a : 0 let html = e.header; html += e.pages[e.page] + "
"; if (e.page > 0) { html += ``; } if (e.page < e.pages.length - 1) { html += ``; } for (i in e.executable) { if (e.executable[i][0] == e.page) { e.executable[i][1](); } } if (e.image === true) { setTimeout(() => { let candImg = $(".person_image")[0]; if (candImg) { candImg.remove(); $("#final_results_description")[0].style = ` display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; color: white; max-height: 300px; overflow-y: auto; padding-right: 5px; margin-bottom: -20px; `; } }, 1); } else if (e.image) { setTimeout(() => { let candImg = $(".person_image")[0]; if (candImg) { candImg.src = e.image; $("#final_results_description")[0].style = ` display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; color: white; max-height: 300px; overflow-y: auto; padding-right: 5px; overflow-y: overlay; margin-bottom: -20px; `; } }, 1); } return html; } endingConstructor = (a = 1) => { $("#final_results_description")[0].innerHTML = construct(a); } // ENDINGS -- this is slightly more reliable than the ending code method e.multiple_endings = true; e.header = "

This is an error handler

"; e.pages = ["

error

"]; e.page = 0; endingPicker = (out, totv, aa, quickstats) => { orderID = [aa[0].candidate, aa[1].candidate, aa[2].candidate, aa[3].candidate]; if (!document.getElementById("ending-slide-style")) { const style = document.createElement("style"); style.id = "ending-slide-style"; style.innerHTML = ` .ending-slide { background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(241, 240, 236, 0.8), rgba(195, 196, 196, 0.6)); padding: 12px; color: #000000; border-radius:4px; box-shadow: 0 0 20px rgba(167, 54, 161, 0.5); margin: 1rem auto; max-width: 610px; line-height: 1.6; } .ending-slide.news { background: #ffffff; border: 2px solid #000; font-family: "Times New Roman", serif; box-shadow: none; } .ending-slide.ao3 { background: #ffffff; font-family: Georgia, "Times New Roman", serif; color: #222; border: 1px solid #bbb; } .ending-slide.ao3 .ao3-header { background: #900; color: #fff; padding: 10px 14px; font-weight: bold; font-size: 18px; } .ending-slide.ao3 .ao3-meta { padding: 10px 14px; font-size: 13px; border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd; line-height: 1.4; } .ending-slide.ao3 .ao3-meta span { display: block; } .ending-slide.ao3 .ao3-title { padding: 12px 14px 0 14px; font-size: 20px; font-weight: bold; } .ending-slide.ao3 .ao3-content { padding: 14px; font-size: 15px; line-height: 1.6; white-space: pre-wrap; } .ending-slide.ao3 a { color: #900; text-decoration: none; } .ending-slide.ao3 a:hover { text-decoration: underline; } .ending-slide.ao3 .ao3-author { padding: 2px 14px 10px 14px; font-size: 14px; } .ending-slide.ao3 .ao3-author a { color: #900; text-decoration: none; font-weight: bold; } .ending-slide.ao3 .ao3-author a:hover { text-decoration: underline; } `; document.head.appendChild(style); } e.pages = []; e.header = ""; e.executable = []; // -------------------------------------------- NORMAL ENDINGS -------------------------------------------------------------------- // king endings if (quickstats[0] == 538 && uniparty == 4) { e.pages.push(`
Archive of Our Own
A Billionaire, A Revolutionary
by Big Toni
Rating:General Audiences Pairing:Sanders/Trump Category:Romance

After a long and hard campaign trail victory has been achieved, and the knight in shining armor, Donald, and helpless princess, Bernie go back to the castle, the White House, to celebrate winning the election together. It wasn’t an easy victory, no, some would say it was a pyrrhic victory, for the pair had realized many things about each other, and truth be told, they were happy as ever to have learned so much about themselves in the process.

As they’re both driven to the White House, Bernie and Donald sit side by side from each other. They were laughing and chatting about all the funny hijinks the two had gotten up to with each other, Covid had brought them both down, it hit the economy so hard, and a lot of lives were lost from it, so the laughter the two shared with each other served as a good respite from all that mess

As the two were laughing, Bernie sat his hand down on the arm rest, and Donald, while not looking where he was going to put his hand down to rest, accidently touched Bernie’s hand. The car was dead silent, and the two looked at each other for a fleeting moment, and looked away, and the two blushed hard. “S-sorry” Donald said, sheepishly, not even managing to look at Bernie. The blush on two faces remained, stagnant, and everlasting.

The driver in the front of the car asked if everything was alright, and two quickly said yes, and the rest of the ride was ridden in silence. When the two reached the White House, they quickly exited the vehicle, and the rest of the night was a blur. The two slept in separate rooms, in separate halls, and the two had separate hearts, and they both woke up to a peaceful morning, a peaceful sky.

As the sun was rising in the sky, Donald walked up to the balcony, and faced the sun. While looking at the beautiful sun, he got a cigar and lit it and started smoking it. He enjoyed the amazing view of the Washington monument, and watched people on their morning jogs in D.C.

He remembers visiting it on the campaign trail, he giggles to himself about that wasted day. As he was enjoying the view, Donald heard a familiar voice, Bernie’s voice

Donald almost drops his cigar, he was scared, he didn’t want to face that man since the incident in the car, but now he has to face him, now he has to face the biggest challenge he has ever faced in his political career. “Donald.” That voice, it came from a Vermonter, from a man raised thousands of miles away from where the two are now today. “Donald”. The voice is getting louder, his heart is pounding. Bernie’s heart is also pounding, the two hearts are at risk of ripping from both of the men’s chest and embracing into one. “Donald!”. He can’t do it, he can’t face him. “DONALD!”. “I LOVE YOU!” Donald says, shouting with all of his might, the power of which shocked the two. “I love you two, Donald.” Donald drops his cigar, and the two embrace, the two hearts embrace, the billionaire and a revolutionary, at last, unite.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/bro.gif'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Y.M.C.A", "Village People", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/4/4c/Cruisin1978.jpg", "khOFB1Erzdg" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 339 && quickstats[0] < 535 && uniparty == 4) { // Massive victory e.pages.push(`

POPULISTS DOMINATE - ESTABLISHMENT IN DISARRAY?

Everyone was glued to their televisions. What were they going to witness? America felt more divided than ever, however, they all came together to watch the cable networks. CNN, Fox, MSNBC, they all showed the 'exit polls'. It astonished them, tab by tab, it all came out for Trump and Bernie. Trusted on the economy? Them. Trusted on immigration? Them. It was just the beginning for their movement. This was their America.

Trump was smug, as he always was. He knew this would happen. Bernie was ecstatic, knowing that their vision for America would beat out the same old establishment rhetoric. The atmosphere didn't feel tense at all, the staff at the White House began to get out the champagne. Celebrations would commence rapidly, as states would be called in for Rebirth. One by one, they fell like dominoes, and ensured that with their purple wave, a new era would follow.

"Guess you are a champagne socialist after all, Bernard!" Trump would say, patting him on the back. Bernie laughed. They felt like their political journeys had just been rejuvenated. Their legacies now woven together, and yet, it was almost never to be. If Clinton had picked Kaine, or if there was just one more Republican Senator, this would have never happened. Luck, and maybe, divine intervention brought them together for a purpose. That was all crazy talk, and yet it felt so real.

Then the call.

Trump was almost too eager to hear them defeated. He would relish it, he loved this sort of stuff. Bernie, on the other hand, didn't enjoy it too much, he would much rather just watch their speech, but it had to happen.

"Angus King is on the line, Mr President."

"Alright, pass him through."

A few moments of tension, it felt like forever, then a voice echoed throughout the phone. It sounded frail, like a bruised ego, but King had to do it, otherwise he'd face tremendous backlash.

"Congratulations, Mr President. Clearly, the nation has spoken, and you have won. I just hope that, uh, we can continue to work together and collaborate on issues where we agre-" He was interrupted, taken aback by how abrupt it was.

"Thank you Angus, truly is a pleasure, thank you. It was a hard fought campaign, and y'know, you were a good opponent, to be honest with you, but I guess some things aren't meant to be!" Trump's voice booming against King's frailness. It was hard to miss.

"Yeah, yeah- sure."

Angus didn't know what to say, what could he say? He was lost for words. He lost to a man who he saw was a threat, but he couldn't do anything now. It was Trump's world, and he was but a minor part in it.

`); e.pages.push(`

Rebirth makes a political COMEBACK of the ages - What's next?

Before election night, many people wrote off Donald Trump's and Bernie Sanders new political party, Rebirth, before it even began. But just 2 years later, they managed to turn the tables and pull out an enormous victory not seen since the 90s. Their purple wave even managed to translate into local and state success, as they had managed to pick up an additional 10 seats in the Senate and 123 in the House, propelling them as the real 3rd alternative in American politics. While the Democratic and Republican parties united under 1 presidential ticket, they did not do so in state or local races, leading to an even more divided legislature and one which coalitions will have to be made.

A few days ago, both Angus King and Mitt Romney, the presidential ticket chosen to stop Trump/Sanders, gave their concession speech to their crowd at D.C.

"It is with deep sorrow, and regret, that we have lost this presidential election. I am sorry to have let you down, and the country down, to have lost the chance to make this country proud of itself again. Proud of it's institutions, proud of it's legacy and it's way of life. Myself and Mitt Romney gave it our all, and we did not succeed. We want to wish President Trump and Vice President Sanders on their victory, and we wish them well. We will try our best to help collaborate and further American interests at home and aboard. That is all from me, Goodnight and God bless the United States of America."

Trump, on election night, declared victory once Texas had been called. He saw the states slide into place, he knew it. Victory was certain.

"Sorry to keep you waiting - complicated business!" Crowds cheered, they knew their savior won, and that they would have a glorious four years.

"This isn't about me or Bernie, this isn't about King or Romney, it's about you, the people. We, the people, as they say, y'know, we're going to Keep America Great, we're going to Make America Fair, and we're going to make sure our nation goes through a REBIRTH!"

"I have to say, that both Angus and Mitt, both good guys, maybe, but still, they fought hard, and deserve respect! They fought tremendously and with a lot of grit, and y'know, not many people can say that, so congratulations."

This new era of American politics has begun, and time will only tell what will happen between the now 3 major parties.

`); e.pages.push(`

Bernie wins the 2024 Rebirth nomination, Hawley and Kennedy bow out.

Bernie Sanders, the current Vice President of the United States and the Chair of the Rebirth Party, has subsequently won the 2024 Rebirth Primary nomination. This comes after both Senator Hawley and New York Representative Robert F. Kennedy Jr challenged him for the nomination. Bernie won 67.9% of the vote, while Hawley came a distant second at 19.2%, with Kennedy coming in at 9%. Hawley's shock win at Iowa gave the VP a run for his money, however he soon rebounded at New Hampshire and went out to win the nomination. The total delegate count for each candidate:

Bernie Sanders: 1236 Delegates Josh Hawley: 593 Delegates Robert F Kennedy Jr: 233 Delegates

Many pundits are expecting Hawley to be chosen as the Vice Presidential nominee, as with the success of tariffs across the globe, it would unify the protectionist element of the Rebirth party, and solidify that policy position. Furthermore, it would continue the status quo of a "Lefty-Righty" co-presidency, as first seen with Trump and Sanders themselves.

They have an uphill battle however, as with the end of the 'Trump' era in politics, new forces are emerging out of the 2 old parties. The Republican and Democratic primaries are incredibly crowded, as it gets closer to the convention, some do stand out, like Gretchen Whitmer of Michigan and Representative Ro Khanna of the Democrats. For the Republicans, Governor of New York Lee Zeldin and Senator Marco Rubio.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/tuff.jpg'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/The%20End%20Is%20Near.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/SandersVice.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "I Just Want To Rule The World", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20171157.png", "cCDxKzeSD7s" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 309 && quickstats[0] < 340 && uniparty == 4) { // Large victory e.pages.push(`

Significant win for Rebirth - Donald and Bernie vindicated?

Trump was happy with the result, as well as he could be. Bernie was feeling good as well, as he had thought they were heading to electoral disaster, but they managed to come out on top. Both men, who had once despised one another, had turned into political friends, and maybe even personal ones. They've had their fair share of arguments, of differences, but that didn't matter now. What mattered is their 'mandate' to rule for another 4 years. Victory.

"Fortune favours the bold Bernie, it's true, otherwise we would not be here right now. You and I, great team, tremendous even. The people put their trust in us, we gotta do something about it!"

Bernie chuckled. He felt lighter than usual, maybe it allowed him to open up a bit more to Donald.

"You're right, I suppose. We're going to Make America Fair, right? It's not your movement or mine, it's ours, and we have to fulfil our obligation to the people."

Then the call. Angus called a few minutes before this, but now it was Mitt's turn. Bernie's face soured a bit, but he had to do it. For 'tradition'

"Mitt Romney is on the line, sir."

"Put him through."

Tension filled the atmosphere. Everyone was eager to just get this done with.

"Hello, Mr Vice President. I've come to call to conceded this race to you and to offer you my congratulations. The American people have been resolute in their choice. I just hope that America can come together after this and heal from divisions."

Romney's voice, trying to sound warm, but it just couldn't. It was tragic.

"Yes, I'd like that very much Mitt, it would be nice to talk sometime again soon. Thank you for your congratulations. It's nice to have some decorum again. Let's all come together for unity of our nation, and our love of America. Again, thank you for the call. Talk to you soon."

Bernie couldn't put the phone down quicker if he tried. Bernie felt like he had a purpose now more than ever, and he was going to fulfill his pledge to the people.

`); e.pages.push(`

Populism validated within the United States - What happens now?

With America waking up to another 4 years of populism, what happens to the old order, the establishment? Some senior figures within the Democratic and Republican parties wish to hold talks of unifying the two parties, to try and prevent a Rebirth midterm/general election victory. On the other hand, they wish to further separate and embrace the more radical wings of their parties, ie the Progressive Caucus of the Democrats or the Tea Party element of the Republicans.

Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders, winners of the 2020 election, come out swinging with boosted approvals and a reinvigorated base of support after their electric victory speech. Here is a portion of Trump's victory speech:

"We're going to build a new, greater and fairer America. One of opportunity, prosperity and winning. Over the past 4 years, with Bernie, such a tremendous guy by the way, everyone give him a round of applause. Couldn't do it without him, y'know? Anyways, we're going to work hard, for you, the American people. And to those who are Democrats, Republicans and Independents, I urge you all now to rally behind the flag, our beautiful flag, we love it, and to bring unity and healing back to our nation."

This comes after a disappointing loss for Angus King and Mitt Romney, who sought to unseat the current occupants of the White House on a promise of renewed integrity and bringing order back into politics. They have called both the President and Vice President offering their congratulations and concessions, but have yet to do a concession speech to their supporters. They're expected to do so tomorrow.

It will be interesting to see what happens with this new populist movement cemented in American politics. Will it continue to grow in support, or will it gradually fade away, like the Progressives under Teddy Roosevelt, or Reform under Perot? Time will only tell.

`); e.pages.push(`

China humiliated - Taiwan remains defiant.

January 13th, 2023

After China invaded Taiwan on February 22nd 2022, efforts have been made by the Trump administration to bring an end to this bloody war on the island of Taiwan. They have succeeded, we can report.

The battle of Taichung proved decisive in kicking out the PLA, and effectively guaranteed the independence of Taiwan (or the Republic of China). This was with the extensive aid of the United States, led by Donald Trump, and further backing by the UK, wider NATO nations and Japan. Xi Jinping was forced into this position through a lack of supplies/logistics, and a demoralised army wanting to go home to their families.

This new peace deal marks a new age in the Asian continent and the wider world itself. America is bolstered by this huge political and economic victory, effectively gaining two permanent allies off the coast of China, and also ensuring that China is isolated from the rest of the world. Furthermore, the PRC has lost it's permanent seat within the United Nations Security Council, handing it over to the Japanese instead. Only the Russians now stand in the way of American dominance of the UNSC.

Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders are hailing this as a major boost for their foreign policy achievements, and this comes at a time where Rebirth did very well at the polls, giving them a slight majority within the House and a plurality in the Senate. In a twitter post, Trump states:

AMERICA IS BACK.

More to follow as China falls into turmoil. Tune in tomorrow at 5pm EST.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/tuff.jpg'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20215203.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20204531.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Avril", "Neil Cicierega", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Mouth%20Moods%20Outakes.jpeg", "z3lElikZD2Y" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 289 && quickstats[0] < 310 && uniparty == 4) { // Large victory e.pages.push(`

Significant win for Rebirth - Donald and Bernie vindicated?

Trump was happy with the result, as well as he could be. Bernie was feeling good as well, as he had thought they were heading to electoral disaster, but they managed to come out on top. Both men, who had once despised one another, had turned into political friends, and maybe even personal ones. They've had their fair share of arguments, of differences, but that didn't matter now. What mattered is their 'mandate' to rule for another 4 years. Victory.

"Fortune favours the bold Bernie, it's true, otherwise we would not be here right now. You and I, great team, tremendous even. The people put their trust in us, we gotta do something about it!"

Bernie chuckled. He felt lighter than usual, maybe it allowed him to open up a bit more to Donald.

"You're right, I suppose. We're going to Make America Fair, right? It's not your movement or mine, it's ours, and we have to fulfil our obligation to the people."

Then the call. Angus called a few minutes before this, but now it was Mitt's turn. Bernie's face soured a bit, but he had to do it. For 'tradition'

"Mitt Romney is on the line, sir."

"Put him through."

Tension filled the atmosphere. Everyone was eager to just get this done with.

"Hello, Mr Vice President. I've come to call to conceded this race to you and to offer you my congratulations. The American people have been resolute in their choice. I just hope that America can come together after this and heal from divisions."

Romney's voice, trying to sound warm, but it just couldn't. It was tragic.

"Yes, I'd like that very much Mitt, it would be nice to talk sometime again soon. Thank you for your congratulations. It's nice to have some decorum again. Let's all come together for unity of our nation, and our love of America. Again, thank you for the call. Talk to you soon."

Bernie couldn't put the phone down quicker if he tried. Bernie felt like he had a purpose now more than ever, and he was going to fulfill his pledge to the people.

`); e.pages.push(`

Populism validated within the United States - What happens now?

With America waking up to another 4 years of populism, what happens to the old order, the establishment? Some senior figures within the Democratic and Republican parties wish to hold talks of unifying the two parties, to try and prevent a Rebirth midterm/general election victory. On the other hand, they wish to further separate and embrace the more radical wings of their parties, ie the Progressive Caucus of the Democrats or the Tea Party element of the Republicans.

Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders, winners of the 2020 election, come out swinging with boosted approvals and a reinvigorated base of support after their electric victory speech. Here is a portion of Trump's victory speech:

"We're going to build a new, greater and fairer America. One of opportunity, prosperity and winning. Over the past 4 years, with Bernie, such a tremendous guy by the way, everyone give him a round of applause. Couldn't do it without him, y'know? Anyways, we're going to work hard, for you, the American people. And to those who are Democrats, Republicans and Independents, I urge you all now to rally behind the flag, our beautiful flag, we love it, and to bring unity and healing back to our nation."

This comes after a disappointing loss for Angus King and Mitt Romney, who sought to unseat the current occupants of the White House on a promise of renewed integrity and bringing order back into politics. They have called both the President and Vice President offering their congratulations and concessions, but have yet to do a concession speech to their supporters. They're expected to do so tomorrow.

It will be interesting to see what happens with this new populist movement cemented in American politics. Will it continue to grow in support, or will it gradually fade away, like the Progressives under Teddy Roosevelt, or Reform under Perot? Time will only tell.

`); e.pages.push(`

China humiliated - Taiwan remains defiant.

January 13th, 2023

After China invaded Taiwan on February 22nd 2022, efforts have been made by the Trump administration to bring an end to this bloody war on the island of Taiwan. They have succeeded, we can report.

The battle of Taichung proved decisive in kicking out the PLA, and effectively guaranteed the independence of Taiwan (or the Republic of China). This was with the extensive aid of the United States, led by Donald Trump, and further backing by the UK, wider NATO nations and Japan. Xi Jinping was forced into this position through a lack of supplies/logistics, and a demoralised army wanting to go home to their families.

This new peace deal marks a new age in the Asian continent and the wider world itself. America is bolstered by this huge political and economic victory, effectively gaining two permanent allies off the coast of China, and also ensuring that China is isolated from the rest of the world. Furthermore, the PRC has lost it's permanent seat within the United Nations Security Council, handing it over to the Japanese instead. Only the Russians now stand in the way of American dominance of the UNSC.

Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders are hailing this as a major boost for their foreign policy achievements, and this comes at a time where Rebirth did very well at the polls, giving them a slight majority within the House and a plurality in the Senate. In a twitter post, Trump states:

AMERICA IS BACK.

More to follow as China falls into turmoil. Tune in tomorrow at 5pm EST.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/tuff.jpg'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20215203.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20204531.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Avril", "Neil Cicierega", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Mouth%20Moods%20Outakes.jpeg", "z3lElikZD2Y" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 269 && quickstats[0] < 290 && uniparty == 4) { // Very close victory e.pages.push(`

VICTORY FOR REBIRTH - CAN IT LAST?

Relief swept over the White House. They had clinched the victory they sought for their whole lives. No longer can they be called 'illegitimate', doesn't matter whether or not it was close or not. Bernie sighed, worrying it could go one way or the other. Donald, on the other hand, never faltered, he always knew, deep down, they were going to win it. All night counting the votes, then recounting, then triple checking, it was exhausting, no one could bat an eye off the television.

"Don't worry Bernard, we have won. Y'know, not by the biggest margin, but hey, we take what we can get, right?" Donald pats him on the back, smiling.

"I suppose so, Donald. We've come far in this administration, I'm glad to call you a friend in this moment. For a better America." His smile was faint, he knew it was a nailbiter, Bernie was just glad to have won.

"Mr President, Senator Angus King wants to call."

This is what Trump was waiting for. Victory, no matter how bittersweet, he would still enjoy it to the last. He had effectively neutered King/Romney's political career.

Tension now filled the air. Then it rang.

"Mr President, congratulations on your victory, we fought a hard campaign, and now, we need to come together in the spirit of America and work collectively to ensure prosperity, for all Americans."

King was baffled. Trump trying to mend bridges? It wasn't like him. With such a 'small' win under his belt, maybe he was trying to bargain for some leverage?

"I agree Mr President, hopefully our nation can come together and unity can be achieved. Anyways, uh, I best make my concession speech. Good talking, Mr President."

Donald hung up. He stood up, and went to Bernie.

"We have to go out to our supporters, we have to, it'll be tremendous, trust me!"

`); e.pages.push(`

Disappointing night for Democrats and Republicans - but there is a glimmer.

As the final results for Congress seep in, it looks to be a deadlocked legislature. Rebirth, once a pariah in American politics, now stands tall with an additional 53 seats in the House and 9 Senate seats. This shockwave across America has startled the two main parties - the Democrats and Republicans - into action. With Rebirth's 84 seats within the legislature, they only control about 20% of the House overall, meaning that both Democrats and Republicans still have a duopoly on the political system. They could very well work together to install an anti-Rebirth figure as Speaker, albeit having to compromise between the wings of each party will be a task not many people want...

As for the Senate, it'll also be very difficult for Rebirth to get any sort of legislation through, as with only 16 Senate seats, it'll be a struggle to reach a consensus on anything major, like a budget or foreign policy. Both parties could use this to their advantage and block the Trump/Sanders administration of anything major, effectively deadlocking government until the tide turns against Populism. Bernie Sanders' influence within the Senate as the VP would be greatly diminished with this as well, cutting off a vital source of executive power from the Administration.

This may be why Trump's rhetoric and demeanour has considerably been toned down, as to hopefully reach a bi-partisan consensus on many issues within America, including the economy and most importantly, on his signature policy, immigration.

"We've got to come together now, as one nation, otherwise this division and hatred we've seen, it'll tear us apart. Bernie tells me about it all the time, and frankly, y'know, we gotta put an end to it. Our people, our American people, they need a break. We'll give that to them. Let's come together now, whether you're a Republican, Democrat, Rebirth or Independent, now is the time to heal. Let's Keep America Great everyone."

This comes after Trump/Sanders approval ratings have enjoyed a rather small bump in the ratings, which might help them recover some lost ground within Congress.

`); e.pages.push(`

SANDERS NOMINATED FOR REBIRTH TICKET - VENTURA TAPPED AS VP

Bernie Sanders, Vice President of the United States, has officially won the nomination of the Rebirth Party. While it wasn't necessarily a 'tough' challenge for the nomination, he did encounter some minor obstacles, like his stance on Trump's national emergency announcement on the Southern Border, and the response to the 2021 Russian annexation of Belarus. Such things did not go unnoticed, and the current approvals of the Trump/Sanders administration are at a bit of a downturn, at -4%. With this in mind, and including many other factors, Bernie Sanders has officially picked former WWE star and Reform/Independence Governor of Minnesota Jesse Ventura.

This has shocked many pundits, and betting markets have also taken a bit of a dive, as many were expecting someone like Josh Hawley or Rand Paul to be selected for VP. However, many at the convention were cheering it on, shouting: "VEN-TURA, VEN-TURA, VEN-TURA!"

This, along with high personal approvals from Independents, has turned out to be a smart move politically by Sanders. While some of the more conservative elements of the party aren't pleased with this, many have stayed with Rebirth, worrying that a potential Newsom/Moore ticket could damage what they've done in office.

Later today, Donald Trump, 45th President of the United States, is expected to speak at the Rebirth National Convention. Already he has spoken on Twitter, stating:

"FULLY ENDORSE Sanders/Ventura. GREAT TEAM!! They know what's going on in this country, THEY SPEAK FOR YOU! I knew Ventura back in the Reform days, HE'S A SMART GUY! Sanders, I've gotten to know for 8 years now. Solid guy, he has my FULL endorsement. DRAIN THE SWAMP!! DJT."

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20215203.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20215033.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/bernieslide.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Sweeter Dreams", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20183108.png", "ijSmOtBiyeY" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] == 269 && uniparty == 4) { // Deadlocked election e.pages.push(`

NATION STUCK IN GRIDLOCK ONCE MORE - THE WHITE HOUSE IN PERIL

It can’t be. How could this happen again? It should’ve been easy, it should have been a toast to Rebirth, but destiny had different plans. You won some swing states, and you lost some, and it’s led to this. Bernie looks horrified, while Trump, sat in the corner, has a frown upon his face. He sees it as a farce, and yet, both of them know what happened last time.

“This- this isn’t quits Bernard, not yet, I won’t allow this shit to ruin my legacy. We were meant to dominate, the polls, they had us so far ahead, they rigged it Bernie, they wanted us gone so badly. It was al-”

“Trump- we fought the good fight, I accept it just as much as you do, but we can still carry on the torch. We have to be realistic, we don’t stand a chance in the House or Senate, the cards are stacked against us, but we can still put up a fight.”

“I won’t let that little man Romney be smug over me, no way in hell. We’re going to fight like hell, call up every friendly Senator, every Representative, we’re going to drag this through as long as possible. I- We, we won’t have our legacy ruined.”

Then they called. Trump let it go to voicemail, he didn’t want to give them the satisfaction of being obsessed, no, he was above that. Besides, why would he want to speak to King or Romney? They were sore losers in his eyes, he couldn’t stand them.

These were going to be a long few months.

`); e.pages.push(`

PRESIDENT KING AND VP ROMNEY SWORN IN - WHAT NOW?

January 21st, 2021.

A Nation stood still, perhaps for the first time in 4 years. Donald Trump was nowhere to be seen at the inauguration. He saw it as an embarrassment on his record and his persona. He couldn’t believe it, his allies in Congress, they betrayed him, they had forgotten all that they did for him. He was fuming, but he had no choice. He was at Mar-A-Lago, playing Golf while Angus King, Independent Senator from Maine, was sworn in as the 46th President of the United States.

“Angus Stanley King Jr., do solemnly swear, that I will faithfully execute the Office of President of the United States.”

Bernie Sanders was in attendance. He had to be. With Donald missing, he was the chief spokesperson there for Rebirth. Even he felt humiliated, his political ally abandoned him to play golf in Florida. Regardless, he did it for the good of the nation. What choice did he have? In any other scenario, he’d be absolutely hounded by the media.

Then, King went over to him.

“I’m sorry it had to come to this, my friend. I know- listen, I know you have your grievances, we both do, but we should put them aside…for the good of the nation. This isn’t about you or me or Trump anymore. It’s about the United States of America.”

Bernie put on a smile.

“Of course. I wish you all the best.”

At the very least, he knew the country was still in safe hands, with Angus anyways. He couldn’t trust Romney, but King was a different story. They were both independents, and knew their electorate well. Bernie just hoped he wouldn’t bow down to the GOP, otherwise, hell would be coming for them both.

`); e.pages.push(`

TRUMP WINS NEW YORK GOVERNORS RACE - PLEDGES COOPERATION WITH MAYOR AOC

November 10th, 2022

Donald Trump, 45th President of the United States, has just re-entered national politics by beating out the two main parties in New York, coming in a strong first place with 45% of the vote. Andrew Guiliani, the Republican, came in a distant second, with 32% of the vote, and Jumaane Williams, the Democrat, came in third, with 23% of the vote, mainly due to his low-profile campaign and a lack of visibility.

This has cemented New Yorks radical shift towards the ‘Rebirth’ Party, as previously in 2021, Alexandria Ocasio Cortez, previously a Representative for New Yorks 14th district, was elected as the Mayor of New York City, focusing on affordability, the cost of living and inaction across the city. This ultimately led to Donald Trump’s push for the Governorship bid, as sources stated:

“Without AOC proving that Rebirth can win in New York, Trump would have bowed out of national politics ever since his bitter loss in 2020. But he now sees an avenue to a return into the spotlight.”

In a statement, Former President Trump said that he is willing to “fully cooperate and give a major helping hand to the people of New York City and to their BEAUTIFUL Mayor Alexandria Ocasio Cortez.”

“Frankly, the Democrats have taken you all for granted, and we’re here to shake things up here in New York. This is my home, and I will always stand with our beautiful people. AOC has done a terrific job, and I cannot wait to meet her. She’s presidential material folks!”

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20204830.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20203344.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20203154.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Night Of Fire", "Niko", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20193642.png", "SRbhLtjOiRc" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] >= 259 && quickstats[0] < 269 && uniparty == 4) { // tiny loss e.pages.push(`

"TRUMP CLAIMS FOUL PLAY - BATTLE RAGES ON FOR WHITE HOUSE"

The wall has crumbled. Donald and Bernie sat together in the White House, seeing the results trickle in. Then it hit. One by one, the states they needed to win, fell into the Establishment's hand. How could this be? They had it in the bag. It felt impossible. Trump couldn't believe it. Bernie was mute. How? Why? This couldn't be. Then it hit. The anger, the frustration, the turmoil.

"Get my staff on the line, I want to do a press conference, NOW." Trump was far from amused. He saw it, everyone did. It was 'stolen' from him. Bernie was trying to calm him down, but to no avail.

"Donald, if you do this, I'm going to have to go against this. American democracy is on the line if you do this, please, reconsider. If not for yourself, then do it for your children and the movement. Everything we will have built, will all be for nothing. Please, Donald, for the country."

Bernie was uneasy, but what he had said, it was genuine. His heart was in the right place. It was all up to Donald Trump now.

"Listen, Bernard, I respect you, truly, I do, but if you can't see what's happening, then frankly you're blind. This election was stolen from us. It was our moment, our real victory. The establishment, those birdbrains in Washington, they have no idea. Our movement, our patriotic movement, they'll see right through it. We have to do it, for our base.

It didn't work. Bernie's words, they meant something to Donald, but to no avail. He was so ingrained that he had won, nothing could stop him.

Then the Press Conference came. Bernie was nowhere to be seen. He was too embarrassed.

"This election, frankly, we should've won it. We did win, in fact. They rigged it against me, against us. These frauds cheated us out of 4 more years. ANGUS KING AND MITT ROMNEY ARE A DISGRACE! This election was RIGGED."

He actually said it. There's no going back now.

`); e.pages.push(`

"BERNIE BREAKS FROM TRUMP - KING/ROMNEY REMAIN DEFIANT"

In a move which surprises political voices across America, Bernie Sanders, the current Vice President, breaks from Trump's rhetoric. In a statement in front of his supporters, Bernie officially conceded to President-Elect Angus King and Vice President-Elect Mitt Romney. The VP and VP-Elect plan to meet in the next few days, to hash out a transition plan and to ensure that it goes as smoothly as possible.

"I am sorry to have lost this battle for you, and I am sorry that I could not deliver the change that Donald and I promised. I won't go down the path of denialism, because that only leads to further division, damage and hostility. Donald Trump is a good friend of mine, but he's wrong, and ultimately, if I have to suffer with that, so be it."

This comes after almost universal condemnation of Trump's comments made on election night, especially from the Democratic and Republican leadership. Now that Bernie has joined in with this chorus, it is unclear what path Trump will take now, but he has doubled down today, stating on Twitter:

"THIS ELECTION WAS RIGGED! COMPLETELY FRAUDULENT!! We need to STOP THE STEAL and MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN!! There will be NO KINGS IN AMERICA!"

It remains to be seen what will happen come certification of the results, however, it seems likely that a protest will take place and the transition somewhat delayed. More to come throughout the day.

`); e.pages.push(`

"Government Shutdown has just happened - The Winners and Losers"

With a disastrous showing at the Midterms, President Angus King and Vice President Mitt Romney have decided to hunker down and veto anything major from a Congress dominated by Rebirth. After getting a vote of confidence from the now diminished Republican Party, there emerges a new speaker.

Donald John Trump is hereby elected on the 15th ballot, and is hereby named Speaker of the House of Representatives.

No one had expected this, however since winning Florida's 22nd Congressional in 2022, Donald Trump has returned to national politics and grabbed the headlines once again. As for Bernie Sanders, the previous Vice President? He fought for his old Senate seat back in Vermont, and won it from Patrick Leahy. Both men have now returned into the national spotlight.

It is unclear as to whether or not the two men have reconciled since 2021, since Donald Trump falsely claimed that the Presidential Election in 2020 was 'rigged against him' and that 'Bernard betrayed him.' However, Trump has signalled his willingness to run for a third time, and potentially with Bernie Sanders as VP again, if he accepts.

"Well, y'know, I wasn't too happy with Bernie when he did what he did, but I'm a forgiving person, that's what Jesus taught, right? Anyways- listen, me and him, we haven't spoken in a little while actually, but I'm sure he's a good man still. We should hash things out, so yeah, I'd be willing to talk with him, one hundred percent.

On the other side, Bernie Sanders has so far refused to comment on this development.

The Winners: Donald Trump, The Rebirth Party, and Bernard Sanders

The Losers: The Democratic Party, President Angus King, Vice President Mitt Romney

Undetermined: The Republican Party, The American People

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20203813.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20202533.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20202237.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Capitol Hill", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20193822.png", "amX6zfQGGtk" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] >= 229 && quickstats[0] < 259 && uniparty == 4) { // Very close defeat e.pages.push(`

"TRUMP CLAIMS FOUL PLAY - BATTLE RAGES ON FOR WHITE HOUSE"

The wall has crumbled. Donald and Bernie sat together in the White House, seeing the results trickle in. Then it hit. One by one, the states they needed to win, fell into the Establishment's hand. How could this be? They had it in the bag. It felt impossible. Trump couldn't believe it. Bernie was mute. How? Why? This couldn't be. Then it hit. The anger, the frustration, the turmoil.

"Get my staff on the line, I want to do a press conference, NOW." Trump was far from amused. He saw it, everyone did. It was 'stolen' from him. Bernie was trying to calm him down, but to no avail.

"Donald, if you do this, I'm going to have to go against this. American democracy is on the line if you do this, please, reconsider. If not for yourself, then do it for your children and the movement. Everything we will have built, will all be for nothing. Please, Donald, for the country."

Bernie was uneasy, but what he had said, it was genuine. His heart was in the right place. It was all up to Donald Trump now.

"Listen, Bernard, I respect you, truly, I do, but if you can't see what's happening, then frankly you're blind. This election was stolen from us. It was our moment, our real victory. The establishment, those birdbrains in Washington, they have no idea. Our movement, our patriotic movement, they'll see right through it. We have to do it, for our base.

It didn't work. Bernie's words, they meant something to Donald, but to no avail. He was so ingrained that he had won, nothing could stop him.

Then the Press Conference came. Bernie was nowhere to be seen. He was too embarrassed.

"This election, frankly, we should've won it. We did win, in fact. They rigged it against me, against us. These frauds cheated us out of 4 more years. ANGUS KING AND MITT ROMNEY ARE A DISGRACE! This election was RIGGED."

He actually said it. There's no going back now.

"BERNIE BREAKS FROM TRUMP - KING/ROMNEY REMAIN DEFIANT"

In a move which surprises political voices across America, Bernie Sanders, the current Vice President, breaks from Trump's rhetoric. In a statement in front of his supporters, Bernie officially conceded to President-Elect Angus King and Vice President-Elect Mitt Romney. The VP and VP-Elect plan to meet in the next few days, to hash out a transition plan and to ensure that it goes as smoothly as possible.

"I am sorry to have lost this battle for you, and I am sorry that I could not deliver the change that Donald and I promised. I won't go down the path of denialism, because that only leads to further division, damage and hostility. Donald Trump is a good friend of mine, but he's wrong, and ultimately, if I have to suffer with that, so be it."

`); e.pages.push(`

"Government Shutdown has just happened - The Winners and Losers"

With a disastrous showing at the Midterms, President Angus King and Vice President Mitt Romney have decided to hunker down and veto anything major from a Congress dominated by Rebirth. After getting a vote of confidence from the now diminished Republican Party, there emerges a new speaker.

Donald John Trump is hereby elected on the 15th ballot, and is hereby named Speaker of the House of Representatives.

No one had expected this, however since winning Florida's 22nd Congressional in 2022, Donald Trump has returned to national politics and grabbed the headlines once again. As for Bernie Sanders, the previous Vice President? He fought for his old Senate seat back in Vermont, and won it from Patrick Leahy. Both men have now returned into the national spotlight.

It is unclear as to whether or not the two men have reconciled since 2021, since Donald Trump falsely claimed that the Presidential Election in 2020 was 'rigged against him' and that 'Bernard betrayed him.' However, Trump has signalled his willingness to run for a third time, and potentially with Bernie Sanders as VP again, if he accepts.

"Well, y'know, I wasn't too happy with Bernie when he did what he did, but I'm a forgiving person, that's what Jesus taught, right? Anyways- listen, me and him, we haven't spoken in a little while actually, but I'm sure he's a good man still. We should hash things out, so yeah, I'd be willing to talk with him, one hundred percent.

On the other side, Bernie Sanders has so far refused to comment on this development.

The Winners: Donald Trump, The Rebirth Party, and Bernard Sanders

The Losers: The Democratic Party, President Angus King, Vice President Mitt Romney

Undetermined: The Republican Party, The American People

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20203813.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20202533.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20202237.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Capitol Hill", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20193822.png", "amX6zfQGGtk" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] >= 191 && quickstats[0] < 229 && uniparty == 4) { // Realistic loss e.pages.push(`

TRUMP BATTLES ELECTION RESULTS - WHITE HOUSE IN CHAOS

One by one, they all fell. Trump was dismayed. Bernie was shocked. How could this be? Their movement, their political 'revolution', it was abysmal. Trump couldn't contain his anger.

"We should've won this, by a lot. I think we did actually, these crooks, the establishment Bernie, it was them. They rigged it against us. We need to fight back, we need to, otherwise we won't have a country anymore. Bernard, we've fought hard, and we need to keep fighting, for the base, they'll love it."

"We should've won this, by a lot. I think we did actually, these crooks, the establishment Bernie, it was them. They rigged it against us. We need to fight back, we need to, otherwise we won't have a country anymore. Bernard, we've fought hard, and we need to keep fighting, for the base, they'll love it."

Bernie looked at him. You can read his face almost immediately. One of sadness that they lost, but also of confusion and slight frustration. Why would Donald Trump suggest this to him? He didn't know what to do.

"Don- Donald, please, we need to calm things down, smooth over relations, we can't, uh, just proclaim it was stolen. Whether or not I agree with you is a uh, separate issue, but we need to cool things down. It's been a tough election, and we need to talk to the public. We have to, for the country."

Tension filled the room, both men looking into each others eyes. Was the movement over at this moment? It was hard to say, but cracks were definitely beginning to show between the two juggernauts.

"Bernard, you can't convince me otherwise. You have to see it, you have to, this was stolen right in front of us. I'm going to speak to the public very soon, and believe me, it'll be a doozy. I won't sit back and watch our country be taken from us. I won't let it happen. You're either with me, or against me."

"Then so be it. I quit."

Bernie was quick to leave the White House, and soon gathered a quick crowd outside Washington D.C.

"I have just left the White House and tendered my resignation. While I tried to work with Donald, and we had found common ground, his rhetoric was too much to bare. He has also claimed that this election was fraudulent. Let me be clear, uh, it wasn't. Congratulations to Angus King and Mitt Romney, and I wish them all the success."

The war of the Populists, had just begun.

`); e.pages.push(`

The Sentence which broke Rebirth - How it unfolded.

The air in D.C felt cold. Too cold. It felt bitter, as if something had died there that day. People didn't leave their homes, the streets emptied. What had happened to cause such disarray? Two men, both giants in their own rights, had caused the biggest political breakup in American history. How did it have happened? Everyone had just witnessed the destruction of Rebirth, and with it, a Populist movement extinguished with one man's ego and another man's speech.

"I have just left the White House and tendered my resignation."

Those words broke the camels back, so to speak. Whatever was said after that was irrelevant. What's done was done. Rebirth, at that very moment, dissolved into a million pieces, and not one person could begin to pick it back up. Trump was raging as he watched his screen, his hands clenched as Bernie continued to speak about what had transpired. He needed to fight back, he needed to bring the narrative back onto his side. He couldn't lose this, he didn't know how to 'lose', as he would put it.

He felt like he had to say something. He had to dispel whatever Bernard had said. He could not be outshined by what he described now as a 'Crazed Communist Lunatic'. Donald Trump, 45th President of the United States and Commander-in-Chief, would NOT stand for it.

"Bernard, I liked him, I liked him a lot actually, but he's betrayed me, and frankly, I won't stand for it. I've given him plenty of chances, plenty of policies, and he throws it back at me. If he doesn't want to stand by me, so be it, but I'll remember. I value loyalty, who doesn't? I guess not Bernie, but it's okay, he'll soon figure it out."

Did he just threaten Bernie Sanders? War had just broken out, and there was no way out. The future will be very interesting...

`); e.pages.push(`

REBIRTH OFFICIALLY DISBANDED - BERNIE PLANS THIRD PARTY RUN

With the Rebirth Party being disbanded just last year, many are still questioning whether or not a third party run is still viable. However, we can report that Bernie Sanders, former Vice President of the United States, is going to run as a third party, joining the Green Party. While many are shocked at this development, people who knew Sanders were not so surprised.

Mary Jane Sanders stated: "He's always disliked how much of a stranglehold the Establishment has on America, and he's always wanted to change that. He saw his chance with Rebirth, and ever since that collapsed, he's been weighing his options. To be honest, I'm not really shocked or surprised. He's a good man at heart, and ultimately, I support him."

With this new development taking place, the Green Party has exploded with membership, going from 250,000 members to 500,000 in just a short few days. Green Party founders such as Howie Hawkins and John Rensenbrink have fully endorsed Sanders, claiming that he can: "Bring back the message of peace, freedom and justice to the American public, and actually mean it."

Meanwhile, other notable Green Party members, like Jill Stein, promises to fight on in the primaries, stating that there shouldn't be a coronation of Bernie Sanders, who she states is a 'deep-state agent' and part of the 'elite'. Bernie Sanders has yet to comment on Jill and her run, but he promises "stability" from the "chaotic four years of a King/Romney Administration". This comes after yet another round of negotiations for a Government Shutdown have failed.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20204830.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20202533.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/bernieslide.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "The End", "Neil Cicierega", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20205628.png", "3cXjcKTRWcg" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 134 && quickstats[0] < 191 && uniparty == 4) { // Landslide loss e.pages.push(`

TRUMP AND SANDERS LOST - THE SOUL OF THE NATION RESTORED

Denial. Anger. Bargaining. Depression. Acceptance. This is what the Trump/Sanders ticket felt during the latter stages of the night. They had lost. The nation soundly rejected their vision for America. Donald looked down, sitting across from Bernie with a glass of water. He never drank, but tonight? He came close to it. Bernie sat opposite, his face muddled with a bitter sadness. What did they do wrong to deserve this? Was it the Almighty sending a signal? Was it that their brand of populism didn't resonate with the public? That was for the analysts to determine, not them. All they could do is bask in the moment of their own making. It came together as quickly as it fell apart.

Donald took some time to reflect. His ego, his macho-man stance, it was bruised. He couldn't accept it at first, he was bitter and he was angry at himself for letting down what he saw as his movement, his prized possession. What could he do now? The public clearly chose them over him. There was no point fighting a battle he knew he couldn't win. He thought, and thought, and ultimately, he decided to concede to Angus King the next day. He couldn't do it now, not in his state.

"I'm sorry, Bernard. I've let down the movement, y'know, the base. They wanted us so badly to win, I could feel it, I truly could, but that's gone now. We were a great team, the best, but again, I'm sorry for the results. I'll take responsibility, for the party, and for you."

Bernie looked up at him. What? He showed some...humility? This wasn't the so-called "con-man" everyone despised, he was a human being. A genuine man, with a heart. The same as him. Sanders didn't know what to say. Why would he? Donald showed compassion, empathy and responsibility. It wasn't like him.

"It's okay Donald, it's alright. Listen, uh, I appreciate you taking responsibility. But it's mine as well. We fought this election, together. We fought for what we believed was right and just, and we gave it our all. There's no shame in that, and uh, thank you for giving the people of America a voice. We both did. You and I, we're not so different after all."

They both stood up and shook hands, and gave each other a solemn hug with a pat on the back. It was over, but they gave it their all, and fought for America.

`); e.pages.push(`

HUMILITY AND GRACE - HOW THE REBIRTH TICKET CONCEDED

It was the end of the road for Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders. They knew that their time in power was over. That morning, they prepared themselves for their speeches. They saw something that they had not seen in their political careers before, a sense of unwavering loyalty to a cause bigger than themselves, a sense of pride that they fought till the end. They gave each other a handshake, and went out to the podium to their most loyal of supporters.

"Last night, I called Angus King to congratulate him on his win. While this wasn't our expected or desired outcome, we need to come together now, for the country, for our great US of A. We fought a tough campaign, it was tough, and we fought like hell, but we came up short. Maybe one day we'll return, who knows? But I'm- I'm sorry for having lost this for you-"

The crowd, once silent and lost for words, now cheer on his name. He may have let them down, but they still believed in him. They wanted him to fight more, to bring out his energy again, but to no avail. Donald was tired from the fight. He knew his end of the road was a dead-end, for now. President Donald John Trump, once a juggernaut, is now a shell of his former self. For once in his life, he felt...lost. Could it be that their era of American politics was over before it even begun? He didn't want to believe it, but it seemed that way...

Then Bernie spoke.

"Well, uh, thank you Donald, we fought for America, and while we came up short, I'm proud of what we've achieved together. We started out rough at the beginning, and now, uh, I can call you a dear friend and a loyal man. We brought it all to the table, and we gave it our all, but sometimes the thing we desire most is out of our reach. It's time to heal now. Thank you, to our uh, loyal supporters. We love you."

Time will tell as to whether or not they can bring back their support, but for now, they have to accept their loss, and take a much needed break from the pressures of Washington politics.

`); e.pages.push(`

REBIRTH STORM CONGRESS - PRESIDENT KING DECLINES TO RUN FOR SECOND TERM

After a miserable performance in the 2022 Midterm elections, President Angus King has officially bowed out of the 2024 General Election. This comes after many months of speculation regarding President King's health and his subsequent 'coughing fits' that have happened during his recent press conferences. In an address to the nation, the President urged for unity and healing after what was considered a 'bloodbath' for the two main parties, as Rebirth took a majority holding in the House and a plurality within the Senate.

"It's time for our nation to come together, to heal. For too long, we've been bitterly divided over left and right, over what's just and immoral. We are Americans, and we will continue to be the best nation on this planet. However, with the results of the Midterms and my own personal life, I hereby withdraw myself from the 2024 Presidential Election. Now is the time for a new generation to take over, and to bring forth a new era for American politics."

While not implicitly stated, the President did not endorse Vice President Mitt Romney, and even suggested that a newer, more younger generation take their place. While the Vice President has kept quiet, insiders are reporting that he does intend to run for re-election, seeking the Presidential nomination for the third time in a row (2008, 2012 and now 2024).

On the other side of the isle, the Rebirth Senate leader, former Vice President Bernie Sanders, who came back to the Senate through Vermont, has declined to run in 2024, citing his own personal life and his previous 2020 loss as 'a stain which can never be removed.'

Donald Trump on the other hand? He has decided to go full throttle back into the spotlight, being elected as the Senator for Florida and filing a run for the Rebirth nomination for the second time. Time will tell as to whether or not he still commands a large part of Rebirth, but he faces challenges from both his left and right flank. These include:

Senator Josh Hawley from Missouri Representative Alexandria Ocasio Cortez from New York Former Governor Jesse Ventura from Minnesota Surgeon Ben Carson from Michigan

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20204830.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-27%20144223.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-27%20145731.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "What A Loser", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-27%20150131.png", "EqFQ61lX528" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 2 && quickstats[0] <= 134 && uniparty == 4) { // Ridiculous loss e.pages.push(`
