Bernie sat opposite, his face muddled with a bitter sadness. What did they do wrong to deserve this? Was it the Almighty sending a signal? Was it that their brand of populism didn't resonate with the public? That was for the analysts to determine, not them. All they could do is bask in the moment of their own making. It came together as quickly as it fell apart.

Donald took some time to reflect. His ego, his macho-man stance, it was bruised. He couldn't accept it at first, he was bitter and he was angry at himself for letting down what he saw as his movement, his prized possession. What could he do now? The public clearly chose them over him. There was no point fighting a battle he knew he couldn't win. He thought, and thought, and ultimately, he decided to concede to Angus King the next day. We fought for what we believed was right and just, and we gave it our all. There's no shame in that, and uh, thank you for giving the people of America a voice. We both did. You and I, we're not so different after all."

`); e.pages.push(`

REBIRTH STORM CONGRESS - PRESIDENT KING DECLINES TO RUN FOR SECOND TERM

After a miserable performance in the 2022 Midterm elections, President Angus King has officially bowed out of the 2024 General Election. This comes after many months of speculation regarding President King's health and his subsequent 'coughing fits' that have happened during his recent press conferences. In an address to the nation, the President urged for unity and healing after what was considered a 'bloodbath' for the two main parties, as Rebirth took a majority holding in the House and a plurality within the Senate.

"It's time for our nation to come together, to heal. For too long, we've been bitterly divided over left and right, over what's just and immoral. We are Americans, and we will continue to be the best nation on this planet. However, with the results of the Midterms and my own personal life, I hereby withdraw myself from the 2024 Presidential Election. Now is the time for a new generation to take over, and to bring forth a new era for American politics."

While not implicitly stated, the President did not endorse Vice President Mitt Romney, and even suggested that a newer, more younger generation take their place. While the Vice President has kept quiet, insiders are reporting that he does intend to run for re-election, seeking the Presidential nomination for the third time in a row (2008, 2012 and now 2024).

On the other side of the isle, the Rebirth Senate leader, former Vice President Bernie Sanders, who came back to the Senate through Vermont, has declined to run in 2024, citing his own personal life and his previous 2020 loss as 'a stain which can never be removed.'

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20204830.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-27%20144223.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-27%20145731.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "What A Loser", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-27%20150131.png", "EqFQ61lX528" ); } } //bloomberg endings if (quickstats[0] == 538 && uniparty == 2) { e.pages.push(`
Archive of Our Own
A Billionaire, A Revolutionary
by Big Toni
Rating:General Audiences Pairing:Sanders/Trump Category:Romance

After a long and hard campaign trail victory has been achieved, and the knight in shining armor, Donald, and helpless princess, Bernie go back to the castle, the White House, to celebrate winning the election together. It wasn’t an easy victory, no, some would say it was a pyrrhic victory, for the pair had realized many things about each other, and truth be told, they were happy as ever to have learned so much about themselves in the process.

As they’re both driven to the White House, Bernie and Donald sit side by side from each other. They were laughing and chatting about all the funny hijinks the two had gotten up to with each other, Covid had brought them both down, it hit the economy so hard, and a lot of lives were lost from it, so the laughter the two shared with each other served as a good respite from all that mess

As the two were laughing, Bernie sat his hand down on the arm rest, and Donald, while not looking where he was going to put his hand down to rest, accidently touched Bernie’s hand. The car was dead silent, and the two looked at each other for a fleeting moment, and looked away, and the two blushed hard. “S-sorry” Donald said, sheepishly, not even managing to look at Bernie. The blush on two faces remained, stagnant, and everlasting.

The driver in the front of the car asked if everything was alright, and two quickly said yes, and the rest of the ride was ridden in silence. When the two reached the White House, they quickly exited the vehicle, and the rest of the night was a blur. The two slept in separate rooms, in separate halls, and the two had separate hearts, and they both woke up to a peaceful morning, a peaceful sky.

As the sun was rising in the sky, Donald walked up to the balcony, and faced the sun. While looking at the beautiful sun, he got a cigar and lit it and started smoking it. He enjoyed the amazing view of the Washington monument, and watched people on their morning jogs in D.C.

He remembers visiting it on the campaign trail, he giggles to himself about that wasted day. As he was enjoying the view, Donald heard a familiar voice, Bernie’s voice

Donald almost drops his cigar, he was scared, he didn’t want to face that man since the incident in the car, but now he has to face him, now he has to face the biggest challenge he has ever faced in his political career. “Donald.” That voice, it came from a Vermonter, from a man raised thousands of miles away from where the two are now today. “Donald”. The voice is getting louder, his heart is pounding. Bernie’s heart is also pounding, the two hearts are at risk of ripping from both of the men’s chest and embracing into one. “Donald!”. He can’t do it, he can’t face him. “DONALD!”. “I LOVE YOU!” Donald says, shouting with all of his might, the power of which shocked the two. “I love you two, Donald.” Donald drops his cigar, and the two embrace, the two hearts embrace, the billionaire and a revolutionary, at last, unite.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/bro.gif'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Y.M.C.A", "Village People", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/4/4c/Cruisin1978.jpg", "khOFB1Erzdg" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 339 && quickstats[0] <= 535 && uniparty == 2) { // Massive victory e.pages.push(`

REBIRTH CEMENTED AS NATIONAL PARTY - UNITY TICKET DESTROYED IN LANDSLIDE

Victory. Revenge. Trump always had those words confused. What he saw on Election Night was pure revenge to him. Bloomberg dared to defy him, his legacy, his status, and for that, he suffered his punishment. Humiliation. It was beautiful in his eyes. Bernie saw it differently. He had given up everything to be in this position now, and it had paid off. What he felt was more relief than anything else. No one blamed him, after all, he was working with someone he called a sexist, a bigot, an enemy of the state. That was 4 years ago. How the country has changed in just 4 short years was remarkable to both men.

“I never had any doubts, none whatsoever Bernard, this was too easy. Mike Bloomberg, what a loser, couldn’t even reach 200 votes! Y’know, I supported this guy for Mayor of NYC, my home, and now he thought to betray me? Now he knows.”

Donald’s face was having all sorts of emotion. One minute he would have that big grin encompassing the room, and the next minute, he would go into a frown. No one was speaking about his ‘enormous’ win, they, the so-called ‘fake news media’ were focused on how Bloomberg did so poorly. This was his night, his moment, his victory, and yet it was not about him. Bernie saw the look on his face, and n

“Well, uh, Mr President, it’s been a real journey, and a long one at that. We’ve toured the country, we’ve made promises, we need to act on them as soon as the celebrations are done with. We have a country to run, and we need to do whatever we can to free the American people from being downtrodden. We-”

A ring from the phone interrupted Sanders. Both of them knew who it was, and neither wanted to pick up the phone.

Donald spoke up.

“Let it go to voicemail. No one, and I truly mean no one, wants to speak to that man. I certainly don’t.”

Business as usual. It was time to address the crowd, fervent as ever. The roars, the cheers, it brought the President’s mood right back up. It was time to embellish himself in glory.

`); e.pages.push(`
>

BLOOMBERG CONCEDES - DJT SAYS ‘AMERICA IS BACK!’ AFTER STUNNING VICTORY

“I have just called President Donald Trump to offer him my congratulations on this campaign. We fought hard, and we fought the good fight, but our message did not reach through as anticipated, and ultimately, me and John have failed you. I’m sorry. Now is the time to come together, as a nation, like I did with John when we decided to put aside traditional party banners and fight this campaign. Whether you’re a Democrat, a Republican, an Independent or even a Rebirther, let us now wish the President the very best as he goes onto his second term.”

Donald turns off the TV.

“What a loser, all he does is lie. Never even answered his concession call. Listen, okay, we need to go out there and speak to the crowd. They’re eager to hear from their FAVOURITE PRESIDENT of all time. The Elites, the Establishment, they have no idea what they have witnessed, shell-shocked, as they say.”

Sanders nodded along. He found himself in a weird state of emotions. He was happy, even grateful, they won in such a major fashion, however he felt a sense of guilt. Guilt about working with Donald Trump. Progressives in his faction are still, at best, weary, and worst, out-right rebellious against his faction of the party. He knew then they had a long 4 years ahead, but he knew he was up for the challenge. Sanders knew this was once in a lifetime chance to bring about real change for Americans. He put aside his reservations, his doubts, and went with Donald to work for the American people.

They walked together towards the arena. The gravity of the situation finally began to set in. They could hear the crowd beneath the curtains.

“USA! USA! USA!”

“FEEL THE BERN!”

“MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN!”

The echoes of their past coming back to them. But it was not of a somber tone. It was beautiful. Donald walked out with Melania. Bernie with Jane followed straight after. Their arms waving to the people who supported them ever since their journeys began.

The crowd roared and cheered. They howled. They screamed. The impossible was indeed possible. Two of the biggest political figures, united against the Establishment, took them down. It was surreal.

Donald approached the podium.

“AMERICA IS BACK!”

`); e.pages.push(`

THE DEMOCRATS AND REPUBLICANS OFFICIALLY UNIFY UNDER A NEW BANNER.

After their abysmal performance in the 2022 Midterms against the incumbent Rebirth Party, amid a resurgence of Populism across the globe, both Democratic and Republican leadership sought, and got, unification of their parties through. This comes after Senate leadership from Schumer and McConnell announced the decision to jointly work together to block the Trump/Sanders agenda from passing in the Senate, effectively stonewalling the President’s foreign policy and the Vice President’s economic policy.

Meanwhile in the House, it was much more of a bitter struggle, with Kevin McCarthy and Nancy Pelosi constantly at one another's throats, it was quite difficult to get to any such arrangement. However, ever since the Jeffries coup against Pelosi herself shortly after the certification of new members, both of these men sought to get a deal through as quickly as possible to stonewall any crucial pieces of legislation through. This comes after the fact Rebirth managed to get 219 seats within the House after the 2022 Midterm elections, a new feat for a third party.

On Twitter, Donald Trump took to lambasting this newly formed opposition, stating:

“WEAK. WEAK. WEAK! These people FRANKLY HAVE NO IDEA WHAT THEY’RE DOING!! I thought CRYIN’ CHUCK and OLD CROW MCCONNELL were bad, BUT THIS IS SOMEHOW, EVEN WORSE? EVER SINCE PELOSI GOT KNIFED (SORRY, NANCY!) BACKSTABBIN’ JEFF HAS SOLD HIS SOUL. What’s next, SHIFTY KEVIN TRYING TO DERAIL ME? WHAT A TRAVESTY. MAKE AMERICA FAIR AGAIN!!!”

Bernie Sanders, on the other hand, was much more restrained in his opinion:

“Listen, I’m not going to comment on the antiques of the old parties, they’re just trying to reinvent themselves for an electorate that isn’t interested in them anymore. The President and I are looking forward to continuing our good progress for our agenda, and, uhm, for America. If you excuse me, I’ve got a plane to catch for the latest Climate Change Summit.”

With a Rebirth majority in the House and a “Republicrat” majority in the Senate, it will be increasingly difficult to find any compromise between the two parties…

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/tuff.jpg'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20215203.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20171331.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "I Just Want To Rule The World", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20171157.png", "cCDxKzeSD7s" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 309 && quickstats[0] < 340 && uniparty == 2) { // Large victory e.pages.push(`

REBIRTH CEMENTED AS NATIONAL PARTY - UNITY TICKET DESTROYED IN LANDSLIDE

Victory. Revenge. Trump always had those words confused. What he saw on Election Night was pure revenge to him. Bloomberg dared to defy him, his legacy, his status, and for that, he suffered his punishment. Humiliation. It was beautiful in his eyes. Bernie saw it differently. He had given up everything to be in this position now, and it had paid off. What he felt was more relief than anything else. No one blamed him, after all, he was working with someone he called a sexist, a bigot, an enemy of the state. That was 4 years ago. How the country has changed in just 4 short years was remarkable to both men.

“I never had any doubts, none whatsoever Bernard, this was too easy. Mike Bloomberg, what a loser, couldn’t even reach 200 votes! Y’know, I supported this guy for Mayor of NYC, my home, and now he thought to betray me? Now he knows.”

Donald’s face was having all sorts of emotion. One minute he would have that big grin encompassing the room, and the next minute, he would go into a frown. No one was speaking about his ‘enormous’ win, they, the so-called ‘fake news media’ were focused on how Bloomberg did so poorly. This was his night, his moment, his victory, and yet it was not about him. Bernie saw the look on his face, and n

“Well, uh, Mr President, it’s been a real journey, and a long one at that. We’ve toured the country, we’ve made promises, we need to act on them as soon as the celebrations are done with. We have a country to run, and we need to do whatever we can to free the American people from being downtrodden. We-”

A ring from the phone interrupted Sanders. Both of them knew who it was, and neither wanted to pick up the phone.

Donald spoke up.

“Let it go to voicemail. No one, and I truly mean no one, wants to speak to that man. I certainly don’t.”

Business as usual. It was time to address the crowd, fervent as ever. The roars, the cheers, it brought the President’s mood right back up. It was time to embellish himself in glory.

`); e.pages.push(`

BLOOMBERG CONCEDES - DJT SAYS ‘AMERICA IS BACK!’ AFTER STUNNING VICTORY

“I have just called President Donald Trump to offer him my congratulations on this campaign. We fought hard, and we fought the good fight, but our message did not reach through as anticipated, and ultimately, me and John have failed you. I’m sorry. Now is the time to come together, as a nation, like I did with John when we decided to put aside traditional party banners and fight this campaign. Whether you’re a Democrat, a Republican, an Independent or even a Rebirther, let us now wish the President the very best as he goes onto his second term.”

Donald turns off the TV.

“What a loser, all he does is lie. Never even answered his concession call. Listen, okay, we need to go out there and speak to the crowd. They’re eager to hear from their FAVOURITE PRESIDENT of all time. The Elites, the Establishment, they have no idea what they have witnessed, shell-shocked, as they say.”

Sanders nodded along. He found himself in a weird state of emotions. He was happy, even grateful, they won in such a major fashion, however he felt a sense of guilt. Guilt about working with Donald Trump. Progressives in his faction are still, at best, weary, and worst, out-right rebellious against his faction of the party. He knew then they had a long 4 years ahead, but he knew he was up for the challenge. Sanders knew this was once in a lifetime chance to bring about real change for Americans. He put aside his reservations, his doubts, and went with Donald to work for the American people.

They walked together towards the arena. The gravity of the situation finally began to set in. They could hear the crowd beneath the curtains.

“USA! USA! USA!”

“FEEL THE BERN!”

“MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN!”

The echoes of their past coming back to them. But it was not of a somber tone. It was beautiful. Donald walked out with Melania. Bernie with Jane followed straight after. Their arms waving to the people who supported them ever since their journeys began.

The crowd roared and cheered. They howled. They screamed. The impossible was indeed possible. Two of the biggest political figures, united against the Establishment, took them down. It was surreal.

Donald approached the podium.

“AMERICA IS BACK!”

`); e.pages.push(`

THE DEMOCRATS AND REPUBLICANS OFFICIALLY UNIFY UNDER A NEW BANNER.

After their abysmal performance in the 2022 Midterms against the incumbent Rebirth Party, amid a resurgence of Populism across the globe, both Democratic and Republican leadership sought, and got, unification of their parties through. This comes after Senate leadership from Schumer and McConnell announced the decision to jointly work together to block the Trump/Sanders agenda from passing in the Senate, effectively stonewalling the President’s foreign policy and the Vice President’s economic policy.

Meanwhile in the House, it was much more of a bitter struggle, with Kevin McCarthy and Nancy Pelosi constantly at one another's throats, it was quite difficult to get to any such arrangement. However, ever since the Jeffries coup against Pelosi herself shortly after the certification of new members, both of these men sought to get a deal through as quickly as possible to stonewall any crucial pieces of legislation through. This comes after the fact Rebirth managed to get 219 seats within the House after the 2022 Midterm elections, a new feat for a third party.

On Twitter, Donald Trump took to lambasting this newly formed opposition, stating:

“WEAK. WEAK. WEAK! These people FRANKLY HAVE NO IDEA WHAT THEY’RE DOING!! I thought CRYIN’ CHUCK and OLD CROW MCCONNELL were bad, BUT THIS IS SOMEHOW, EVEN WORSE? EVER SINCE PELOSI GOT KNIFED (SORRY, NANCY!) BACKSTABBIN’ JEFF HAS SOLD HIS SOUL. What’s next, SHIFTY KEVIN TRYING TO DERAIL ME? WHAT A TRAVESTY. MAKE AMERICA FAIR AGAIN!!!”

Bernie Sanders, on the other hand, was much more restrained in his opinion:

“Listen, I’m not going to comment on the antiques of the old parties, they’re just trying to reinvent themselves for an electorate that isn’t interested in them anymore. The President and I are looking forward to continuing our good progress for our agenda, and, uhm, for America. If you excuse me, I’ve got a plane to catch for the latest Climate Change Summit.”

With a Rebirth majority in the House and a “Republicrat” majority in the Senate, it will be increasingly difficult to find any compromise between the two parties…

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/tuff.jpg'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20215203.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20171331.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "I Just Want To Rule The World", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20171157.png", "cCDxKzeSD7s" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 289 && quickstats[0] < 310 && uniparty == 2) { // Large victory e.pages.push(`

MAJOR NATIONAL UPSET - TRUMP/SANDERS WIN CONVINCINGLY AGAINST BLOOMBERG/KASICH

Jubilance. It had finally come. The destruction of the two-party system. As soon as the Plains and the West Coast started to be called, it was already over for Mike and John. Trump, ever the egomaniac, already boasted about his ‘ENORMOUS’ win against the ‘CROOKS IN THE MEDIA’. Bernie was sat in the corner, quietly planning what he was going to say to the crowd outside. Both Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders felt one mutual emotion: Pride. They had worked together, hand in hand, to destroy a system which plagued American politics for centuries. They had ‘crossed the Rubicon’ and effectively planted their flag within D.C. They weren’t going anywhere.

Donald turned off the TV, and went to Bernie, who was still sat down, deep in thought.

“Bernie, these total idiots, they don’t know what the fuck they’re doing. You look in the Rust Belt, you look in the South, you look in the West, we won EVERYWHERE! Y’know, Mike was at one point, a friend of mine, but not anymore. He tried and failed miserably to try and replace me. What a shame.”

“Ye-yes, Donald, you’re right, we need to get back to work immediately, I don’t want, uh, the media, to go after us for celebrating too much. You know what they’re like…”

Then the phone rang. The call.

Donald sprang up from his seat to pick it up.

“I’ve been waiting for this. I can’t wait to hear that little shit come crying back to me, it’ll be rewarding.”

But it wasn’t Mike Bloomberg. It was Kasich.

“Hello, Mr President. Uh-”

Donald didn’t want a second-rate politician talking to him. He passed the phone to Bernie.

“Sorry Mr Kasich, it’s Bernard on the phone. I’ll gladly, uh, pass on any messages you might have for the President.”

“Oh. Right. Okay, well, congratulations on your win, Mr Vice President. I hope you lead this country into the success it deserves, and bring stability and strength back home for America. We should talk soon about the develo-”

Donald was getting impatient.

“Ask where Bloomberg is. I want to hear that bastard grovel at the knees.”

Bernie obliged, although he didn’t really appreciate how the President was acting. He thought the power had gotten to his head, but thankfully, he was there to keep him in check. They saw each other as friends, and most importantly, counsel.

“Mr Kasich, may I ask, where is Mr Bloomberg?”

“He’s, uhm, preparing his speech. He thought he ought not to call the President, considering their history.”

“Alright, alright, well thank you for the call. See you soon- okay, okay bye.”

Bernie wasn’t looking forward to telling Donald the truth.

`); e.pages.push(`

PRESIDENT TRUMP CALLS OUT DEFEATED BLOOMBERG - CALLS HIM ‘SAD MAN’ IN HIS VICTORY SPEECH

The crowd went wild. The working class of America rose up in defiance and had chosen their champions to lead them into prosperity. No longer was America controlled by foreign lobbyists, by the oligarchy, by the Wall Street class. A new era had dawned upon them all. Power to the people. Once deemed ‘the Masses’ by the elites, are now a force to be reckoned with. No longer were they numbers on an economic spreadsheet, they were alive. No longer were they statistics for marginal profits or economic growth, they had a mind of their own. Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders harnessed that power like no other force seen since Ross Perot. It was ironic. A Billionaire and A Revolutionary forced to work together for the country, now found each other as friends and leading a new movement.

Trump walked onto the stage, glistening with ego and joy. This was his element. Bernie walked side by side with him, and they both put their fists up in defiance of the status-quo. Chants of MAFA, MAGA, Feel The Bern, they could all be heard throughout the arena. It was extraordinary. 4 years ago, they did not see eye to eye on barely anything, but now, they had built a real movement. They had tapped into something that was destined for greatness. They had found their salvation.

Donald taps the mic to see if it works, and then…

“Sorry to keep you waiting, complicated business!”

The chuckles, the laughs, he felt at ease.

“I mean, look what happened, didn’t we win in a HUGE fashion? Those crooks, the media, they painted us out to be evil, that we’re a scourge to be destroyed. Desperation at it’s finest. These people, they have NO IDEA!”

The crowd erupts. Bernie claps along. Hawley, AOC, Kennedy, Ventura, all in the sidelines, cheer on his name.

“But, anyways, did you guys hear what Bloomberg was saying? Saying how he conceded so gracefully and being such a ‘mature’ guy. What a liar. HE AVOIDED THE CALL! He had to get Kasich to do it for him. What a wimp. Bloomberg is a SAD MAN! He avoids ALL RESPONSIBILITY. But this isn’t about him, it’s about us, our movement that WE have created. We’re just the figureheads, you, the PEOPLE, rose up and FOUGHT LIKE HELL against the liars, the thieves, the rot. THANK YOU for your AMAZING help! It will NEVER be forgotten.”

The fight has only just begun.

`); e.pages.push(`

PRITZKER/KHANNA DEFEAT KENNEDY/VENTURA - WHAT HAPPENS NOW?

November 8th, 2028.

Rebirth have officially lost their first election ever since their formation back in 2019, when firebrands Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders saw that they weren’t going to be nominated by their respective parties. Bernie Sanders, the 46th President of the United States, bowed out of the primaries, citing health concerns and wanting to fully focus on the Presidency rather than fight out a long drawn out election campaign. His Vice President, Robert F. Kennedy Jr, decided to take up the mantle and tapped Jesse Ventura, former Reform Governor of Minnesota and activist, to be his Vice Presidential Nominee.

The race has been a real nail-biter for both sides, however Pritzker and Khanna delivered a respectable 306 Electoral College Votes compared to Kennedy and Ventura had only 232 Electoral College Votes. Some of the states had produced abnormal results, such as Oregon, flipping to Rebirth once more, along with Colorado. On the opposite site, Indiana swung completely to the Pritzker ticket, along with the entire Rust Belt (Ohio, Michigan, Pennsylvania and Wisconsin).

This has also been the first election without a real ‘right-wing’ candidate, with the Kennedy leaning towards the soft-left Rebirth faction. This may explain why the Libertarians managed to get upwards of 6% of the Popular Vote.

More to come as we follow the transition period between Bernie Sanders and JB Pritzker's team.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/tuff.jpg'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20171000.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20170920.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Avril", "Neil Cicierega", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Mouth%20Moods%20Outakes.jpeg", "z3lElikZD2Y" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 269 && quickstats[0] < 290 && uniparty == 2) { // Very close victory e.pages.push(`

NAIL BITER ELECTION - ELECTORAL COLLEGE HANDS THE KEYS TO REBIRTH AS UNITY TICKET FALLS SHORT.

Luck. That’s how it can be described. Through sheer luck, both Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders prevailed against the machine that was the Establishment. The counting, it dragged on, and on, and on. It didn’t feel like there would be an end in sight. But sure enough, the final state was called for them. Cheers from staffers filled the room, finally, a break from all the silence and torment that haunted them.

Donald was sat down with a glass of water within his hand. There were only a few moments where he felt like he needed to have alcohol. These were one of the times, but he resisted. Bernie sat beside him, looking at the TV screen.

“And now we can project that Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders have won re-election, and will return to the White House in an unprecedented election night. The Democrats and Republicans unified on a ticket failed to defeat the Populist incumbents in office. Stay tuned as we are to soon hear from-”

They couldn’t listen to it anymore. It was finally over. A breather from all the political fanfare. Bernie put his head in his hands, rubbing his eyes.

“Thank God that’s all over with. I, y’know, I’m exhausted. This political battle, it’s gone on for a few days now, and I’ve barely caught a wink of sleep. I don’t know how you do it Donald.

He begins to stand up.

“We should do the speech tomorrow, we both need lots of rest. We’ve got lots to do once we head back to the Capitol. It’d be smart.”

Donald looked up.

“Yeah, you’re right, probably. Our people, they also need a breather, right? Tremendous people, it’s because of them we won. I won’t forget that. I’m just glad it’s not another fucking deadlock. I’d rather have this drag on for a few more days than whatever shithole situation that was 4 years ago, I’m sure you’d agree.”

Bernie chuckled, patting Donald on the back.

“Just be grateful you don’t have to deal with Kasich!”

The doors shut, and with it, an era of politics cemented in the American political system.

`); e.pages.push(`

BLOOMBERG DEMANDS RECOUNTS ACROSS AMERICA - SAYS ‘FIGHT ISN'T OVER

November 12th, 2020.

A cold morning in New York. Bloomberg, sat on his empty throne, descends to the masses in Manhattan. He says the following:

“My friends, the fight isn’t over. I have reason to believe that the Russians interfered in this election, just as they did 4 years ago. I am demanding that the states do an immediate recount of ALL the votes. Hillary was robbed 4 years ago through the House Delegations and bribery. I won’t be robbed now. I think this election was fraudulent.”

The public, they couldn’t believe their ears. What did he say? The election was…fradulent? They knew Trump could be capable of something like this, but Bloomberg? The shriveled billionaire? The shriek establishmentarian? It didn’t seem real.

The screaming. The booing. It was almost immediate. Clearly he didn’t pay these people enough to swallow *that* pill. He stood there, stuttering.

“I- uh, listen, folks, I can- I can explain. Donald Trump, his, uh, cronies. They’re behind all this- just look at what he says! He’s a CRIMINAL. People say- no, fuck it, I KNOW he is. I’ve known- I-”

He was being drowned out by his own crowd. He’ll now be in the halls of infamy. John Kasich was nowhere to be seen. He wanted out as soon as possible. Kasich was always part of the Establishment, but at the very least, he wasn’t stupid. He stayed put in Ohio, there was no way in hell he was going to stand by Bloomberg. Why would he? He tried, and failed, to win. That was his political career over. There was no need to fight anymore.

Both Trump and Sanders were in the Oval office when they had heard Bloomberg say these remarks. You could hear the laughter from miles away.

“First Hillary, and now Bloomberg! Y’know, out of all the people who can actually claim it was rigged against them, it would be you, Bernard! These people- crooks, I call em’, they tried to steal your thunder. Your nomination of the Democratic Party. And after it was all said and done, they gave you the pity prize of the Vice Presidency. What a big mistake for them that was!”

The fight for the White House was only just beginning.

`); e.pages.push(`

REPORTS OF ATTACKS ON THE MEXICAN BORDER - CARTEL INTERFERENCE IN TEXAS?

There have been numerous reports of Mexican Cartels crossing the border, armed with conventional weapons, attacking Immigration & Customs Enforcement (ICE) and local police patrols. The most recent reports that we have so far are in Laredo, where many Americans are under siege by the Los Zestas Cartel, who have gotten so far as to almost capture the international airport within the city.

This also comes as the Juárez Cartel has attacked El Paso, with reports coming in that over 50 are dead at the border. With most of the police force occupied and the army on it’s way, local residents have taken up arms against the invading forces and are using their own guns to fight back against the Cartel insurgents.

The President has authorised the use of any force necessary against the recent Cartel invasion, stating on Twitter:

“This attack on the United States will be met with FIRE AND FURY LIKE THE WORLD HAS NEVER SEEN. MEXICO BETTER START ANSWERING OUR QUESTIONS, OTHERWISE HELLFIRE WILL BE BROUGHT TO THESE THUGS, CRIMINALS AND RAPISTS. I will authorise ANY FORCE NECESSARY TO DISPELL THESE INVADERS. NO ONE ATTACKS AMERICA WITHOUT STRONG REPERCUSSIONS. OPERATION AMERICAN EAGLE IS A GO!!!”

Bernie Sanders has also taken to a personal statement with the press, stating to us:

“This attack on our nation is a gross violation of international law. We’re calling on all nations to disavow and help us in this heinous violent provocation. The President is right in using the military to help dissent against the Cartel. They are an unspeakable evil, and I stand behind President Trump one-hundred percent.”

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20215203.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20171011.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20151952.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Sweeter Dreams", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20183108.png", "ijSmOtBiyeY" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] == 269 && uniparty == 2) { // Deadlocked election e.pages.push(`

NATION FACES GRIDLOCK ONCE MORE AS ANOTHER ELECTORAL TIE HAPPENS - WHAT’S NEXT?

Terror. How? Why? It was denial at first. Then anger. They had worked so hard to make it where they were now, and now the electorate decided to gift them another deadlock they were sure to lose. It doesn’t matter whether or not they won the Popular Vote, it didn’t matter whether or not they gained a few House or Senate seats. It was all for nought. They was no way in hell they could both progress towards a victory in January. They had already resigned in their minds already.

Staffers were mute. The President was mute. Stone cold. The Vice President? He looked up at the screen, then to Donald Trump.

“Listen, Donald, we gave it our best shot. We came up short. I think we should get some rest. It’s been a long few days. Maybe the concession spee-”

He heard ‘concession’. He couldn’t do it. He was too prideful, too proud of himself to ever give in to that thought. No. He’d fight on. Till the bitter end.

“Concession? What concession? You really think I’m going to concede to that scum that is Bloomberg? No- we’re fighting this Bernard, or I’ll do it myself, I don’t care. My dignity, my honour is on the line here. I won’t surrender to some corporate schmuck who only made it because of ME! Without me, he’d be NOTHING.”

Silence. You could hear a pin drop. Sanders, wide eyed, retorted.

“You’ll have to do it yourself, then. We have no chance in the House or the Senate. The math isn’t there. If you want to fight it, go ahead. But I’ve already made my choice. I won’t fight a battle which we are destined to lose.”

Then the phone rang. It was Bloomberg. Donald’s face turned red. Part of it was embarrassment for failing, but it was mostly a sense of anger, frustration and a deep resentment towards a man who bought his way in politics. Before Donald could pick up the phone, Bernie reached for it and answered.

“Hello?”

You could hear that shit-eating grin from Bloomberg. Snivelling bastard. Who needs charisma when you have money?

“Hello Mr Sanders.”

Couldn’t even address the Vice President properly. Trump always had the thought he was the scum of the Earth. He was proven right.

“Well, I just wanted to call to say that it was a hard-fought campaign, and I think we both realise the situation. You came up a bit short, and so did we, to some extent. Listen, we’ll have to talk later, the crowd needs me.”

Then the phone hung up.

Bernie was back in the ring. One. Last. Fight.

`); e.pages.push(`

NATION BRACES ITSELF FOR THE POLITICAL FIGHT OF THE CENTURY

After a tense few days of silence from the Trump/Sanders team, they have decided to come out with a statement.

“We’re in this to win this. Don’t count us out.”

The usual bombastic President has been increasingly reserved over these past days, as speculation arises that he is trying to court GOP members into voting for him in the House Delegations. While nothing official has been confirmed yet, it is to be believed that many GOP House members are increasingly hesitant to vote for Bloomberg, given his past when it comes to New York and the way that he has presented himself over the election cycle.

The same can be said for the Senate. Bernie Sanders is apparently talking with many Progressive Senators, including Elizabeth Warren, to vote for him when it begins. Reports suggest that these talks have so far been extensive, and close to a deal. This would greatly help any chance that Rebirth can still get a seat at the table once it is all said and done.

Both Bloomberg and Kasich remain at ease, according to multiple sources. They believe that they are ultimately the best alternative to another 4 years of chaos under Trump/Sanders, and that many GOP members will come to their ‘senses’ once they realise Trump will apparently ‘dupe’ them like he did in 2016.

“Listen, these rumours are unfounded.”

Kasich was firm in his tone.

“The GOP stand behind Bloomberg, and they’re ready to put him in charge and as our Commander-in-Chief. He’s a solid guy, and someone who will lead a steady hand. I trust that my friends in the House won’t fall for Trump’s trickery. We learnt in 2016 not to trust that charlatan, and we can’t afford to make the same mistakes we did 4 years ago. I stood against him then, and I continue to do so now.”

Bloomberg has yet to comment. However he is said to be calm and reserved about the whole process. There is speculation among many of the public that he is using his money to buy the loyalty of many members in the House to ensure he is chosen to be President, but again, there is no evidence for this.

More to come in the coming weeks.

`); e.pages.push(`

PRESIDENT BLOOMBERG ADDRESSES NATION - VP SANDERS MISSING AFTER CAPITOL BOMBING

“My fellow Americans. We have just been attacked by a unknown force which has, in no uncertain terms, meant to harm, disrupt and destroy our nation. I stay vigilant against this terrorist threat, and I swear by Almighty God that they will be punished for their actions.”

The rage, it was palpable. You could see the twitch in one of his eyes. The anger. The pain. The fire. It was there. The fury of an American. At this very moment, he embodied the American Spirit. It didn’t matter about Republican, Democrat or Rebirth. What mattered was the sanctity of the nation was not in peril.

“We will find these terrorists, we will hunt them down, and execute them. There is no place for mercy when our Capitol is attacked. The American Spirit is unconquerable. Al Qaeda tried, ISIS tried, the Axis tried. They all failed. And these terrorists will fail as well. I make that absolutely clear.”

But still, there was panic. Who did this? Why? How? And where was the Vice President? What happened? Rumours spread like wild-fire, people said he had died in the attack, while others suggested he was hiding within the Capitol bunker. No one knew. Not even the President. While he did not intend for him to become Vice President, he still ultimately cared for his safety.

“I have yet to find the whereabouts of our Vice President, Bernie Sanders. He is a good man, and we’re doing our very best to try and find him. I hope he is alive. I pray to God that he is alive somewhere within the Capitol, and that he can return to safety as soon as possible. If, God forbid, he is to pass, we will avenge him. No one targets our leaders and get away with it.”

NATO stands on high alert. Russia denies any responsibility. China remains mute on the whole situation. Iran condemns it, trying to avoid any outright invasion. Israel says it stands behind America 100%. The international order, which has stood for 80 years, has been shook to it’s fundamental core. There is increasing concern this could spark a war which hasn’t been seen since…The Second Great War.

DEFCON 2 INITIATED. PREPARE.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20204830.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20203344.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20171000.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Night Of Fire", "Niko", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20193642.png", "SRbhLtjOiRc" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] >= 259 && quickstats[0] < 269 && uniparty == 2) { // Close loss e.pages.push(`

"TRUMP CLAIMS FOUL PLAY - BATTLE RAGES ON FOR WHITE HOUSE"

Shock. The moods of everyone in the room immediately changed. Once hopeful of an upset, dashed away by the latest calls out of the West Coast. Anger began to swell up within certain people. Others felt guilt, that they didn’t do enough. But the one thing they had in common? Anguish. They fought for what they believed was right, and they failed. It felt, and was, a tragedy. Trump sat there, looking at the TV screen. He wasn’t very happy with the results. Bernie sat next to him, looking solemn. They had failed in their mission to make America a fairer, more just nation. Bernie felt guilt more than anything, not for himself, but for the people he failed. He truly cared for the down-trodden of America, for the working classes of America. Those were his people, and he wanted to make things right.

But Donald? Sure, there was that feeling. But what he felt was jealously. Bitterness. In no world would he accept that Bloomberg ever beat him. It just couldn’t happen. In his eyes, Bloomberg was a sleaze bag, he never put the effort, or the attention, or really anything he wanted to do. He just expected it. Donald finally understood the one lesson in life: Life is unfair. Always has been, always will be.

Donald looked to his side, seeing Bernie with his hands covering his face. Embarrassment.

“This can’t be real, surely not, no? Bernie, am I having a dream? Heh, surely? Bloomberg can’t win, he’s mentally deficient. Just look at the guy- we won, big league, didn’t we?”

Donald was worried. There was no way, right? Victory was within their grasp, and yet somehow, they lost it? Bernie replied:

“Mr President…we didn’t win. This isn’t a dream. It’s reality. Bloomberg has won. He’s expecting your call any moment.”

He couldn’t believe his ears. No way. This wasn’t real.

“Bernie, this isn’t over, listen- look, okay, we can just call up the states- we need to find the votes. It’ll be so easy. Trust me Bernard, I know people, and people know me. These people owe me their political careers. I made them. It was rigg-”

Bernie stopped him. He couldn’t, in good conscience, allow this to pass. It would go against his entire principles.

“Donald- please, as your friend, you need to stop this. We lost. Accept it, remember, there’s always 2024. We still have a chance to-”

“Shut the fuck up. We won this, okay?”

And with that, Rebirth was split. ‘Normalcy’ won, while the infighting of the Populists had only just begun…

`); e.pages.push(`

"PUBLIC FALLOUT OF REBIRTH - TRUMP DOES NOT CONCEDE WHILE SANDERS DOES. BITTER BREAKUP?"

After what was a few tense nights in both camps following the counting of the votes - a winner was chosen by the American electorate. Mike Bloomberg and John Kasich are elected as President and Vice President of the United States. This comes after the latest calls from the West Coast, with many of them tipping it just over the edge to secure them a victory, but a narrow one at that.

However, in an address that has been lambasted throughout America, President Donald Trump has said that the election results are ‘not what they appear’ and declares:

“I won this election, BY A LOT! They tell me, the lawyers, I mean, they say ‘Mr President, there is a way forward. I’m trusting these people, very smart, academic scholars in law. We won this election, perhaps, in a LANDSLIDE. It’s a shame how Bernie refuses to join me. He knows it pains me just as much as it does him. Truly, it’s a sad day. But if he doesn’t want to join us, that’s on him, not me!”

He has repeated unfounded rumours that ballots were stuffed with mail-in votes, and that the Bloomberg campaign smuggled in thousands of votes to swing states to switch them from leaning Rebirth to going back to their ticket.

While Trump/Sanders were leading in these states by tens of thousands (in some cases, hundreds of thousands), they were yet to account for the mail-in ballots, which while narrower than Election-Day voters, still had a heavy majority towards Bloomberg/Kasich. This has led Trump to believe that ‘fraudulent activity’ was taking place in many of these ballot boxes.

Meanwhile, the Vice President, Bernie Sanders, has conceded to Bloomberg/Kasich and pledges full co-operation within the transition team. In a statement following Trump’s comments about election fraud, he told reporters:

“Listen, I know they’re unfounded, and I think he does too. It’s, well, it’s sad to see. I called him a friend once. Now he’s going down a path that I cannot, in good conscience, follow. I worry deeply for the state of our country and indeed our democracy, but this isn’t the way to go about it.”

As the legal challenges mount up, more news will follow shortly.

`); e.pages.push(`

"DONALD TRUMP WINS AND BECOMES THE 47TH PRESIDENT OF THE UNITED STATES UNDER NEW PATRIOT PARTY - NATION SPLIT 4 WAYS."

No one could see it coming. Not even Trump himself could believe it. Victory. Newsom was nowhere to be found. And Sanders? He was secretly glad. He knew Trump, he worked with him for 4 years. He knew what was to come. President Kasich was despondent. After Bloomberg’s resignation over tapping Rebirth’s internal communications, his approvals ever since hovered at around the 35% mark. It didn’t help the ‘America First’ wing of the GOP were outright rebellious over immigration amnesty. That prompted Trump to make his new party, the “Patriot Front”, which stood on mass deportations, putting an end to the ‘foreign wars’ and putting ‘America First’.

But this is not to say the Democrats had their own issues. With Bloomberg's resignation and underwater approvals for working with the GOP, many of the Progressives still in the Democratic Party decided to form their own party. The “Justice & Freedom Party” was born, with Senator Elizabeth Warren and former Vice President Bernie Sanders leading this insurgent ‘revolution’ against the ‘tired, weak parties’. This encouraged many Representatives to flip between all the 4 parties, most notably ‘The Squad’, consisting of AOC, Ilhan Omar, Rashida Tlaib and Ayanna Pressley. On the opposite end of the political spectrum, Marjorie Taylor Greene, Matt Gaetz and Lauren Boebert joined the Patriot Front, hoping to ride the coattails of the resurgent Trump.

Here are the results:

Patriot Front - Donald Trump/Tucker Carlson, 270 EV Justice & Freedom - Bernie Sanders/Elizabeth Warren, 126 EV Democratic Party - Gavin Newsom/Chris Van Hollen, 84 EV Republican Party - John Kasich/Don Bacon, 58 EV

Truly an astonishing result considering the mess that Trump/Sanders left behind just 4 short years ago, with continuous bickering, arguing and division. This election was no different, and the nation has not felt this divide ever since the Civil War, almost 200 years ago.

America was entering yet another point of divergence. The ‘Soul of America’ was beginning to warp and distort, with each political figure wanting a piece of the pie.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20203813.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20202533.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20171655.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Capitol Hill", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20193822.png", "amX6zfQGGtk" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] >= 229 && quickstats[0] < 259 && uniparty == 2) { // Very close defeat e.pages.push(`

“This can’t be real, surely not, no? Bernie, am I having a dream? Heh, surely? Bloomberg can’t win, he’s mentally deficient. Just look at the guy- we won, big league, didn’t we?”

Donald was worried. There was no way, right? Victory was within their grasp, and yet somehow, they lost it? Bernie replied:

“Mr President…we didn’t win. This isn’t a dream. It’s reality. Bloomberg has won. He’s expecting your call any moment.”

He couldn’t believe his ears. No way. This wasn’t real.

“Bernie, this isn’t over, listen- look, okay, we can just call up the states- we need to find the votes. It’ll be so easy. Trust me Bernard, I know people, and people know me. These people owe me their political careers. I made them. It was rigg-”

Bernie stopped him. He couldn’t, in good conscience, allow this to pass. It would go against his entire principles.

“Donald- please, as your friend, you need to stop this. We lost. Accept it, remember, there’s always 2024. We still have a chance to-”

“Shut the fuck up. We won this, okay?”

"DONALD TRUMP WINS AND BECOMES THE 47TH PRESIDENT OF THE UNITED STATES UNDER NEW PATRIOT PARTY - NATION SPLIT 4 WAYS."

No one could see it coming. Not even Trump himself could believe it. Victory. Newsom was nowhere to be found. And Sanders? He was secretly glad. He knew Trump, he worked with him for 4 years. He knew what was to come. President Kasich was despondent. After Bloomberg’s resignation over tapping Rebirth’s internal communications, his approvals ever since hovered at around the 35% mark. It didn’t help the ‘America First’ wing of the GOP were outright rebellious over immigration amnesty. That prompted Trump to make his new party, the “Patriot Front”, which stood on mass deportations, putting an end to the ‘foreign wars’ and putting ‘America First’.

But this is not to say the Democrats had their own issues. With Bloomberg's resignation and underwater approvals for working with the GOP, many of the Progressives still in the Democratic Party decided to form their own party. The “Justice & Freedom Party” was born, with Senator Elizabeth Warren and former Vice President Bernie Sanders leading this insurgent ‘revolution’ against the ‘tired, weak parties’. This encouraged many Representatives to flip between all the 4 parties, most notably ‘The Squad’, consisting of AOC, Ilhan Omar, Rashida Tlaib and Ayanna Pressley. On the opposite end of the political spectrum, Marjorie Taylor Greene, Matt Gaetz and Lauren Boebert joined the Patriot Front, hoping to ride the coattails of the resurgent Trump.

Truly an astonishing result considering the mess that Trump/Sanders left behind just 4 short years ago, with continuous bickering, arguing and division. This election was no different, and the nation has not felt this divide ever since the Civil War, almost 200 years ago.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20203813.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20202533.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20171655.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Capitol Hill", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20193822.png", "amX6zfQGGtk" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] >= 191 && quickstats[0] < 229 && uniparty == 2) { // Realistic loss e.pages.push(`

AMERICA DECIDES AGAINST POPULISM - TRUMP/SANDERS EVICTED FROM THE WHITE HOUSE.

The projections in the late hours of the night weren’t looking good. Bernie had already accepted defeat at around 10pm. There was no path to victory. Trump clung onto hope, hope that maybe, the patriots, in their darkest hour, would turn out and sweep the country. Alas, it wasn’t meant to be. Staffers went quiet, eerily quiet. The room, once bright with hope and vigour, was now asunder. The drinks stop flowing, and with it, conversation. People had no idea what to make of it.

“And we can now project that Mike Bloomberg will become the 46th President of the United State-”

The TV shut off. Donald couldn’t bear to listen to it any longer. He hadn’t felt like this ever since his brother had died. He was mourning. There wasn’t even any anger. He knew when the gig was up, when it was time to pack away his things and move forward with something else.

Bernie, seeing what state the President was in, stood next to him, brought him up next to him, and gave him a pat on the back.

“We tried Donald, we really did, but it’s the end of the road now. We have to concede. It’s the only right thing to do. Maybe the country just isn’t ready for this sort of collaboration.”

Donald was despondent. His friend, Bloomberg, steals his job, his thunder. Wasn’t being another billionaire in Manhattan enough? Wasn’t being Mayor of his home, New York City, enough? Donald adored that place, and he supported Mike through thick and thin. Now, he was abandoned by that very ‘friend’ who sought to remove him. It was almost…pitiful.

Donald choked up a response. He was unusually quiet.

“Yeah, yeah, I know. I’ll, uh, draft a statement…”

`); e.pages.push(`
TRUMP CONCEDES RACE - BLOOMBERG SAYS ‘NORMALCY IS BACK IN AMERICA’

“My friends, patriots, the race is over. We lost, and I am sorry that I did not deliver that strong, decisive mandate that we had all worked very hard for. Y’know, the fight in the future isn’t over, and while we may have lost this political battle, the dream for a greater America IS. NOT. OVER! In just 4 years, we fought against the establishment, the status-quo, the Washington machine, and we’re here to stay. Rebirth is a national phenomenon, and we will continue day and night to ensure every American gets a fair, just and good chance at LIFE.”

The crowd cheered, albeit not as enthusiastically if they had won. They knew they had fought the good fight, the honourable fight, but they had come up short. They were used to that- coming up short, that is. Everyone in that room knew one thing:

Life is unfair. Life is cruel. But life can be so beautiful, so unique, if you only put to your mind to it. Just one simple choice can change an entire person’s life. But with politics, it’s just not an entire person’s life, it’s an entire nation’s future. That’s what Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders had accomplished. They managed to reshape the entire American political system, and with it, gave a voice to those who previously were unable to speak at the table. They gave representation to the people who woke up, did their hard work, and went back home to provide for their families. It wasn’t about them, it was about the people. That’s what they both campaigned on in 2016, and they continued that message until now. It may not break through now, or maybe for a long time, but it was cemented into the minds of millions of Americans.

Bloomberg, on the other hand, was gloating. That’s his hobby, other than making money, of course.

“My fellow Americans, WE HAVE WON! America has chosen normalcy over chaos. America has chosen steady leadership over infighting. America has chosen FREEDOM over DIVISION. Our spirit remains UNBREAKABLE. We have rescued the Soul of America from those who wish to exploit it for political gain. We’re the real fighters in this race, and we have prevailed. Now is not the time to insult or divide, but to heal and grow as a nation. Many Independents, Democrats and Republicans voted for Trump and Sanders, but know, we are always for ALL Americans, not just those that voted for us. We’re going to work day and night for you, and ensure that we can provide the best leadership possible. Thank you, God bless you, and goodnight.”

`); e.pages.push(`
PRESIDENT-ELECT SANDERS ANNOUNCES FORMER PRESIDENT TRUMP AS SECRETARY OF TREASURY - REBIRTH LEADERS REUNITED

After suffering a heart attack in 2023, Donald Trump officially bowed out of the Rebirth Party primaries - opting to endorse his former Vice President, Bernie Sanders, to run for office instead. This led to a 48 state sweep for the previous VP (only missing out on Iowa and New Hampshire, both won by Trump previously). With this elected mandate from the party, he chose Rebirth Senator from Ohio, James David Vance, to be his Vice Presidential Nominee. This was to ensure that the so-called ‘Trump wing’ of the Rebirth Party were satisfied and were fully prepared to throw their full weight behind a self-described “Democratic Socialist”.

This was also coming in at a time of great disunity among the Democratic and Republican leadership, as with heavy losses during the 2022 Midterms, they had resorted to blaming each other rather than focusing on the day to day governance of America. With rife disloyalty among their ranks, President Michael Bloomberg and Vice President John Kasich sought to bring themselves together again to face Sanders/Vance.

This was not an easy feat however, with them struggling initially within their own primaries, especially in the Democratic Party, as President Bloomberg had abysmal showings in Iowa and Nevada, crucial swing states needed for the General Election in November. This would be exacerbated with the increasingly vocal Progressive wing of the DNC, opting to almost rebel against party leadership and defect to Rebirth, seeing as Bloomberg as nothing more than a “Corporatist billionaire who has no ties to the real world.” (Khanna, on the eve of the DNC in Boston, 2024).

With the war in Taiwan raging for it’s second year in a row, along with an Iranian invasion of Iraq, the DemReps were pushed into a corner, and ultimately, lost decisively to the Sanders/Vance Rebirth ticket, with them picking up 448 Electoral Votes compared to President Bloomberg's 90. Even safe states that were once solidly in the President’s hands, slipped away, such as New York, Illinois and much of New England.

Sanders' impressive win would allow him to go truly bold in his approach on how to administer America. One of the first things that he announced would be that Donald Trump would be making a return to the Capitol, being selected as Secretary to the Treasury. In a statement, President Sanders said:

“Listen, Donald is a well respected businessman and can get the economy moving again. He’s feeling up for it, he’s healthy, and he’s ready to fight for America and her interests. I’m glad to have him in my cabinet. As he would say, he’s a tremendous figure in American politics!”

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20204830.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20172417.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20152035.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "The End", "Neil Cicierega", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20205628.png", "3cXjcKTRWcg" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 134 && quickstats[0] < 191 && uniparty == 2) { // Landslide loss e.pages.push(`

TRUMP AND SANDERS LOST - THE SOUL OF THE NATION RESTORED

Disappointment. How could this be? Donald was completely distraught. So early on in the night? He didn’t even feel anger over this loss, just pure regret and frustration. How could he be angry? The President got rejected soundly. Bernie was taking it much better. He knew what this sort of feeling was. He felt it during 2016, and during these past 4 years. Ever bitter struggle for success, every fight for justice, all for naught. They had both tried and tried for change, and yet a system hellbent on the status-quo refused to budge. It wasn’t their fault. It was the system’s, and as Donald would say, ‘THE SYSTEM IS RIGGED!’

The President ordered everyone out bar the Vice President. He needed to speak to him, alone. This was going to be a difficult conversation. They had to face reality- that their vision, their hope for a brighter, more fair America, was beginning to wind down. They gave it their all, hell, Trump made himself bankrupt to fund this election for Rebirth, and they had still come up short. It was nothing more than a humiliation ritual, or at least that is what Donald thought.

“We fought Bernie, we really did, and I thought- well, I thought we’d have more of an impact. I suppose not. Listen, I don’t even think we’ll have a chance beyond this election, our party, our future, it’s down the drain. Y’know, Bloomberg, he was a nice guy, but he turned real nasty during this cycle. I don’t know why, you try and play nice with some of these people, and I mean, you see what they say about you, or me, or our people. The things that they say, it’s just disgraceful, fraudulent even. I have every right to challeng-”

Bernie interrupted him. He wouldn’t allow Donald to be corrupted enough to think to contest these elections. It was over from the start. They both saw it, and yet he still wanted to deny it. Bernie saw it for what it was: a coping mechanism. He knew what he felt at that very moment, he saw it when Clinton disregarded him all those years ago. He knew how to solve it.

“Donald. You have to face reality. We lost. They won. It’s as simple as that. I hate it just as much as you do, and I wish people would see those oligarchic figures for who they are, but they put their trust in them. We have to respect that, and move forward. It won’t be easy, and I know how you think. I was dealt the same hand, and look what happened. We fought the biggest Establishment, and we gave it our all. We can’t deny we put up a good fight. Hell, they had to unite to beat us! Think of it like that.”

The race was over. Donald had accepted defeat. It was time to finally realise the truth.
“I’ll give Bloomberg a call. I couldn’t believe he’d run against me to begin with, y’know, we were good friends, although I don’t think he’d appreciate that now, but still, we got along. We were both businessmen, and he still went after me. A tragedy, truly it is.”

`); e.pages.push(`
MOMENTUS VICTORY FOR UNPRECEDENTED UNITY TICKET - POPULISM NO LONGER POPULAR?

It was a harrowing night for the Rebirth Party, formed just a few years ago by Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders. In their first ever Presidential Election, they had lost significantly to the recently unified Democratic and Republican parties, who had nominated Mike Bloomberg, former Mayor of NYC and John Kasich, former Governor of Ohio.

While both President Trump and Vice President Sanders lost in a landslide to the Dem-Rep ticket, their congressional outlooks are a bright spot in an increasingly bleak moment for their upstart party. On top of their 52 seats they defended, they had also picked up an extra 30, mostly within the Midwest and the Plains region, meanwhile within the Senate, they had managed to pick up an extra 6 seats, from Montana all the way through to Oklahoma.

Now as the transition period begins, cracks are already beginning to form amongst the new union, with Progressive Democrats and Christian Right Republicans already incredibly frustrated with how much ‘compromise’ has taken place, and feel that they can not co-exist within the same deal altogether. This has culminated in spats between congressmen, especially between Mike Johnson of Louisiana and Ro Khanna of California, which has already led to factions forming between the disgruntled parties.

What’s next for President Elect Bloomberg is difficult to tell, with him being quiet on any potential nominees for his cabinet, as to hopefully prevent any early rebellions against his leadership. VP Elect Kasich has already signalled that it will be filled with both Democrats and Republicans from all stripes of field, which could make for one of the most interesting cabinets in US history, bar from the previous Trump/Sanders administration, which openly admitted that they had come to an arrangement relatively early on within their premiership.

More will come as time develops.

`); e.pages.push(`
How President Barron Trump broke the 3-Party System.

Barron Trump, running as an independent in the 2048 Presidential Election against President Mamdani (eligible to run after the 29th Amendment), former Republican Governor of New York Lee Zeldin and Rebirth Senator for Ohio Nina Turner, successfully beat all his opponents and won the necessary 275 Electoral College Votes to become President-Elect of the United States. How did this happen?

With President Mamdani’s mishandling of the Israeli-Egyptian war and the soaring gas prices straight after, he was in an incredibly difficult position. Furthermore, with his progressive base in open revolt over the actions of his administration towards Israel, they had decided to back the insurgent campaign of Nina Turner, who had rejuvenated the Rebirth Party after what many pundits described as a ‘managed decline’ for the party, as with it’s two juggernauts, former President Trump and former VP Sanders dying in 2032 (two weeks apart!), they had saw a small, yet gradual decline. Paired with a Republican Party rebounding after their recent midterm successes in 2046, they had felt emboldened to pick a conservative firebrand, former Governor of New York Lee Zeldin, to lead the Republican ticket as what they saw as a ‘communistic takeover of America’ from both Mamdani and Turner.

All this culminated in Barron Trump’s Independent run for the Presidency. In his statement just 2 years ago, he said:

“Our country is going down a path of economic suicide and a lack of any real patriotism. I have a new vision for America. Many of you here will remember my father’s Presidency. He was a good man, and someone I looked up to like an idol, but he was not a good President. He lost against a tired establishment, and failed to bring any meaningful change. I’m going to rectify that, here, today, by announcing my run for the Presidency of the United States. We need a new deal for ordinary Americans who are stamped on day in, day out. I’m not running under any labels, I’m my own man, just like my father. I’m going to make sure Americans get a fair shot in life, and that we, as a nation, can remake the American dream.”

This noble, ‘patriotic’ approach resonated with much of the American public, specifically the working-classes of America, whose keys issues were that of fairness, immigration and the cost of living.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20204830.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-27%20144223.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20172553.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "What A Loser", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-27%20150131.png", "EqFQ61lX528" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 2 && quickstats[0] <= 134 && uniparty == 2) { // Ridiculous loss e.pages.push(`

TRUMP AND SANDERS LOST - THE SOUL OF THE NATION RESTORED

Disappointment. How could this be? Donald was completely distraught. So early on in the night? He didn’t even feel anger over this loss, just pure regret and frustration. How could he be angry? The President got rejected soundly. Bernie was taking it much better. He knew what this sort of feeling was. He felt it during 2016, and during these past 4 years. Ever bitter struggle for success, every fight for justice, all for naught. They had both tried and tried for change, and yet a system hellbent on the status-quo refused to budge. It wasn’t their fault. It was the system’s, and as Donald would say, ‘THE SYSTEM IS RIGGED!’

The President ordered everyone out bar the Vice President. He needed to speak to him, alone. This was going to be a difficult conversation. They had to face reality- that their vision, their hope for a brighter, more fair America, was beginning to wind down. They gave it their all, hell, Trump made himself bankrupt to fund this election for Rebirth, and they had still come up short. It was nothing more than a humiliation ritual, or at least that is what Donald thought.

“We fought Bernie, we really did, and I thought- well, I thought we’d have more of an impact. I suppose not. Listen, I don’t even think we’ll have a chance beyond this election, our party, our future, it’s down the drain. Y’know, Bloomberg, he was a nice guy, but he turned real nasty during this cycle. I don’t know why, you try and play nice with some of these people, and I mean, you see what they say about you, or me, or our people. The things that they say, it’s just disgraceful, fraudulent even. I have every right to challeng-”

Bernie interrupted him. He wouldn’t allow Donald to be corrupted enough to think to contest these elections. It was over from the start. They both saw it, and yet he still wanted to deny it. Bernie saw it for what it was: a coping mechanism. He knew what he felt at that very moment, he saw it when Clinton disregarded him all those years ago. He knew how to solve it.

“Donald. You have to face reality. We lost. They won. It’s as simple as that. I hate it just as much as you do, and I wish people would see those oligarchic figures for who they are, but they put their trust in them. We have to respect that, and move forward. It won’t be easy, and I know how you think. I was dealt the same hand, and look what happened. We fought the biggest Establishment, and we gave it our all. We can’t deny we put up a good fight. Hell, they had to unite to beat us! Think of it like that.”

The race was over. Donald had accepted defeat. It was time to finally realise the truth.
“I’ll give Bloomberg a call. I couldn’t believe he’d run against me to begin with, y’know, we were good friends, although I don’t think he’d appreciate that now, but still, we got along. We were both businessmen, and he still went after me. A tragedy, truly it is.”

`); e.pages.push(`
MOMENTUS VICTORY FOR UNPRECEDENTED UNITY TICKET - POPULISM NO LONGER POPULAR?

It was a harrowing night for the Rebirth Party, formed just a few years ago by Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders. In their first ever Presidential Election, they had lost significantly to the recently unified Democratic and Republican parties, who had nominated Mike Bloomberg, former Mayor of NYC and John Kasich, former Governor of Ohio.

While both President Trump and Vice President Sanders lost in a landslide to the Dem-Rep ticket, their congressional outlooks are a bright spot in an increasingly bleak moment for their upstart party. On top of their 52 seats they defended, they had also picked up an extra 30, mostly within the Midwest and the Plains region, meanwhile within the Senate, they had managed to pick up an extra 6 seats, from Montana all the way through to Oklahoma.

Now as the transition period begins, cracks are already beginning to form amongst the new union, with Progressive Democrats and Christian Right Republicans already incredibly frustrated with how much ‘compromise’ has taken place, and feel that they can not co-exist within the same deal altogether. This has culminated in spats between congressmen, especially between Mike Johnson of Louisiana and Ro Khanna of California, which has already led to factions forming between the disgruntled parties.

What’s next for President Elect Bloomberg is difficult to tell, with him being quiet on any potential nominees for his cabinet, as to hopefully prevent any early rebellions against his leadership. VP Elect Kasich has already signalled that it will be filled with both Democrats and Republicans from all stripes of field, which could make for one of the most interesting cabinets in US history, bar from the previous Trump/Sanders administration, which openly admitted that they had come to an arrangement relatively early on within their premiership.

More will come as time develops.

`); e.pages.push(`
How President Barron Trump broke the 3-Party System.

Barron Trump, running as an independent in the 2048 Presidential Election against President Mamdani (eligible to run after the 29th Amendment), former Republican Governor of New York Lee Zeldin and Rebirth Senator for Ohio Nina Turner, successfully beat all his opponents and won the necessary 275 Electoral College Votes to become President-Elect of the United States. How did this happen?

With President Mamdani’s mishandling of the Israeli-Egyptian war and the soaring gas prices straight after, he was in an incredibly difficult position. Furthermore, with his progressive base in open revolt over the actions of his administration towards Israel, they had decided to back the insurgent campaign of Nina Turner, who had rejuvenated the Rebirth Party after what many pundits described as a ‘managed decline’ for the party, as with it’s two juggernauts, former President Trump and former VP Sanders dying in 2032 (two weeks apart!), they had saw a small, yet gradual decline. Paired with a Republican Party rebounding after their recent midterm successes in 2046, they had felt emboldened to pick a conservative firebrand, former Governor of New York Lee Zeldin, to lead the Republican ticket as what they saw as a ‘communistic takeover of America’ from both Mamdani and Turner.

All this culminated in Barron Trump’s Independent run for the Presidency. In his statement just 2 years ago, he said:

“Our country is going down a path of economic suicide and a lack of any real patriotism. I have a new vision for America. Many of you here will remember my father’s Presidency. He was a good man, and someone I looked up to like an idol, but he was not a good President. He lost against a tired establishment, and failed to bring any meaningful change. I’m going to rectify that, here, today, by announcing my run for the Presidency of the United States. We need a new deal for ordinary Americans who are stamped on day in, day out. I’m not running under any labels, I’m my own man, just like my father. I’m going to make sure Americans get a fair shot in life, and that we, as a nation, can remake the American dream.”

This noble, ‘patriotic’ approach resonated with much of the American public, specifically the working-classes of America, whose keys issues were that of fairness, immigration and the cost of living.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20204830.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-27%20144223.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20172553.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "What A Loser", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-27%20150131.png", "EqFQ61lX528" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] < 3 && uniparty == 2) { // Won ZERO states e.pages.push(`

It wasn’t their fault. It was the system’s, and as Donald would say, ‘THE SYSTEM IS RIGGED!’

They had to face reality- that their vision, their hope for a brighter, more fair America, was beginning to wind down. They gave it their all, hell, Trump made himself bankrupt to fund this election for Rebirth, and they had still come up short. It was nothing more than a humiliation ritual, or at least that is what Donald thought.

“We fought Bernie, we really did, and I thought- well, I thought we’d have more of an impact. I suppose not. Listen, I don’t even think we’ll have a chance beyond this election, our party, our future, it’s down the drain. Y’know, Bloomberg, he was a nice guy, but he turned real nasty during this cycle. I don’t know why, you try and play nice with some of these people, and I mean, you see what they say about you, or me, or our people. The things that they say, it’s just disgraceful, fraudulent even. I have every right to challeng-”

Bernie interrupted him. He wouldn’t allow Donald to be corrupted enough to think to contest these elections. It was over from the start. They both saw it, and yet he still wanted to deny it. Bernie saw it for what it was: a coping mechanism. He knew what he felt at that very moment, he saw it when Clinton disregarded him all those years ago. He knew how to solve it.

“Donald. You have to face reality. We lost. They won. It’s as simple as that. I hate it just as much as you do, and I wish people would see those oligarchic figures for who they are, but they put their trust in them. We have to respect that, and move forward. It won’t be easy, and I know how you think. I was dealt the same hand, and look what happened. We fought the biggest Establishment, and we gave it our all. We can’t deny we put up a good fight. Hell, they had to unite to beat us! Think of it like that.”

The race was over. Donald had accepted defeat. It was time to finally realise the truth.
“I’ll give Bloomberg a call. I couldn’t believe he’d run against me to begin with, y’know, we were good friends, although I don’t think he’d appreciate that now, but still, we got along. We were both businessmen, and he still went after me. A tragedy, truly it is.”
