MOMENTUS VICTORY FOR UNPRECEDENTED UNITY TICKET - POPULISM NO LONGER POPULAR?

It was a harrowing night for the Rebirth Party, formed just a few years ago by Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders. In their first ever Presidential Election, they had lost significantly to the recently unified Democratic and Republican parties, who had nominated Mike Bloomberg, former Mayor of NYC and John Kasich, former Governor of Ohio.

While both President Trump and Vice President Sanders lost in a landslide to the Dem-Rep ticket, their congressional outlooks are a bright spot in an increasingly bleak moment for their upstart party. On top of their 52 seats they defended, they had also picked up an extra 30, mostly within the Midwest and the Plains region, meanwhile within the Senate, they had managed to pick up an extra 6 seats, from Montana all the way through to Oklahoma.

`); e.pages.push(`
How President Barron Trump broke the 3-Party System.

Barron Trump, running as an independent in the 2048 Presidential Election against President Mamdani (eligible to run after the 29th Amendment), former Republican Governor of New York Lee Zeldin and Rebirth Senator for Ohio Nina Turner, successfully beat all his opponents and won the necessary 275 Electoral College Votes to become President-Elect of the United States. How did this happen?

With President Mamdani’s mishandling of the Israeli-Egyptian war and the soaring gas prices straight after, he was in an incredibly difficult position. Furthermore, with his progressive base in open revolt over the actions of his administration towards Israel, they had decided to back the insurgent campaign of Nina Turner, who had rejuvenated the Rebirth Party after what many pundits described as a ‘managed decline’ for the party, as with it’s two juggernauts, former President Trump and former VP Sanders dying in 2032 (two weeks apart!), they had saw a small, yet gradual decline. Paired with a Republican Party rebounding after their recent midterm successes in 2046, they had felt emboldened to pick a conservative firebrand, former Governor of New York Lee Zeldin, to lead the Republican ticket as what they saw as a ‘communistic takeover of America’ from both Mamdani and Turner.

All this culminated in Barron Trump’s Independent run for the Presidency. In his statement just 2 years ago, he said:

“Our country is going down a path of economic suicide and a lack of any real patriotism. I have a new vision for America. Many of you here will remember my father’s Presidency. He was a good man, and someone I looked up to like an idol, but he was not a good President. He lost against a tired establishment, and failed to bring any meaningful change. I’m going to rectify that, here, today, by announcing my run for the Presidency of the United States. We need a new deal for ordinary Americans who are stamped on day in, day out. I’m not running under any labels, I’m my own man, just like my father. I’m going to make sure Americans get a fair shot in life, and that we, as a nation, can remake the American dream.”

This noble, ‘patriotic’ approach resonated with much of the American public, specifically the working-classes of America, whose keys issues were that of fairness, immigration and the cost of living.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20204830.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-27%20144223.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20172553.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "What A Loser", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-27%20150131.png", "EqFQ61lX528" ); } } // -------------------------------- if (quickstats[0] == 538 && uniparty == 3) { e.pages.push(`
Archive of Our Own
A Billionaire, A Revolutionary
by Big Toni
Rating:General Audiences Pairing:Sanders/Trump Category:Romance

After a long and hard campaign trail victory has been achieved, and the knight in shining armor, Donald, and helpless princess, Bernie go back to the castle, the White House, to celebrate winning the election together. It wasn’t an easy victory, no, some would say it was a pyrrhic victory, for the pair had realized many things about each other, and truth be told, they were happy as ever to have learned so much about themselves in the process.

As they’re both driven to the White House, Bernie and Donald sit side by side from each other. They were laughing and chatting about all the funny hijinks the two had gotten up to with each other, Covid had brought them both down, it hit the economy so hard, and a lot of lives were lost from it, so the laughter the two shared with each other served as a good respite from all that mess

As the two were laughing, Bernie sat his hand down on the arm rest, and Donald, while not looking where he was going to put his hand down to rest, accidently touched Bernie’s hand. The car was dead silent, and the two looked at each other for a fleeting moment, and looked away, and the two blushed hard. “S-sorry” Donald said, sheepishly, not even managing to look at Bernie. The blush on two faces remained, stagnant, and everlasting.

The driver in the front of the car asked if everything was alright, and two quickly said yes, and the rest of the ride was ridden in silence. When the two reached the White House, they quickly exited the vehicle, and the rest of the night was a blur. The two slept in separate rooms, in separate halls, and the two had separate hearts, and they both woke up to a peaceful morning, a peaceful sky.

As the sun was rising in the sky, Donald walked up to the balcony, and faced the sun. While looking at the beautiful sun, he got a cigar and lit it and started smoking it. He enjoyed the amazing view of the Washington monument, and watched people on their morning jogs in D.C.

He remembers visiting it on the campaign trail, he giggles to himself about that wasted day. As he was enjoying the view, Donald heard a familiar voice, Bernie’s voice

Donald almost drops his cigar, he was scared, he didn’t want to face that man since the incident in the car, but now he has to face him, now he has to face the biggest challenge he has ever faced in his political career. “Donald.” That voice, it came from a Vermonter, from a man raised thousands of miles away from where the two are now today. “Donald”. The voice is getting louder, his heart is pounding. Bernie’s heart is also pounding, the two hearts are at risk of ripping from both of the men’s chest and embracing into one. “Donald!”. He can’t do it, he can’t face him. “DONALD!”. “I LOVE YOU!” Donald says, shouting with all of his might, the power of which shocked the two. “I love you two, Donald.” Donald drops his cigar, and the two embrace, the two hearts embrace, the billionaire and a revolutionary, at last, unite.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/bro.gif'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Y.M.C.A", "Village People", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/4/4c/Cruisin1978.jpg", "khOFB1Erzdg" ); } } if (quickstats[0] >= 535 && uniparty == 3) { e.pages.push(`
Archive of Our Own
A Billionaire, A Revolutionary
by Big Toni
Rating:General Audiences Pairing:Sanders/Trump Category:Romance

After a long and hard campaign trail victory has been achieved, and the knight in shining armor, Donald, and helpless princess, Bernie go back to the castle, the White House, to celebrate winning the election together. It wasn’t an easy victory, no, some would say it was a pyrrhic victory, for the pair had realized many things about each other, and truth be told, they were happy as ever to have learned so much about themselves in the process.

As they’re both driven to the White House, Bernie and Donald sit side by side from each other. They were laughing and chatting about all the funny hijinks the two had gotten up to with each other, Covid had brought them both down, it hit the economy so hard, and a lot of lives were lost from it, so the laughter the two shared with each other served as a good respite from all that mess

As the two were laughing, Bernie sat his hand down on the arm rest, and Donald, while not looking where he was going to put his hand down to rest, accidently touched Bernie’s hand. The car was dead silent, and the two looked at each other for a fleeting moment, and looked away, and the two blushed hard. “S-sorry” Donald said, sheepishly, not even managing to look at Bernie. The blush on two faces remained, stagnant, and everlasting.

The driver in the front of the car asked if everything was alright, and two quickly said yes, and the rest of the ride was ridden in silence. When the two reached the White House, they quickly exited the vehicle, and the rest of the night was a blur. The two slept in separate rooms, in separate halls, and the two had separate hearts, and they both woke up to a peaceful morning, a peaceful sky.

As the sun was rising in the sky, Donald walked up to the balcony, and faced the sun. While looking at the beautiful sun, he got a cigar and lit it and started smoking it. He enjoyed the amazing view of the Washington monument, and watched people on their morning jogs in D.C.

He remembers visiting it on the campaign trail, he giggles to himself about that wasted day. As he was enjoying the view, Donald heard a familiar voice, Bernie’s voice

Donald almost drops his cigar, he was scared, he didn’t want to face that man since the incident in the car, but now he has to face him, now he has to face the biggest challenge he has ever faced in his political career. “Donald.” That voice, it came from a Vermonter, from a man raised thousands of miles away from where the two are now today. “Donald”. The voice is getting louder, his heart is pounding. Bernie’s heart is also pounding, the two hearts are at risk of ripping from both of the men’s chest and embracing into one. “Donald!”. He can’t do it, he can’t face him. “DONALD!”. “I LOVE YOU!” Donald says, shouting with all of his might, the power of which shocked the two. “I love you two, Donald.” Donald drops his cigar, and the two embrace, the two hearts embrace, the billionaire and a revolutionary, at last, unite.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/bro.gif'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Y.M.C.A", "Village People", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/4/4c/Cruisin1978.jpg", "khOFB1Erzdg" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 269 && quickstats[0] < 535 && uniparty == 3) { e.pages.push(`

TRIUMPHANT POPULISTS WIN MANDATE - BUTTIGIEG AND HALEY DEFEATED

A victory to last the ages. That's what the White House staffers were saying.
"We can now project, relatively early on in the night, that Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders have won the election with a convincing majority. The electorate have decided to follow the Populist road and have given them a mandate to carry out their joint vision for America. There hasn't been any word on what Pete Buttigieg or Nikki Haley might say or do, but their supporters are despondent."

Donald had that grin he always had. He came out victorious, his ego ballooning over his enormous victory. Bernie was much more reserved, but you could still see the joy from a mile away. They had earned this victory. They had everything against them, and still managed to beat back the mayor and the governor. They had come a long way together, but now was the time to celebrate their win.

Bernie and Donald gave each other a look, and smiled. All that hard work, all that campaigning, all that energy that they had invested into their campaign paid off with a win, and God willing, a Trifecta in Congress. That was too soon to tell, but it looked likely. Lots of work was still to be done, but for this day, they could celebrate this achievement. They saw their shares of horror, of fright, and now it came back to frighten the Establishment. America spoke, and it wanted CHANGE.

"Well, Bernard, y'know, we did it, because frankly, it was us, no one else would've been able to pull it off, right? We've made it through. We're going to Make America Great Again, and we're going to make Rebirth stay relevant, believe me."
"I hope so, Donald, we've come a long way, and uh, well, thank you for this. It's not everyday you get an opportunity to fight the establishment. I'm glad it was with you, and I'm glad we won. Now's the time to reign it in, focus on policy, and get the work done. We can't get distracted."

Then a staffer walked in.
"Mr President, Pete Buttigieg is on the line."
"Put him through. Oh boy, this'll be fun."

The line connected. Buttigieg sounded like he was about to cry, and yet, he was relieved that it was over.
"Congratulations Mr President, clearly, uhm, the country has spoken, and well, uh, you won. I just hope that, uh, we can move forward as a nation and begin to he-"
"Yes, thank you, thank you. You were a good opponent, to be honest with you. A real hard fought campaign, y'know, I respect it, you didn't give up. Hopefully I shall see you soon Pete, goodbye."
The line cut. He didn't want to hear anymore whining.

Then came Haley.
“Do we really have to listen to her drivel? Buttigieg was bad enough but this-”
“Donald, please, at the very least, be a bit more respectful. It’s only one phone call, after this, well, you won’t speak to her again, will you?”
Then he picked up the phone and answered.
"Congratulations, Mr President, uh, it was a good fought campaign, and I'd like to send my best wishes to you. We need unity in these times of per-"
"Thank you Haley, thank you, it was a hard fought campaign, you're right, and sure, we'll have unity, so long as you play ball! I'm just kiddin, y'know, I'm sure it'll all come together nicely. Thank you, truly, great call. I'll see you soon, alright, bye bye."

`); e.pages.push(`

RESULTS FINAL: REBIRTH AT 221, DEMS AT 123, GOP AT 91 - REBIRTH MAJORITY IN THE HOUSE

With the final results being counted across the nation, the Rebirth party, spearheaded by both Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders, have come out victorious within the House of Representatives, securing a much needed majority to be able to pass their agenda. This comes after a blowout defeat to the tickets of Pete Buttigieg and Nikki Haley, who were expected to deadlock and keep Trump and Sanders out of the White House, however Rebirth defied the polls once more and managed to win in a shock landslide.

However, the outlook in the Senate is a bit more grim for Rebirth, as they had only gained 7 seats within the chamber, boosting their numbers up to 26 Senate seats, and ultimately having to work with both parties to get major pieces of legislation completed. This might complicate things for the administration, as some of their major policy platforms like Tariffs or Medicare for All could further be stalled by a Democratic and Republican establishment unwilling to budge on these issues.

Both Democratic and Republican leaders are despondent at the outcome of the Presidential and local results of the election - completely dashing what was supposed to be a victory night for them. With a big win for Rebirth, more and more politicians might decide to jump ship and join them rather than face more 'humiliation' (as Donald Trump said) at the hands of the anti-establishment.

With both major parties collapsing at the seams and a new resurgent populist movement taking the helm, America once again stands at a crossroads between itself. Donald Trump, co-founder of the Rebirth Party, had this to say:

"We fought like hell and we won, I mean look what happened! It was a big win, I mean it, they're saying it was the biggest win, maybe ever. Not seen since Washington! But y'know, we need now to come together, in peace, harmony, and let's drive our message. MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN! MAKE AMERICA FAIR AGAIN! Thank you and GOD BLESS."

`); e.pages.push(`

GREENLAND PURCHASED - ADMITTED AS FIFTY-FIRST STATE. MANIFEST DESTINY REBORN?

Greenland has been admitted into the United States of America, officially making it the 51st state in the Union and a vital base of operations for America as a whole. This comes after numerous threats from the Russian Federation and the People's Republic of China, with the US making moves to secure it's hold over North America. Denmark agreed to sell Greenland for $12.5 billion USD, and also got guarantees of increased investment within the Danish economy.

This is yet another win for the Trump/Sanders administration, as with the previous purchases of Vancouver Island and Iwo Jima, it has expanded American hegemony across both the Pacific and Atlantic Oceans, preventing any headway towards their adversaries. Furthermore, with a renewed 'Atlantic Charter' with Prime Minister Nigel Farage and a strengthened NATO, America seems poised for another few decades of continued global resilience and domination.

With the 2028 Election close approaching, this would surely be seen as a boost for the Rebirth ticket of Josh Hawley/Alexandria-Ocasio Cortez, who have been struggling recently due to their lack of a cohesive policy positions and decreased campaign funds. They have needed desperately for any sort of win, and they have just gotten it, as to whether or not it'll have a poll effect is yet to be seen, but it is likely.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/tuff.jpg'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aZyAKy2e7UEiSmg5/rebirthhouse.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aZyAKy2e7UEiSmg5/Map_of_the_United_States_Including_Greenland.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Sweeter Dreams", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20183108.png", "ijSmOtBiyeY" ); } } else if (e.final_overall_results[0].candidate === 50000 && e.final_overall_results[0].electoral_votes > 269 && uniparty == 3) { e.pages.push(`

BUTTIGIEG COMES OUT ON TOP - CALLS FOR NATION TO ‘HEAL’ AFTER BITTER DIVIDE

“And we can now project that Pete Buttigieg, a rising star within the Democratic Party, has won the Presidential race, and will become the 46th President of the United States. At a time with massive unrest and COVID-19 spreading throughout America, Buttigieg has been able to keep his coalition together while also-”

Trump turned off the TV. He couldn’t bear listening to it anymore. He knew that he had lost, he didn’t want the so-called ‘crooked media’ to hang it over his head. It was embarrassing. Losing to some ‘no-name mayor’ in Indiana out of all places? He came from the Empire State. Yet he was reduced to being humiliated.

How could this have happened? Surely, with 3 major parties going for the Presidency, surely, it would end in deadlock? Surely, there was some wiggle room? It was Trump’s element, he wrote the ‘ART OF THE DEAL’. It was all for naught. Buttigieg had successfully become the 46th President of the United States, and effectively cut Trump’s reign short. How could this have happened? They had campaigned so hard, so vigorously, and still he crossed the ‘magical 270’.

“Fucking Haley, this is her fault. If she didn’t get nominated, the Republicans, they would have joined us Bernie, I can see it now, we would’ve won a TOTAL LANDSLIDE. But they sold out, they couldn’t do it. I was their savior once, y’know, their ‘salvation’. The Christians, they loved me. The free-traders, all of them, they nominated me, but couldn’t do it 4 years later? What a sham. I mean, look at Haley, no brain whatsoever, threw this election to the Democrats, it’s so sad to think about really.”

Bernie was sitting down, listening to what Trump was saying. He didn’t agree with him, why would the Republicans nominate him after abandoning the party? They would never sign up for it, especially since he was on the ticket. A self described “Democratic Socialist” on a Republican ticket? No chance. But he didn’t allow that to cloud his judgement. He spoke to Trump in a firm manner.

“We need to call Buttigieg and concede, it’s the right thing to do, otherwise, well, the ‘crooked media’, while I wouldn’t go so far, certainly have their…problems. Either way, they’ll boil you over it, they’ll use everything against you. We both know this.”

Donald nodded along. He had accepted what had happened. He knew, for the time being, that it was time to concede. But deep inside his mind, he only wanted to do one thing: run for the Office of President of the United States. No matter what the results were today, or in a year's time, or in the midterms. He knew he was ready to take up the mantle again. For America. With, or without, Bernie.

`); e.pages.push(`

TRUMP AND HALEY CONCEDE - NATION RETURNED TO NORMALCY?

Haley conceded first. She performed just as badly, if not worse than Rebirth’s electoral performance. It was almost embarrassing seeing a party that was well established for over a hundred years not even come close to the Presidency. A tragedy which could have easily been avoided if Republicans had spent a bit more time trying to break Trump up from Bernie and his rhetoric. They should not have placed so much trust in one man who is easily swayed. They thought he was manipulated by the ‘socialists’, by the so called ‘agitators of Washington D.C’. They couldn’t stop their meteoric rise from irrelevance in the Presidency to leading a whole new revolution full to the brim of populism and “hope”.

Then, Trump conceded through a speech. He couldn’t bring himself to call Buttigieg himself. He saw that as beneath him. Why call him when he already knew he won? Polite courtesy? Since when have the establishment ever been polite or ‘courteous’ to Donald Trump? He saw them as sycophants, as leeches waiting to move onto the next victim.

“Well, congratulations on Buttigieg winning, I mean, I don’t think he deserved it frankly, he had nothing to campaign on, but he won, so I respect that and wish him ‘the best’.”

That sarcasm wasn’t left on anybody.

“I think, now, we need to move forward as Americans, and to continue working for a greater America. I fought for that, I fought for it hard, and while we did come up short, we lost by a whisker, I mean, look- we’re going to continue to build up this movement, Bernie and I, and we’re going to keep fighting for you, THE PEOPLE. It’s super important, truly it is. Bernie, would you like to say a few words?”

Bernie looked at him, slightly surprised. Vice Presidents don’t typically talk at these sorts of events, hence why he didn’t have any notes or speech prepared, but he went up anyways, out of respect for Trump.

“Well, uh, thank you Donald, and he’s right, we’re going to continue this movement, our movement, because this isn’t about me or him, or our jobs as politicians, but it’s about you, the American people, and how we can serve you to the best of our ability. We may have lost this race, but we’re in this for the long haul. We want to stand for all Americans, and fight for a more prosperous and unified America. We could not do it without you. Please, join our movement, and let’s Make America Fair Again. Thank you.”

The chanting started soon after.
“USA! USA! USA!”
The fight was only just beginning.

`); e.pages.push(`

BUTTIGIEG STEADY WITH 50% APPROVAL RATING - MIDTERMS BODE GOOD RESULTS FOR DEMOCRATS

In what is considered an upset, Democrats had successfully managed to win back both the House and Senate, ensuring an actual majority within Congress without relying on the Republican or Rebirth parties. Despite recent defections and a slow economic recovery, Buttigieg managed to galvanise his supporters after recent Supreme Court rulings had stonewalled much of his legislative agenda, including further student debt relief efforts and implementing looser immigration controls.

This enabled Democrats to campaign on human rights, justice for students and democracy, which core liberals and progressives overwhelmingly responded positively to, and including many Rebirth supporters as well. It shouldn’t be surprising then to see a Democratic majority in the House (226) and 50 Seats within the Senate (VP as tiebreaker vote).

Some surprises of the night included when Former President Trump won the Florida Senate seat that was up for election, with Former VP Sanders also re-taking his Vermont Senate Seat from Patrick Leahy, who had previously endorsed Bernie back in 2020. With both men back on the front line of politics, many are speculating that they are indeed considering another run for the Presidency in 2024. As to whether or not they’ve actively made a deal between the two is unknown.

Republicans on the whole suffered the worst on election night, with losing up to 30 seats within the House and 4 within the Senate. Notable names include House Minority Leader Kevin McCarthy, Jim Jordan from Ohio and Dan Crenshaw of Texas. This, coupled with losing 2nd place to Rebirth, many within the Republican Party are calling for a significant rebrand and reposturing of policy, with differing opinions. Representative Mike Johnson is calling for more conservative policies, while Representative Ryan Zinke of Montana is calling for more moderation, signalling that he had almost lost his seat to Rebirth and that:

“Populism, as a whole, is sweeping the Plains. We need to embrace a more sensible policy which can appeal to traditional Republican voters.”

With Democrats jubilant over their recent victories, they will now be able to push forward with their agenda and not have to rely on Rebirth or the Republicans to deadlock/pass legislation.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Alfred%20E%20Neuman.jpeg'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Alfred%20E%20Neuman.jpeg'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Alfred%20E%20Neuman.jpeg'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "High Hopes", "Jazzwave", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/4/4c/Pray_for_the_Wicked.png", "GJY8OMJXRAk" ); } } else if (e.final_overall_results[0].candidate === 50002 && e.final_overall_results[0].electoral_votes > 269 && uniparty == 3) { e.pages.push(`

HALEY DEFIES EXPECTATIONS - REPUBLICANS RETAKE PRESIDENCY AFTER POPULIST TAKEOVER

What? How? Why? Those were all the questions floating within Trump and Bernie’s head. Did America really want a hardline Neo-Conservative within the White House? They couldn’t believe it. Donald Trump, once a paragon of the Republican Party, now has been considered a relic. Nikki Haley, the reinvention of George Bush, successfully operated a regime change within the United States of America. Iran was next after this. It just wasn’t possible in their eyes. Was this under some divine intervention? Neither men were particularly religious, nor believers in the superstitious, but surely America didn’t decide to elect Haley?

Maybe they could find solace in the fact that she only won maybe a third of the vote, or at the very least, under 50%. And if the returns from outcalling states looked good, maybe they could come second, but this was something else. Donald thought Jeb was bad, but her? It was dreadful. She was like Jeb Bush in every way, but more hawkish and more ‘violent’ in his words. It was like the whole country was playing a whole joke on him. He thought it was a sham. He thought it was rigged. The Electoral College, in all it’s infinite wisdom, decided to hand the keys to a warmonger. Alas, reality was indeed reality, and they had to accept that fact.

Bernie looked troubled. All his work, all his compromises, all his dealings, threw out the window in an instant. A sense of dread washed over him. He looked distraught. So did Trump, to some extent, but that was more hidden behind his utter contempt and anger.

Trump spoke up:

“How did she win. How. Fucks sake, I fought against politicians like her all my life, y’know, I’ve been against these wars, these policies, ever since the 80s. Remember Reagan? I liked him, liked him a lot, no one talks about it, but it’s true. But the one thing, in fact there were many things, but the one thing I really didn’t like were these trade deals, we were being scammed Bernie. We both were united behind these scam deals, even then. Haley wants NAFTA, she wants that Trans Pacific Partnership, she wants to sell out America. It’s so sad, so sad really, America has fallen for her lies. We need to fight it Bernie- we need to, otherwise we simply won’t have a country anymore.”

Bernie looked up. Did he just hear what he think he just heard? It was out of the question. Claiming it was rigged? It wasn’t in his nature. But…something told him that this wasn’t right. Something wasn’t adding up. Everything told him that this wasn’t the way forward, that this moment right now shouldn’t even be happening, but the President was resolute in this. Bernie had a choice to make.

“Donald, look, we uh, we fought the good fight-”

“Bernie, don’t give me that whole speech. We both know what happened.”

He was stunned. It was like he read his mind. Maybe Donald was good at reading people after all. He read Hillary, he read Buttigieg, he read Haley like a book.

“Are you suggesting we challenge this?” Donald gave a nod. “Okay, okay, well, uh, I’ll back you in this. We need to, uh, save our country. The Establishment rigged it. You saw it plain as day. We need to fight this.”

“That’s the spirit Bernie, look, we’re going to go out there and fight like hell, trust me, our people, our beautiful patriots, they’ll stand behind us. We’re the real winners, not these phonies. They have no idea what they have just done.”

`); e.pages.push(`

TRUMP & SANDERS CALL ELECTION ‘RIGGED’, BUTTIGIEG & BEL EDWARDS CONCEDE TO VICTORIOUS HALEY.

“I have just congratulated Governor Haley on her victory tonight. I hope that the country can heal and I wish her the very best in government. I also congratulate her on being the first female President of the United States of America, and the first woman of colour to be President. That, is a historical achievement, and she deserves it. I will always be here to be of any assistance for her should she need it. While we may have lost this campaign, our movement hasn’t stopped. No. It will grow to new heights. We will hold government to account, we will fight for rights across America, and we will pursue what is best for America. Thank you, and God bless.”

Bernie and Donald were sat down watching Buttigieg give his speech. They both had thoughts that this was some sort of stitch-up, that they had some sort of informal agreement. Both the main parties, they agreed on so much, and yet they refused to face reality. They were one of the same.

They couldn’t bear it anymore. Trump, with Sanders, went out to Mar-A-Lago, to their crowd of supporters. They were fervent. The energy, the passion, they knew what was about to happen. History in the making.

“USA! USA! USA!”

Screams of ‘STOP THE STEAL’, ‘FEEL THE BERN’, ‘MAFA/MAGA’ could be heard all across the room. It could be heard all across America.

`); e.pages.push(`

PRESIDENT HALEY TO ADDRESS THE NATION ON RECENT STRIKES IN NORTH KOREA - KIM JONG UN REPORTED MISSING

January 15th, 2023

With the recent Midterm results being abysmal for the Republicans, President Haley has decided to take action on her own. Last night, American forces were deployed in South Korea, and in a show of might against the communist North Korea, the United States has decided to bomb Pyongyang, with early reports suggesting that the ‘Supreme Leader’, Kim Jong Un, has been hit by these strikes and is reported missing.

This comes after multiple threats from the North Koreans, especially on the American bases in Guam and the Pacific in general. Both Japan and South Korea have backed the United States in these targeted strikes against the North Koreans, and promised that should America require any support, they will receive it. NATO as a whole has also been fully supportive of these strikes, calling on North Korean citizens to ‘rise up’ and ‘take action against the brutal regime’.

The response at home has been overwhelmingly negative, with many younger Americans firmly opposing the war in North Korea, with polls showing that over 60% of Americans aged 18 - 24 opposing, while just 21% support the war. This is polar opposite to much of the older generation, with 45% of Americans aged 40 - 65 supporting, with 29% opposing.

This could very well spell a doom spiral for the Haley Administration, with Trump and Sanders mounting their own Presidential run for November of next year, while the Democrats scramble to find any suitable candidates which can beat back Neo-Conservatism and Populism as a whole.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/haleytooltip.jpg'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/haleytooltip.jpg'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-05%20130818.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Bomb Iran", "Vince Vance & the Valiants", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-05%20133150.png", "s8hEtI9AI0U" ); } } else if (e.final_overall_results[0].candidate === 301 && e.final_overall_results[0].electoral_votes < 269 && uniparty == 3) { e.pages.push(`

REBIRTH WITH MOST ELECTORAL VOTES - BUTTIGIEG AND HALEY TRAIL BEHIND POPULIST FORCE

No one could have expected this. Rebirth, which once started as a protest party led by Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders, had successfully deadlocked the Electoral College, and even managed to come first place. Both men were surprised. They certainly weren’t expecting this, let alone coming close to the top of the line. But it felt good to achieve such a historic result in such a short period of time. They both felt, at the very least, proud of what they had accomplished. So many people, in so many states, saw their potential, their brand, and wanted that change. The status-quo, which seemed unstoppable to mount a challenge against just 4 short years ago, now seemed beatable for those who put their time, effort and resources in. Late into the night, as congressional returns were coming in, Rebirth were doing far better than expected. Donald and Bernie gave each other a pat on the back as they saw the announcement that the Rebirth Party had just passed over 120 seats in the House, and 12 Senate seats. Bernie hadn’t felt this way since his first ever win back in Burlington, Vermont. Trump was his usual self. They had created a movement which would outlive them. That was the objective.

However, it was short lived. They soon realised that they did not have over 270, and in fact, it was going to be a long, gruesome few months in order to have any chance to re-enter the White House & Naval Observatory. Careful optimism had turned into sour pessimism. How were they going to grapple with the fact that they were very unlikely to make any headway through the House or the Senate? Republicans now saw Trump as a traitor to their cause - a ‘bleeding heart liberal’, while Bernie Sanders was being grilled over his newfound ties to Trump and his cronies. There was no way in hell that they would get any sort of deal struck.

That reality hit hard. Harder than any other loss they had endured. They knew then that their time was up. A respectable performance, for two people who had hated each other just 4 years ago and in an even shorter time formed a revolutionary party.

“We fought Bernie, we fought tremendously well, some are saying we’re the most successful third party, maybe ever? Listen- those crooks, they couldn’t stand a chance, and yet, the system still favours them so much, it’s so sad, so terrible, we should’ve campaigned on abolishing this frankly awful system of governance. From being way down in the polls, to way up, in such a short amount of time, I mean, frankly, no one’s been able to do it before, y’know?”

Bernie nodded along. Their political journeys, from opposite ends of the political spectrum, ended up together, as one, united behind a common cause.

“I agree Donald. I’ve called for the abolition of the Electoral College for years, and yet my former Democrat colleagues were too scared of real change. It saddened me quite a bit. I, uh, well, we fought for change, and we might still get it, but we have to remain tough, firm and vigilant. I’ve seen what the establishment can do- I mean, look at wikileaks, the DNC, the pushback on our agenda. It worries me greatly that ‘dark money’ can still hold such an influence.”

They both carried on their conversation, until they had realised something.

Neither Buttigieg or Haley called. Something was up. Something sketchy.

`); e.pages.push(`

BUTTIGIEG AND HALEY STITCH UP - COMBINED EVS TO PROPEL THEMSELVES TO TOP JOBS - REBIRTH CALL FRAUD

“What? Are you fucking serious? No- no, I don’t believe it. They truly have sold their souls. I told you Bernie, I said it, they’re crooks, frauds, they’ll do anything for themselves, I mean, look, can you imagine President Buttigieg? With VP Haley? It makes me sick, frankly I am DISGUSTED in what they have done. I said that they’d do it.”

Unbelievable. Donald had expected it. Bernie was blindsided. Were they really that terrified of taking it to Congress? Were they afraid of losing? Regardless of whether or not the President expected it, he was boiling with rage. He thought 2016 was bad, with the stitch-up with Bernard (back then, anyways), but this was a new low.

“Can you imagine Buttigieg delivering the State of the Union? Haley clapping along? It’s shameful Bernie, shameful, they ought to have some decency. I’m not going to call them, no way, they CHEATED.”

Bernie was still taking it all in. He looked at the map, then the TV, and then the map again. There really wasn’t a path for victory for Rebirth, even with the recounts they would ask for.

“Donald- look, think about it this way, they’re ‘scared’ of our successes abroad and at home. That’s why they united, it may be a tough pill to swallow, but politically speaking, this may be good for us. I know you don’t like sitting on a losing streak or whatnot, so what if we pledge to run again in 4 years? In private of course, we can’t reannounce immediately, we’ll seem desperate, but if they can play dirty and play theft, then so can we. We can steal their voter base right from under their noses. They can’t stand one another. Will you at least give it some thought?”

Donald looked at Bernie. Was he serious? Was he actually doing a bit of ‘politicking’? He didn’t expect this from Bernie out of all people, this was completely out of field for him. But he liked it. If Donald had shown Bernie anything, it was to be more ‘tricky’ and head-on.

“Okay, okay, fine, but we’re staying in the same positions. I’m not giving up my Presidential spot. No way. We need to use this time to, to, I don’t know, continue to build up Rebirth. Look- We need to build up the grassroots, they love us, and only us, and we need to use that to our advantage. Our megafans, the real patriots, they’ll buy into anything we give them. We need the money Bernie, if we are to do this, you can’t continue to rely on my personal fortune.”

It almost sounded like a parody of Engels and Marx. One was the inspirational revolutionary leader who wanted to free the masses and help the down-trodden, and the other was the businessman sympathetic to his messaging and would help fund his career and ambitions.

`); e.pages.push(`

GAETZ/CORTEZ WIN 2028 PRESIDENTIAL ELECTION - FIRST 2 FULL TERMS FOR REBIRTH - WHAT HAPPENS NOW?

In a huge upset election, President-Elect Matt Gaetz, Representative from Florida and VP-Elect Alexandria Ocasio Cortez, Representative from New York, won the 2028 election with historic swings, building a ‘grand coalition’ of America First Republicans, Progressive Democrats and the traditional Rebirth base. This, paired with the scandals revolving Liz Cheney and Beto O’Rourke, would allow the reinvented Rebirth ticket to successfully break the deadlocks and come out victorious in an unfavourable political system. Matt Gaetz after Oregon was called, successfully passing him over 270 Electoral College Votes needed to win:

“America, we have come so far, and let me tell you this - Rebirth will move America forward, and we will put American citizens first. Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders started a movement. That movement was built on the principles of fair trade, focusing on American lives and bringing back the American Dream. That dream has been awakened today. We’re going to bring justice reform. We’re going to bring our factories back. We’re going to bring fairness back. We’re going to bring AMERICA BACK!”

Both Cheney and O’Rourke had called both Gaetz and AOC to congratulate them on their wins and wishing them the best possible success for their administration and for America. In both statements, they called for unity around the flag and that whether you’re a Republican, Democrat or Rebirth, now was the time to ‘heal the divide and come together as Americans’.

Many are now speculating on what cabinet positions will be filled in first, and who they will be occupied by. Rumours are circulating that Ilhan Omar, Thomas Massie, Josh Hawley and Elizabeth Warren are all in consideration, but nothing has been confirmed by the Presidential transition team.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/tuff.jpg'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/tuff.jpg'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-05%20130531.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "What A Loser", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-27%20150131.png", "EqFQ61lX528" ); } } else if (e.final_overall_results[1].candidate === 301 && e.final_overall_results[0].electoral_votes < 270 && uniparty == 3) { e.pages.push(`

POPULIST FORCE COME IN SECOND PLACE - BATTLE FOR THE WHITE HOUSE JUST BEGINNING

The expected result. Trump was obviously hoping for first place. Maybe a few thousand more votes here and there in certain states, but they just couldn’t get the numbers for it. Still, for what they were given, it was certainly a respectable result, and nothing to be ashamed of. Bernie was more than happy with this- in fact, he was amazed they even came up second place. It was surprising. What started as a movement for Populism turned into a well-trained political machine which could outlive both their personalities. This result proved that. America, once a bastion for the status-quo, once a beacon for the two party system, had been systemically smashed by Trump/Sanders. They had achieved the nigh-impossible. They brought America from the brink of indifference and brought a fervor which couldn’t be removed.

Their hardship was finally over. It didn’t matter whether Haley or Buttigieg were first or third, what mattered was that they had successfully planted a foothold across America. Whether it was in the Plains, whether it was in the Midwest, whether it was in New England or wherever else, they knew, without any uncertainty, that they were going to continue to be a force. They knew, that in spite of everything being thrown against them, having all their records scrutinised, their personal lives attacked and demolished, that they, as political figures, could stand tall and say that they Establishment, in all their power and might, were scared of them.

They saw the projections. They saw the votes. They saw what needed to be said. Trump saw what he had to do. Bernie saw it too. They knew they had lost, but they saw this as a victory. A victory for the common man. Haley, Buttigieg, the lesser parties, they can get all the spoils for all they cared. They knew history was made this day. Donald looked to Bernie, and they both gave each other a handshake and a hug.

“We fought well Bernie, we did very well, maybe we could’ve done better, who knows, but we brought out the biggest threat to the Establishment that hasn’t been seen since Teddy Roosevelt. These politicians, grifters I call em’, they haven’t seen the end of us yet Bernie, just look at these results, I’m proud of it, to be honest with you. They had no idea what hit them. Doesn’t matter who came first, honestly, they’re the same party if we’re being honest. What we did this night will be remembered in American history. We will be taught about in schools, we’ll be known as the people who stepped up and rose against the ROT.”

Bernie agreed. They both knew that what they had accomplished, together, was truly a remarkable feat. One that could not be replicated.

“I think, Mr President, the nation spoke, and it gave us an answer. They want an alternative. They want real change, I can’t blame them, you just have to look at the Midwest, which has been crying out for change for decades. You look at Obama’s win, you look at Reagan's win, and you see, time after time, they cried out for change, and they were abandoned. It’s sad.”

There was hope. A hope for a brighter America. Even if it was just a spark, a light at the end of the tunnel, it was there. A rejuvenated America. The fight for freedom was only just beginning.

`); e.pages.push(`

ROAD TO WHITE HOUSE UNCLEAR - ALL THREE PARTIES VYING FOR CONTROL

America was at a crossroads. The hegemony of the two main parties were completely shattered. Rebirth, with all the momentum in the current landscape, left it’s mark on the map. Second place was no easy feat. It felt comforting for both Trump and Sanders. Their conflicting, yet similar visions for America, proved to be a winner. Or, at the very least, popular with segments of the population. It was awe-inspiring. Two men, of polar opposite upbringings, of polar opposite political thought and belief, managed to put their differences aside, for the ‘good of the country’, and fought against a common enemy. A common enemy which plagued their agenda every step of the way. It was a difficult road, a slippery slope, but they had managed to do it.

With the election results now locked in place, the Republicans, Democrats and the new Rebirth parties would have a choice to make. Form a coalition between one party or another, or duke it out for a brawl that would last for months on end. They had chosen the latter. All three parties remain confident of victory come January, all of them saw one thing: Victory.

As America woke up to see the results, they were baffled. Just 4 years ago, they had saw relatively the same thing: A deadlocked election. Now, they had to deal with it again. They were sick of it. More infighting. More rebellions. More destruction.

It was difficult to explain to the public what had happened. Why was there more elections taking place? Why was there even an Electoral College present? What was it’s purpose? Many thought it gave stability, it gave certainty, but at this very moment, it failed to give any sort of purpose.

All three Presidential nominees vowed to keep on fighting, and vowed to win. Time would tell whether or not they would be able to keep that promise.

`); e.pages.push(`

CONGRATULATIONS PRESIDENT HALEY…AND VICE PRESIDENT SANDERS?

Four short years ago, the nation was shocked with what seemed to be an impossibility. A Donald Trump Presidency, with Bernie Sanders becoming his Vice President. It seemed too ludicrous to be true, and yet, it happened. Now the nation faces a similar predicament. Nikki Haley, a true-bred Conservative, having to work with a ‘Democratic Socialist’ and recently turned Rebirth politician. It seemed too ludicrous to be true, and yet, it happened.

It was a surprise to both politicians. In a statement to the press, President-Elect Haley stated:

“Well, it wasn’t the intended result, and I’m just as much surprised as you are, but I want to ensure a constructive and working relationship with Vice President Sanders. He’s a, uh, principled man, and I think I can get along just fine with him. It’s just up to him now, whether or not he wants to work with me. That’s all I have to say really. I want to work for the American people, and I will ensure that their voice is heard.”

Democrats were astonished. They were blocked out of any sort of power again. Buttigieg was despondent.

“Well, I congratulate President-Elect Haley. She clearly fought hard, and she should now work for what the American people want. I also want to congratulate Vice President Sanders.”

While Buttigieg was relatively statesmanlike, Donald Trump, the outgoing President, was relatively quiet. When quizzed about what had taken place, he said:

“Well, I just want to congratulate my terrific VP Sanders, he’s a good guy, a loyal guy, to be honest with you. He’s a good man, and I’m sure he’s going to fight for us very hard. I trust in his abilities. There’s been no finer Vice President than Bernie. I really, truly believe that. I have faith in him.

It was going to be a rocky four years ahead for this precedented administration.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/tuff.jpg'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/tuff.jpg'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-05%20131341.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "What A Loser", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-27%20150131.png", "EqFQ61lX528" ); } } else if (e.final_overall_results[2].candidate === 301 && e.final_overall_results[0].electoral_votes < 270 && uniparty == 3) { e.pages.push(`

FLOPULISTS COME THIRD IN DEADLOCKED ELECTION - TWO-PARTY SYSTEM VINDICATED?

Despondent. How could this have happened? They were set to win, or come second at the minimum. The crooked media, it was their fault. Or was it the two parties bullying the upcoming one? Or was it a lack of funding? Maybe a diminished spirit? It was hard to tell, and even when the mudslinging was over and the election was heading into full gear, no one was certain who was going to end up on top. Democrats, on the betting markets, had 50% chance of becoming first, while the Republicans were on 35%. Rebirth was just on 15%. The people who put money on the line, their reputations at stake, were right. Rebirth, while making a significant impact on the Electoral College, were still a dud. The hype, the promise, the momentum, it was all behind them, and yet, they had failed to secure the top two. It was a shambles. President Trump saw it as a disgrace. His legacy, his tenure as President, would be reduced to this. Forever a laughing stock. Vice President Sanders didn’t fare much better.

Anger swelled up within Donald Trump. How could he be belittled like this? Such an insignificant result, he thought. He thought he was better than this. Anger was just the beginning. Jealously soon set in. How did Buttigieg, let alone Haley, beat him out? How did a man who barely had any titles under his belt, and a neo-con, beat him? He was enraged. Jealous. He wanted revenge. Retribution. There was no way this could have happened. It was just the beginning stages of grief.

Bernie was handling it much better than his superior. He had expected this. All his life, he always knew he was the underdog, but he never gave up hope. He fought, even against all odds, for what he believed was right. He fought for justice. He thinks back to his activist days, when Civil Rights was just the leading topic of discussion and debate within America. He thinks back to his Mayoral race in Burlington. He then turns back to 4 years ago, and the situation he faced then. He had to work with a man who he had fought for a better part of a year. And look what happened, just a few short years later. They had put aside differences for the greater good. It didn’t matter if this was the result they wanted or not, they did something remarkable. It was just a shame Donald couldn’t see it yet.

“I may not be at my best right now…”

Bernie was sat down, looking at the TV. His face perplexed with all sorts of emotions.

“Bernie, look, we should’ve fought harder. We did fight hard, frankly, but we didn’t get our base, our patriots, they didn’t turn out. It doesn’t help- the fact that- Republicans and Democrats, they wanted us gone frankly. Sick, sick people, they ought to have some shame. We’ll be back soon, roaring back, trust me Bernie, it’ll happen.”

Trump was still in this race. He wasn’t about to give up. But Sanders? He couldn’t be nominated. He knew that. Trump knew that. It was a bittersweet moment.

`); e.pages.push(`

DEMOCRATS VOW TO PUT BUTTIGIEG IN OFFICE - REPUBLICANS AND REBIRTH SILENT ON RESULTS

What more could be said? Rebirth simply didn’t have the numbers to put Donald Trump back in office. It was impossible. The same with the Republicans. They couldn’t admit that. Democrats had the numbers and the leverage to bring Buttigieg to the Presidency. The only way that Republicans (Rebirth, practically speaking, are out of this race) would have a hope of winning the Presidency is by getting Rebirth votes in the House and in the Senate. Both of which were incredibly unlikely. The Progressive Faction would rather vote for Buttigieg, and the America First Faction would rather, stubbornly, vote for Trump than Haley. Buttigieg was guaranteed the Presidency. It was now only a question of how big his margin would be.

Both President Trump and Governor Haley vowed to continue on the fight, regardless of who came out on top. Both repeated similar talking points, which included that the ‘fight was not over yet’ and that ‘votes were still to be counted’. Governor Haley was far more optimistic though, stating:

“I think we can get enough votes in the House to get me across the finish line. Everyone who knows me says that I’m a fighter, and that I don’t give up. That’s what I’m doing now. I’m fighting for every day Americans and their needs, and I won’t quit on them now. Buttigieg has the advantage, there’s no denying that, but I’m going to put up one hell of a fight and ensure that I will become President of the United States.

Donald Trumps response was rather muted however, posting on Twitter

“Look, we’re in this to win this, okay? NIKKI “BIRDBRAIN” HALEY HAS NO CHANCE. ALFRED HAS A SLIGHTLY BETTER CHANCE, BUT STILL VERY WEAK. I AM PRESIDENT OF THE UNITED STATES! Me and Bernie, we’re a TREMENDOUS team, we’re going to continue fighting DAY AND NIGHT for the American people. These crooks in the media, they don’t report it, but they know, and I think THE AMERICAN PUBLIC DESERVE TO KNOW THE TRUTH. MAFA!”

`); e.pages.push(`

SCOTLAND BREAKS FREE FROM UNITED KINGDOM - FORMER PRESIDENT TRUMP MOVES TO SCOTLAND

After Scotland broke away from the United Kingdom in 2029, in what was considered a very surprising move, former President Donald Trump officially decided to reside and live in Scotland, rather than in the United States of America. The former President defended his recent actions after receiving backlash from many conservative influencers, stating that ‘his mother was from Scotland’ and that ‘he has many ties to the area’. In his full statement, he wrote:

“I love Scotland, I think it’s my second home to be quite honest with you, I mean, my mother was originally from Scotland, as you know, and uh, I get along with the Scottish people just fine. I will always love America, truly, I do, but I think in my life now, I need a break. I’ve been active for 50, 60, 70 years, and people don’t really appreciate that, y’know? I’ve got so many connections, so many links with this part of the world. I think, uh, I think I’ll become a citizen.”

Many Americans consider this especially out of the blue for the 2-term President, who had recently presided over a rebounding economy and foreign threats rescinding from America as a whole. Whether it was the re-taking of Crimea in the Black Sea from the Russians, or the reunification process between North and South Korea, both Donald Trump and current President Bernie Sanders have notched many foreign policy wins.

President Sanders has refused to comment on the situation.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20181006.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-05%20132514.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/scotland.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "What A Loser", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-27%20150131.png", "EqFQ61lX528" ); } } else if (e.final_overall_results[0].candidate === 301 && e.final_overall_results[0].electoral_votes == 269 && uniparty == 3 ) { e.pages.push(`

REBIRTH ONE VOTE AWAY FROM PRESIDENCY - COUNTRY IN DEEP TURMOIL OVER CONFUSING RESULTS

Livid. So close, and yet so far from victory. Donald was angry. Why didn’t all of Maine go his way? Why didn’t Nebraska? Why didn’t these swing states vote for him? Was he the issue facing Americans? They were so close to giving him an awesome mandate - one which would have transformed American politics. Buttigieg and Haley were fighting for the remaining scraps. Trump didn’t care. All he wanted was that one vote that could change American history forever.

Bernie sat beside him, looking at the map, then back at Trump, then back at the map again. It was unbelievable. How could this have happened? If a few hundred votes swung one way or another, they’d be comfortable enough to know that they could have had a mandate to carry on their vision for the country. Bernie didn’t want to face reality, just as much as Trump did. It was sort of a heartbreaking result for them.

But.

But there was hope. Faithless Electors. It could- it would work. Trump remembered that these sorts of people exist, and could very easily be manipulated. That’s what he had in his mind, anyways. He dare not say it aloud. If he did, well, Bernie would be right up his ass. He had to play it smart. Cool. Precise.

Bernie knew what Trump was thinking. It was obvious, but he didn’t speak up. He knew, in his lifetime, this would be once in a generation. He knew it went against his moral convictions. He knew it went against what he stood for, but he also saw the opportunity. He saw what could be done.

“Bernie, look, I think we have a real shot, I mean, we’re one off, right? We can very easily win this thing. Clearly, people put trust in us, our movement, I think we can do it, right? People won’t complain, look- our people, patriots, the real winners, they’ll cheer us on no matter what, okay? We both know, frankly, what needs to be done. I, Donald J Trump, will be the one to fix this, and these losers and suckers in the crooked media, the establishment, they won’t stand a chance, trust me.”

Bernie stood up, walked up to Donald, and shook his hand. Right then, a silent deal was made. It was time to pull in a few favours.

`); e.pages.push(`

TRUMP/SANDERS REACH 272, GAINS 3 FAITHLESS ELECTORS FROM CALIFORNIA.

A mandate. Or that’s what Trump was saying, anyways. Bernie was mute. He couldn’t tell his base that they had won through a technicality. Trump didn’t care though. He openly bragged about it, saying:

“People are saying these electors are ‘FAITHLESS’? It couldn’t be further from the TRUTH. They put their FAITH in me, your favourite President, DONALD J TRUMP, and their favourite VP, BERNARD SANDERS! I’d call that a very big LEAP OF FAITH, and frankly, anyone saying otherwise BELIEVE THE CROOKED MEDIA LIES!”

Haley, Buttigieg, the irrelevant ‘third parties’, they all whined, they moaned, they cried about how the rules weren’t fair. They finally had a taste of their own medicine. Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders, two of the biggest political juggernauts in American politics, had destroyed the American duopoly on politics, all through a technicality. Twice. Everyone said 2016 was a fluke, that those types of results wouldn’t happen again. But they were proven deadly wrong. Four years later, it happened, again, but only this time Trump had a plan. Technicality or not, they had won the most Electoral Votes regardless, and commanded a large section of States, and therefore, the country.

Already both Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders have decided to go full force with existing policies that they had campaigned on, which included rehabilitation, focusing on justice for people unfairly prosecuted, and a reinvigoration of COVID-19 policies. Both men have also confirmed that many of the cabinet positions filled now will continue to remain the same, with some set to apparently be shuffled around and moved to different departments.

`); e.pages.push(`

REBIRTH DO POORLY IN MIDTERM ELECTIONS - REPUBLICANS GAIN GROUND AS DEMOCRATS FALTER.

With the Midterm elections out the way and certified, who are the real Winners and Losers of these results?

Coming in first place, Republicans have successfully rebounded their popularity after catastrophic results from the previous General Election back in 2020, under Nikki Haley. This newfound popularity coincides with a lack of real policy wins and a culmination of underwater economic results for the Rebirth Party, led by President Trump. This, along with Democratic leadership having several major gaffes throughout the campaign, including calling Republican-leaning voters “Bigots, sexists and disgusting people” and a lack of enthusiasm for their base, who seem deflated after recent defections from Democratic to Rebirth.

In second place, comes the Rebirth Party. While they did pick up a handful of seats within the Senate and 60 seats in the House, they vastly underperformed expectations, considering the momentum that was following Rebirth after the 2020 ‘victory’. Technicality or not, they had persuaded many Americans that what they did was ‘necessary for the stability of the country’ and to ensure ‘steady, firm leadership in the face of global extremism’. While the majority of the American public believe the economy is rebounding (51% to 42%), the majority believe that the recovery, overall, is beginning to slow down, which has affected recent inflation figures, which now stand at 4.5%, compared to 2% at the same time in 2019.

In last place, the Democratic Party. The Democratic Party has significantly lost it’s footing, with multiple defections, serious gaffes on The Campaign Trail, and a lack of any policy initiatives that they have proposed while in Congress. This lack of pushing for any real significant change upset many core demographics Democrats used to heavily rely on. For example, much of the African-American community, frustrated with Democrats lack of progress on justice and civil reform, have bolted to both the Republican and Rebirth Platforms. While they still hold a big portion of the African-American vote (45%), Rebirth (32%) and Republicans (23%) are catching up significantly. This is the same with the Latino vote, which now favour the Republicans significantly over their social conservative stances.

Current House Standing:

Republicans: 174 House Seats

Rebirth: 158 House Seats

Democratic: 103 House Seats

Current Senate Standing:

Republicans: 35 Senate Seats

Democratic: 37 Senate Seats

Rebirth: 28 Senate Seats (+VP)

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20204830.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20204830.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-05%20140636.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Night Of Fire", "Niko", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20193642.png", "SRbhLtjOiRc" ); } } //killary and Phil endings if (quickstats[0] > 535 && uniparty == 1) { e.pages.push(`
Archive of Our Own
A Billionaire, A Revolutionary
by Big Toni
Rating:General Audiences Pairing:Sanders/Trump Category:Romance

After a long and hard campaign trail victory has been achieved, and the knight in shining armor, Donald, and helpless princess, Bernie go back to the castle, the White House, to celebrate winning the election together. It wasn’t an easy victory, no, some would say it was a pyrrhic victory, for the pair had realized many things about each other, and truth be told, they were happy as ever to have learned so much about themselves in the process.

As they’re both driven to the White House, Bernie and Donald sit side by side from each other. They were laughing and chatting about all the funny hijinks the two had gotten up to with each other, Covid had brought them both down, it hit the economy so hard, and a lot of lives were lost from it, so the laughter the two shared with each other served as a good respite from all that mess

As the two were laughing, Bernie sat his hand down on the arm rest, and Donald, while not looking where he was going to put his hand down to rest, accidently touched Bernie’s hand. The car was dead silent, and the two looked at each other for a fleeting moment, and looked away, and the two blushed hard. “S-sorry” Donald said, sheepishly, not even managing to look at Bernie. The blush on two faces remained, stagnant, and everlasting.

The driver in the front of the car asked if everything was alright, and two quickly said yes, and the rest of the ride was ridden in silence. When the two reached the White House, they quickly exited the vehicle, and the rest of the night was a blur. The two slept in separate rooms, in separate halls, and the two had separate hearts, and they both woke up to a peaceful morning, a peaceful sky.

As the sun was rising in the sky, Donald walked up to the balcony, and faced the sun. While looking at the beautiful sun, he got a cigar and lit it and started smoking it. He enjoyed the amazing view of the Washington monument, and watched people on their morning jogs in D.C.

He remembers visiting it on the campaign trail, he giggles to himself about that wasted day. As he was enjoying the view, Donald heard a familiar voice, Bernie’s voice

Donald almost drops his cigar, he was scared, he didn’t want to face that man since the incident in the car, but now he has to face him, now he has to face the biggest challenge he has ever faced in his political career. “Donald.” That voice, it came from a Vermonter, from a man raised thousands of miles away from where the two are now today. “Donald”. The voice is getting louder, his heart is pounding. Bernie’s heart is also pounding, the two hearts are at risk of ripping from both of the men’s chest and embracing into one. “Donald!”. He can’t do it, he can’t face him. “DONALD!”. “I LOVE YOU!” Donald says, shouting with all of his might, the power of which shocked the two. “I love you two, Donald.” Donald drops his cigar, and the two embrace, the two hearts embrace, the billionaire and a revolutionary, at last, unite.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/bro.gif'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Y.M.C.A", "Village People", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/4/4c/Cruisin1978.jpg", "khOFB1Erzdg" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 269 && quickstats[0] < 535 && uniparty == 1) { e.pages.push(`

REBIRTH BEAT BACK CLINTON AND SCOTT IN AN EPIC UPSET OF THE DECADE - POPULISM VINDICATED IN AMERICA?

Victory. It never tasted so good. Hillary? Destroyed. Scott? Annihilated. They had no idea what hit them. The phonies, the frauds, they all cast doubt on Rebirth, they all cast doubt on their success. They all laughed at them. Well, they weren’t laughing now, were they? Both Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders, two political heavyweights, were vindicated. So-called ‘CROOKED HILLARY’ and ‘SLOPPY SCOTT’ couldn't challenge them anymore. They had reached the end of the road, the light had faded.

Rebirth’s vision for America, one of triumph, one of fairness, one of equality, it had prevailed. The Democrats had failed to capture the imagination of the public, and the Republicans were irrelevant. It didn’t matter now. What mattered now was how Trump and Sanders could command this amazing mandate for America. They had to make sure they did what they had promised to do. They had to do what was right, for the American people. Hillary can cry wolf, and so can Scott, but they know full well that they had political capital to spend. Healthcare, Immigration, Foreign Policy, it all had to be done.

The fight, for America, had only just begun. The past 4 years were full of chaos, full of damage for public trust, but now, it was time to bring work for the public. They needed stability. They needed trust. America decided to put that stability, to put that strength, within an insurgent third party. It was unprecedented. It was unparalleled. It had to be done.

“We did it Bernie, we did it, we finally beat back the establishment. We faced loss after loss, and many wins, but we did it. Hillary Clinton, the crooked media, the lies, Phil Scott, they had no idea what hit em’. I mean, look what happened, right? They like to think of themselves as God’s, as the bringers of so-called ‘truth’, well, we showed them, and we brought a victory for the ages. For once in our lives, we got a break. These people, they have no idea what we’re going to unleash. We’re going to PUT AMERICA FIRST!”

Bernie was ecstatic. He finally had something to work on. No more were the days of senseless Senate Committees, or the days of bills being shot down. This was his moment just as much as it was Donald’s. He had fought for this, and he deserved it.

“I know Donald, I know, we won. The people of this country, the majority of them, anyways, they put their faith in our vision, our vision for America. We fought for their rights. We saw it all across America. You heard them. All these people, who lived in terror from the establishment, who lived in fear of what they may bring, they saw what we could accomplished, what we promised. You’re right, the top 1%, they see themselves as superior. They feed on all the real people, the working-class, those who give the most to our country. I deeply care for them. We’ve both lived long lives, and we’ve seen a few things. I’ve seen what’s happened to our country the past 50 years. It’s a sham. We’re going to do what we said. We’re going to bring America back.”

They knew what had to be done. Victory. They knew what was coming for the establishment. They could cry all they want, they could fight it, they could try and prevent change. But change, for Rebirth, they know- they know it’s inevitable.

Fight for what is right.

`); e.pages.push(`

SCOTT CONCEDES WHILE CLINTON CALLS FOR ‘ELECTION INTEGRITY’, BLAMES RUSSIA FOR REBIRTH WIN

Phil Scott, the eager collaborator of the Establishment, the man who sought to bring back the Liberal Republicans. All for naught. He was a decent enough man, a family man, a man who had brought Republicanism back to Vermont after many years. He knew what to say. He knew what policies they enjoyed. Was it any wonder he did so well in many “liberal” states? Is it any wonder he ‘stole’ votes from Hillary? She knew, just as much as anyone else, that Scott was fighting for the same votes as she was. She was angry. Scott? He knew what had happened. He conceded.

“My fellow Republicans, I am, uh, sorry that we did not win this race for us. Me and Charlie Baker gave it our all, our finest, but we came up short. I congratulate President Trump and Vice President Sanders on their win tonight, and my thoughts and wishes are with them as they continue to lead America for the next 4 years.”

He paused. He couldn’t really believe it. But he had to do this, for his country. His honour was on the line.

“I, well, we uh, we put up a good campaign, and I had called them a few hours before this, and it was a good, productive conversation. They’re both, well-intentioned individuals. I have the upmost faith that they can succeed for the American people.”

That cackling laugh. It could be heard for miles.

“Well, to be completely fair with you, the public, I think Bernie and trump got the Russians involved again. You have to look at these states, especially in the north-east, the rustbelt. It was, well, uh, stolen. They rigged it against me because they knew I would win. I know this. We’re going to fight these results. Putin must be laughing in the Kremlin. They’re, uh, Russian agents, Bernie and Trump, I know it. I know them both- look, it’s true, the evidence, it’s all there. ”

It was difficult not to laugh. Seriously? She was rejected 4 times. FOUR whole times she was rejected by the public, but she didn’t mind the humiliation ritual. That’s what went through Trump and Bernie's mind, anyways.

Rebirth won. She couldn’t do anything about it.

`); e.pages.push(`

CLINTON ON TRIAL FOR INCITEMENT OF VIOLENCE AND INSURRECTION, PLEDGES TO RUN FOR THE DEM NOMINATION FOR THE 4TH TIME

After January 6th, 2021, Democratic nominee Hillary Clinton, who had claimed that the 2020 Presidential Election was ‘fraudulent’ and ‘rigged’, had led an armed insurrection against Congress to block the certification of results for the Presidency/Vice Presidency of the United States. After the abysmal failure of such attempts, and a huge boost of approvals for Rebirth following the incident, Hillary Clinton was prosecuted by the DOJ for incitement of violence and intent to insurrect the current government of the United States.

After many of these indictments of the former Secretary of State and the following ‘infamous’ picture of her mugshot in late 2023, she had formally decided that she would run for the Democratic nomination for the 4th time, and seek to become the first convicted felon President.

“The fight, it isn’t over yet. That glass ceiling has yet to be shattered, and I intend to become the first female President of these United States. But- our country is more divided than ever. Both Trump and Sanders, who are the real deep state agents, they have unfairly and unjustly prosecuted me. It’s a disgrace. We’re going to fight this, and we’re going to win. I don’t care who I face, whether it’s Sanders or anyone else, just know this America, we will win, and we will take our country from those who seek to divide it.”

However, this will be a very difficult challenge for Clinton, as many Democratic politicians want to distance themselves from her, with many governors entering the race, such as JB Pritzker of Illinois, Gretchen Whitmer of Michigan and Wes Moore of Maryland. Few have decided to stick with the former First Lady, however notable names include Gavin Newsom of California and Chuck Schumer, Senator for New York.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/tuff.jpg'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-05%20142536.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-05%20142750.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Sweeter Dreams", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20183108.png", "ijSmOtBiyeY" ); } } else if (e.final_overall_results[0].candidate === 50000 && e.final_overall_results[0].electoral_votes > 269 && uniparty == 1) { e.pages.push(`

REPUBLICANS AND REBIRTH REBUKED AS CLINTON CLINCHES TOP JOB - STATUS-QUO PREVAILS OVER POPULISM?

Fear. That’s what Trump and Sanders felt. Fear. They both knew Hillary well. One of them faced Hillary in the general election, and knew her as a friend. She went to Donald’s wedding, Donald knew Bill Clinton. They had gotten to know each other quite well before he entered politics. The other faced her in the primary season, and was her Vice Presidential running mate. How big of a mistake for her that was. Now, she had to face both of her former ‘friends’ and ‘rivals’. It was almost surreal. All these people, from the same area, all relatively close to one another. It was a messy affair.

Both men knew what would happen. She wanted revenge. That’s why she ran. It wasn’t to do it for the betterment of the country, it wasn’t out of some blind loyalty to America. She wanted retribution against her loss 4 years ago. You could see it in her eyes. It was…worrying.

“Bernie, we have to realise, she doesn’t want what’s best for America, she doesn’t know what it’s like, y’know? She doesn’t know our patriots like you or me- she’s a fraud, plain and simple. I know you worked with her, but that was a pity prize, you didn’t deserve that sort of treatment, frankly. She cheated you out of it, to be hones with you, and, well, she probably cheated now. Whether it was Russia, China or anybody else, I don’t know, but she’s a crook, plain and simple.”

Bernie, for the most part, agreed. He was awarded a pity prize for basically submitting to a dying establishment. Justice wasn’t allowed in the DNC. You could play fair, you could even win a few primaries, but the result was the same - a coronation. The last ‘real’ primary was 2008, and Bernie knew what happened. Hillary lost. So it didn’t seem entirely lost on him, that, potentially, she could have rigged this in favour of her, just like in 2016.

“I don’t know- Donald, I just don’t know…I think, well, she could very well have, but our country, our democracy. I don’t want to go down this dark road. I think we need to consult legal experts, and see any route. I don’t want to challenge this outright just yet. It seems- it seems premature. Can we at least do what I suggest first?”

Donald gave it some thought, and realised that Bernie was probably right.

No call, not now. Now was the time to plan ahead. The country depended on it.

`); e.pages.push(`

DEMOCRATS HOLD PLURALITY IN THE HOUSE AND IN THE SENATE - CLINTON OFFERS CABINET POSITIONS TO REPUBLICANS FOR CONCESSIONS

She knew what she was doing. Republicans in cabinet with a Democratic President? It wasn’t unheard of, per say, but it was highly irregular. She needed all the help she could get in order to block any Rebirth momentum going forward, even if it meant betraying some of her ideals to stop them. This was normal Washington politics. You take some, you give some, and you win some more. Whether it was the Department of Homeland Security, or Immigration services, whatever she could give out, she did. Scott was excluded. As much as his influence was now a part of the Republican Party, she had to block him out so that any deal that would be made would leave her in a more powerful position.

Americans could cry about ‘the Establishment’ all they want, but she knew, along with Republicans, that they could keep them safe. That’s why the PATRIOT ACT was enacted, that’s why they went to war in Iraq and Afghanistan. They knew what they were doing, the public just had to trust them.
