With Democrats securing 175 seats in the House of Representatives and 39 in the Senate, this would enable them to actively get agenda passed through, with some negotiations by Republican colleagues, and effectively pass through any agenda the President-Elect wishes.

However, it is not all plain sailing, as Republicans and Rebirth will push to get their own agenda through, especially before the critical 2022 midterms, which already seem to be gearing up as both President Trump and VP Sanders vow to ‘continue the fight, whatever the cost’ and that Hillary Clinton is a ‘fraud, con-artist and a choke’.

`); e.pages.push(`

Why the Iran war is good, actually. Analysis on how America is coming out on top after a rough few years.

December 13th, 2024.

After the Clinton administration decided to embark on an invasion of the Republic of Iran in late 2023, they have struggled to win crucial support for their war against the Iranian regime and against it’s proxies. However, things do seem to be turning for the defeated administration and what it wants to accomplish within the region.

At the start of the war, many American lives and servicemen were lost within the hills and deserts of Persia, suffering heavy attritional losses as Iran braced for what it deemed a ‘Holy War Against Imperialism’. While this propaganda worked on the Iranian people for a time, soon, they realised that freedom, and American freedom at that, would help liberate the Persian people and establish a secular Republic, built on justice, liberty and the freedoms of every western nation.

Coupled with civil unrest, a lack of a proper army and low morale, the Iranian regime fell on November 18th, 2024. This led to the rise of the Persian Republic, the sole successor of the previous regime, and which had already firmly backed American ties in the region. While there are small insurgency groups, they remain localised and lack any sort of air to breathe.

This, coupled with a bolstered alliance within the Middle East with the signing of the Jerusalem Pact between Israel, the United States and Saudi Arabia, would push Iran further to it’s limits, and as such, weakened their international standing. With Russia occupied with internal strife and China backing down from international disputes, this led the Clinton administration to pick up some big foreign policy wins in early 2025, just after they had lost a crucial presidential election to Rebirth candidates Josh Hawley and RFK Jr. These firmly anti-war candidates sought to destroy any progress made by the previous administration, and as such, remove years of freedoms and progress made by Clinton and her team of specialists.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-05%20151318.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-05%20152017.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-05%20152445.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "For Whom The Bell Tolls", "Metallica", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/f/f4/Ridetl.png", "B_HSa1dEL9s" ); } } else if (e.final_overall_results[0].candidate === 50002 && e.final_overall_results[0].electoral_votes > 269 && uniparty == 1) { e.pages.push(`

REPUBLICANS EDGE OUT A WIN IN THE ELECTORAL COLLEGE - TRUMP AND CLINTON CLAIM FOUL PLAY.

Surprise. How did this happen? How did Phil Scott, the Governor of one of the smallest states in the Union, somehow win the Presidency? It was unreal. Trump and Bernie couldn’t believe it. Hillary was fuming. Yet again, her coronation, her Presidency, was stolen by Republicans. It deeply hurt her. How could this have happened?

Donald and Bernie were told well into the night that a deadlock was going to happen, that, against the odds, they could probably eke out a win somewhere. But their projections were wrong. Whether it was the mail-in ballots or the so-called ‘secret Republican vote’, Phil Scott had just become the most powerful man on earth. Donald wasn’t happy with this. He was quite angry, in fact. How could he lose to this little man? There was no such thing as a ‘Liberal Republican’. There were Republicans, and then there were RINOs (Republicans In Name Only). Of course, that never mattered to Trump ever since Rebirth, but to lose to who he saw as a RINO, was an insult to him and to his ego. He couldn’t let that stand. Neither could Hillary.

It was now left to Bernie to be the ‘middle man’, something which he found to be impossibly difficult. A principled man, one of integrity, honour, respect and duty to his country. He was stuck between a rock and a hard place. He saw Hillary as an opportunist, someone who was willing to say or do anything to achieve power. While he warmed up to Donald, and saw him as a dear friend, the flaws were still clear as day. It was time to do the talking.

“Donald, look, I know you’re, uh, angry. I know the feeling- but we can’t claim, y’know, this was rigged or anything. The country needs stability. America needs leadership. We still lead the helm for the next 3 or so months. Let’s not do anything rash, let’s focus on getting COVID-19 gone, and we need to ensure a peaceful transition of power takes pla-”

He was interrupted by the TV. Hillary came up on her stage in New York. Her eyes were tired, her demeanor was slouched. She looked like she hadn't slept in days.

“Thank you, thank you, to all my wonderful supporters. This fight, our election, it isn’t over yet. Republicans cheated last time, and they did so now. Phil Scott lost this election, and so did Donald…poor ego. Look- I mean, this is a win for the Democratic Party, and I fully intend to be the first female President of the United States. ONWARDS AND UPWARDS!”

Donald heard her loud and clear. He was fighting this just as much as she was. He was not going to let that so-called ‘nasty woman’ belittle him again.

`); e.pages.push(`

PRESIDENT-ELECT SCOTT, AND HOW LIBERAL REPUBLICANISM HAS MADE IT’S WAY TO THE PRESENT DAY

With Phil Scott’s improbable win for the White House in full swing, and both Rebirth and the Democrats retreating on their false claims of electoral fraud, how will Phil Scott govern? He made some significant promises to the electorate, which included amnesty for Illegal Aliens who had been in the country for more than 10 years, lowering the cost of healthcare through some sort of government program, and a renewed initiative to keep foreign relations stable. With these promises and a convincing win, he will now have to enact what many people see as Democrat, or in some cases, Rebirth-lite policies.

With this broad stroke of policy initiatives, he managed to galvanise many moderates, independents and many liberals who were frustrated with Bernie’s tack to the Trump administration and Clinton’s unwillingness to budge on certain key issues, and a lack of trust. This, along with a divided country, allowed Scott to push forward and unite many different groups to coalesce around his candidacy. Many reporters are saying that this coalition is unprecedented, especially given the lack of any strong ‘right’, which would have been almost a given for a Republican.

However, this sort of coalition had existed before, but in a much smaller capacity. During the 1950s and 1960s, there were multiple factions of the Republican Party. One of which, was the Liberal Republican. Rehabilitation. Civil Rights. Laissez faire economics.. Core Republican values that were abandoned by the New-Right in the 1980s and pushed themselves towards ardent social-conservatism and religious rhetoric.

With a Trump/Sanders administration being firmly populist in it’s rhetoric, with elements of both progressivism and Clinton running a more traditional campaign, it allowed both Phil Scott and Charlie Baker to ‘retake’ the Republican Party from one of New Right Conservatism to a more Liberal Establishment path.

Time will tell whether or not this will last with them, or carry on well into the future, but America has transformed into another party system.

`); e.pages.push(`

ScottCare, or scotfree? How President Scott’s healthcare plan sounds awfully familiar…

The Care & Consumer Protection Act, or more commonly known as ScottCare, has recently passed both Chambers of Congress, and is awaiting signature by President Scott. However, many of their own supporters are calling out the administration over ‘copying someone else's homework’, in reference to what both Trump/Sanders had wanted to do during the campaign for President in 2020. Trump, during the campaign, called for more universal healthcare for Americans and for it to be sponsored by the American Government. This came after negotiations by both Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders, who had varying degrees of views on the matter.

This allowed for Scott to point out the divisions, take the ‘high road’ and as he won the election, he had a mandate to push through his own healthcare agenda plan. With this in mind, he had used the previous framework of both Obama’s Healthcare Affordability Act, along with Rebirth’s plans for healthcare, to try and get a possible solution to the healthcare crisis that had encompassed America during the COVID-19 pandemic.

ScottCare includes many provisions for prescription affordability, pushing for more regulations on pharmaceutical companies, and a push for investment into new hospitals and screening for all sorts of diseases. President Scott has also pushed for increased state budgets with healthcare and is giving further funding for states on the whole.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-21%20163935.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-21%20163935.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-21%20163935.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Let's Go Away", "SEGA Sound Team", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/4/4e/Daytona_USA_screenshot.png", "YVlcADaT94o" ); } } else if (e.final_overall_results[0].candidate === 301 && e.final_overall_results[0].electoral_votes < 269 && uniparty == 1) { e.pages.push(`

REBIRTH CELEBRATE AS THEY TAKE FIRST PLACE, BATTLE FOR THE WHITE HOUSE IS NOT OVER YET.

Shock and Awe. Rebirth, which once started as an insurgent populist movement led by Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders, had successfully deadlocked the Electoral College,forcing it to go into a contingent election. Even more surprisingly, they came in first place. Both men were ecstatic. They certainly weren’t expecting this, let alone coming close to the top of the line. It was a miracle. Did God intervene on their behalf? Feelings of relief swept over them. It was amazing to achieve such a historic result in just under 2 years. They both felt, at the very least, proud of what they had accomplished. America saw their potential, their firebrand of politics, and decided that this was a political force here to stay. The Establishment, which seemed on course for an amazing landslide in 2020, now seemed weaker than ever before. For once in many people’s lives, they could have a fighting chance against what they saw as a corrupt and elitist establishment. Late into the night, as congressional returns were coming in, Rebirth was doing far better than anyone had anticipated. Donald and Bernie gave each other a look as they saw the announcement that the Rebirth Party had just passed over 150 seats in the House, and 15 Senate seats. They knew what they were doing. They had hope of a brighter tomorrow. They knew what they had accomplished. While it wasn’t an outright victory, there was HOPE. Bernie hadn’t felt this way since his first ever win back in Burlington, Vermont. Trump was his usual self, but yet felt real pride at this very moment. They knew now that they had to carry on the fight. They had created a movement which would outlive them.

However, it was short lived. They soon realised that they did not have over the finish line. They did well, undoubtedly so, but they did not get the 270 votes required. The fight for the White House & the Naval Observatory was going to be a long one. A gruesome one. One that wouldn’t see any truces. Appreciation of the results had mellowed out into pessimism. They could look at coalitions, or some sort of bargaining with different parties, but it seemed just as likely that the other 2 parties would rather work with each other than Rebirth. Republicans now saw Trump as a weakling in the face of a Democratic Socialist- a ‘social justice warrior’, while Bernie Sanders was being grilled over his newfound ties to Trump and his ‘oligarch’. It was highly unlikely any sort of deal would get passed through.

It was difficult to accept, but they had to endure it. They put up an amazing fight, they put up an insurgent campaign, and defied the odds once more. But now, it was time to fight well into December and January. Hopefully, anyways…

“We fought Bernie, we fought tremendously well, some are saying we’re the most successful third party, maybe ever? Listen- those crooks, they couldn’t stand a chance, and yet, the system still favours them so much, it’s so sad, so terrible, we should’ve campaigned on abolishing this frankly awful system of governance. From being way down in the polls, to way up, in such a short amount of time, I mean, frankly, no one’s been able to do it before, y’know?”

Bernie nodded along. Their political journeys, from opposite ends of the political spectrum, ended up together, as one, united behind a common cause.

“I agree Donald. I’ve called for the abolition of the Electoral College for years, and yet my former Democrat colleagues were too scared of real change. It saddened me quite a bit. I, uh, well, we fought for change, and we might still get it, but we have to remain tough, firm and vigilant. I’ve seen what the establishment can do- I mean, look at wikileaks, the DNC, the pushback on our agenda. It worries me greatly that ‘dark money’ can still hold such an influence.”

They both carried on their conversation, until they had realised something.

Neither Scott nor Hillary called. Something was up. Something sketchy.

`); e.pages.push(`

CLINTON OFFERS SCOTT VP POSITION FOR VOTES - SCOTT TO MAKE ANNOUNCEMENT SHORTLY

“Yesterday, Hillary Clinton called me to make an offer. I give Republican votes in the Electoral College, in exchange to be Vice President. This was not an easy decision to make, believe me, but I have done what is in the best interest for the United States of America. I have decided to accept her deal. She is-”

Donald turned off the TV.

What had just happened? American politics was forever changed. This was something new. Something different. Donald was fuming with rage. He saw it coming, and yet, he was powerless to do anything about it. There was no way he was backing down without a fight. He was ready to go in against them both. He was ready for another fight.

“What? Are you fucking serious? No- no, I don’t believe it. They truly have sold their souls. I told you Bernie, I said it, they’re crooks, frauds, they’ll do anything for themselves, I mean, look, can you imagine President Clinton? With VP Scott? It makes me sick, frankly I am DISGUSTED in what they have done. I said that they’d do it.”

Unbelievable. Donald had expected it. Bernie was blindsided. Were they really that terrified of taking it to Congress? Were they afraid of losing? Regardless of whether or not the President expected it, he was boiling with rage. He thought 2016 was bad, with the stitch-up with Bernard (back then, anyways), but this was a new low.

“Can you see Hillary working with Congress? Hell- could you see her work with anyone at all? It’s a disgrace Bernard, a disgrace, she has no shame. I said 4 years ago she was a nasty woman, a no good rotten person, and now she has the keys basically. I see it now.”

Bernie was still taking it all in. He looked at the map, then the TV, and then the map again. There really wasn’t a path for victory for Rebirth, even with the recounts they would ask for.

“It’s a lot to take in, Donald. I know. We need to figure out something soon. But now we know, they’ll do, uh, anything for votes. We’re going to rally the nation, and we’re going to fight. I’m with you 100%. We’re going to bring our case to the American people. It’s time they know they have politicians on their side rather than those who fight for themselves. It’s time.”

It was going to be another 4 long years. The Republicrats, as Donald called them, would now begin to replant their roots back in Washington. But Rebirth would always be there now. To weed them out, to take out the ‘deep-seeded’ problems of the Washington elite and provide a much needed check. It was time to fight.

`); e.pages.push(`

How the Democratic-Republican alliance has shaped American politics so far.

The Democratic-Republican Alliance, more commonly known as ‘D-R’ or the Republicrats, have successfully held onto both the House & the Senate, and rebuked the supposed ‘purple wave’ that was meant to hit them in 2022. With 230 seats in the House and 55 seats in the Senate, they have the ability to produce major pieces of legislation with little opposition from the Rebirth Party.

With a new, more ‘centrist’ focused administration coming from President Clinton and Vice President Scott, they have successfully managed to recapture the American voter into trusting in their vision for the country. This comes amidst a new wave of populism across the wider globe, specifically in France, Germany, Japan and the United Kingdom, with each country either having a Majority Populist government, or a Right-leaning government.

This has increasingly isolated America from its international partners, and as such, they have begun to make a significant effort to try and bridge whatever divides they may have with foreign powers. Whether this was done through doing separate trade deals or forming a spearhead against aggressive nations, the Republicrats are trying their best to stave off any further Populist encroachments back into government.

Policy differences that once disunited both Democrats and Republicans now find themselves agreeing with one another to stop any sort of momentum for Former President Trump’s and Former Vice President Sanders’ party, and ultimately, try to remove any chance of them running again in 2024, with their approvals reaching new heights as the economy begins to slow down from inflation. This can be seen with the policy in Ukraine and certain economic policies. These include giving massive funding for Ukraine in its defensive war against Belarus and Russia, along with supporting a strengthened NATO, which had its relationship damaged during the last administration. This, coupled with a free-market approach to the economy means that, for now, the Republicrats are sticking together for the foreseeable future.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/tuff.jpg'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/tuff.jpg'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aZyAKy2e7UEiSmg5/republicrats.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "What A Loser", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-27%20150131.png", "EqFQ61lX528" ); } } else if (e.final_overall_results[1].candidate === 301 && e.final_overall_results[0].electoral_votes < 270 && uniparty == 1) { e.pages.push(`

REBIRTH MANAGE SECOND PLACE AS THEY OUTPERFORM EXPECTATIONS - TRUMP CLAIMS ESTABLISHMENT STITCH-UP IS IN THE WORKS

The expected result, but a proud one. They had managed to capture a significant chunk of the Electoral College, and a sizable portion of the vote, but they just couldn’t get the numbers for it. It was amazing they had even made it this far, it was as Donald put it, ‘tremendous’. Bernie was jumping for joy, this is what he had expected. Sure, it wasn’t an outright win, nor was it any real progress to first place, but he was happy with it. It was glorious. What was an insurgent political force was now a real alternative for America. It proved that, with time, effort and a little bit of luck, anything was possible. This result proved that. America had stability with the 2-party system, had stable policy decisions and an electorate which was comfortable with itself. Now, they had choices, and the Establishment was scared of that. They had achieved what was considered impossible. They brought America from the brink of indifference and brought a fervor which couldn’t be removed.

Their struggle was finally over. Who cared if Clinton came third, or Scott came first, or vice versa. What mattered now was that their flag was firmly planted on the political landscape. Whether it was in the Plains, whether it was in the Midwest, whether it was in New England or wherever else, they knew, without any uncertainty, that they were going to continue to be a force. They knew, despite all the abuse that they had received, all the threats, all the political rhetoric and barbs, they could put their heads above their shoulders and say they fought for what they believed in. They fought for what was right. They knew that they gave it their all, and they were immensely proud.

Both men realised what had happened. A political fight had just culminated, and ensured that their legacy would not be forgotten. It didn’t matter now if Scott or Clinton won the election out-right, they were happy knowing that this administration left its mark on America.

Donald looked to Bernie, and they both gave each other a handshake and a hug.

“We fought well Bernie, we did very well, maybe we could’ve done better, who knows, but we brought out the biggest threat to the Establishment that hasn’t been seen since Teddy Roosevelt. These politicians, grifters I call em’, they haven’t seen the end of us yet Bernie, just look at these results, I’m proud of it, to be honest with you. They had no idea what hit them. Doesn’t matter who came first, honestly, they’re the same party if we’re being honest. What we did this night will be remembered in American history. We will be taught about in schools, we’ll be known as the people who stepped up and rose against the ROT.”

Bernie agreed. They both knew that what they had accomplished, together, was truly a remarkable feat. One that could not be replicated.

“I think, Mr President, the nation spoke, and it gave us an answer. They want an alternative. They want real change, I can’t blame them, you just have to look at the Midwest, which has been crying out for change for decades. You look at Obama’s win, you look at Reagan's win, and you see, time after time, they cried out for change, and they were abandoned. It’s sad.”

There was hope. A hope for a brighter America. Even if it was just a spark, a light at the end of the tunnel, it was there. A rejuvenated America. The fight for freedom was only just beginning.

`); e.pages.push(`

SCOTT UNITES WITH CLINTON, EASILY OVERPOWERING REBIRTH’S VOTE SHARE - TRUMP ANNOUNCES LEGAL CHALLENGES.

How could this have happened? Clinton, giving up her share of the pie, for what, some cabinet position? It was unlikely, but this is the world that they lived in. Trump was boiling with rage over this. Scott and Clinton sold out their principles for a chance at power again. It wasn’t happening. Surely, this was a nightmare, surely, this wasn’t real, right? How? Why? Was this some sort of trick?

“Bernie, no, I won’t let this happen, I mean, is this even legal? I can’t believe it. I really can’t. These people- crooks, they’ll cheat with just about anything. You look at Clinton, selling her soul to block us. This is a vendetta. We’re going to fight this Bernie, tremendously, otherwise we won’t have a country anymore. This isn’t legal, and even if it is, we’re going to make it illegal. We won’t be stopped by the Electoral College being rigged against us.”

Bernie was despondent. After all they had done, after all the effort, it was for nothing. The Establishment used its dirty tricks to win on what he saw as a parlor trick. He was just as dismayed as Donald was. He was angry. But what could he do now? He was going to support Trump with the legal proceedings, but, knowing their luck and political capital, it was going to be dragged through the courts with no end in sight.

“Donald, I agree. We’re going to fight this, and I think we should use every available avenue. I mean, it’s sad. I’ve called to abolish the Electoral College for years, and we should pursue that as well. I think it’s the right thing to do.”

The next few months were going to be a slog. They were going to be difficult, but in all their political fights, this was the one worth fighting for the most. The Establishment played all its tricks, all its whacky tools, but now, it was time for the real fight back. The people of America, who Trump and Sanders adored, would be with them. Or they would like to think that, anyways.

`); e.pages.push(`

BERNIE WINS THE 2024 PRESIDENTIAL ELECTION - SAYS THAT HE WILL ‘EMBRACE TRUMPS LEGACY’

November 8th, 2024

Bernie Sanders, Former Vice President of the United States, has officially become the 47th President of the United States, beating back Republican President Phil Scott and Democrat nominee Mark Kelly. This comes after both Democrats and Republicans have had severe arguments and infighting amongst themselves over the past 2 years after their disastrous midterm results, with them losing both the House of Representatives and the Senate to Rebirth. This was due to significant voter dissatisfaction with the handling of the economy, COVID-19 and a lack of real policy direction from President Scott and VP Baker.

The Results:
Bernie Sanders (REB): 322 Electoral College Votes
Phil Scott (GOP, Incumbent): 133 Electoral College Votes
Mark Kelly (DEM): 83 Electoral College Votes

This win comes after multiple years of turmoil within each of the 3 parties. With Donald Trump’s death in October 2023, Bernie Sanders sought to get the Presidential nomination for Rebirth. This would ultimately lead to a much stronger, more unified front against both Democrats and Republicans.

In his victory speech, Bernie Sanders praised Donald Trump, stating:

“America, while we have won this election, we are still a nation in mourning. I wish that President Trump would be here with us now. He tragically lost his life, but now it is up for all of us to try and remember his legacy. I embrace his legacy. He was a good man, and an even finer President. I was glad to call him a friend. We will always honour his memory, and I will always continue to fight for the American people.”

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/tuff.jpg'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/tuff.jpg'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/BernieTooltip.jpeg'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "What A Loser", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-27%20150131.png", "EqFQ61lX528" ); } } else if (e.final_overall_results[2].candidate === 301 && e.final_overall_results[0].electoral_votes < 270 && uniparty == 1) { e.pages.push(`

REBIRTH SLUMP IN POLLS AS THEY NOTCH 3RD PLACE WIN, CLINTON AND SCOTT PUSH OUT INSURGENT THIRD PARTY

Agony. It felt unreal. They were set to definitely come first, or second, hell, some projections had them winning. The frauds in the media, the crooked pundits, the liars on all the networks. It was their failure. Their fault. It felt like the two main parties clamped down on this new party. They were scared of what it would become. An actual fighting force. Or maybe, it just wasn’t meant to be. Maybe the country didn’t want populism as a whole. Maybe it rejected them because of the chaos in the past 4 years. Maybe, even, the country had lost its vigor. It’s spirit. It was hard to tell, and even when the mudslinging was over and the election was heading into full gear, no one was certain who was going to end up on top. Everyone had already had their hopes of who would come where, and what the electoral map would look like, but certainly no one had expected this, bar Clinton herself. She wanted to see it, she wanted to see the two men who beat her (electorally) destroyed. She was right. Rebirth, while making a significant impact on the Electoral College, was still a dud. It was a disaster for them. It was a tragedy, and they couldn’t wash away that stain. The hype, the promise, the momentum, it was all behind them, and yet, they had failed to secure the top two. President Trump saw it as an abstract failure. He couldn’t bear to see it anymore. His legacy, his tenure as President, would be reduced to this. Forever a laughing stock. Vice President Sanders didn’t fare much better. He felt hopeless, for the first time in a while. It was daunting. Saddening. What more could he have done?

Bitterness built up within President Trump. How could he be humiliated like this? Such a puny result, he thought. He thought he was better than this. Bitterness was just the beginning. Jealousy soon set in. How did Clinton, let alone Scott, beat him? How did a man who barely had any titles under his belt, and a neo-con, beat him? He was enraged. Jealous. He wanted revenge. Retribution. There was no way this could have happened. He saw it for what it was: Rigged. Rigged against him, the whole political class was against him from day 1. It was enraging. Bernie felt similar, although he was much more composed.

Bernie was handling it much better than his superior. He had expected this. All his life, he always knew he was the underdog, but he never gave up hope. He fought, even against all odds, for what he believed was right. He fought for justice. He thinks back to his activist days, when Civil Rights was just the leading topic of discussion and debate within America. He thinks back to his Mayoral race in Burlington. He then turns back to 4 years ago, and the situation he faced then. He had to work with a man who he had fought for a better part of a year. And look what happened, just a few short years later. They had put aside differences for the greater good. It didn’t matter if this was the result they wanted or not, they did something remarkable. It was just a shame Donald couldn’t see it yet.

“I may not be at my best right now…”

Bernie was sat down, looking at the TV. His face perplexed with all sorts of emotions. Anger but Hope. Acceptance and Guilt. It was all surreal to him. He fought his hardest.

“Bernie, look, we should’ve fought harder. We did fight hard, frankly, but we didn’t get our base, our patriots, they didn’t turn out. It doesn’t help- the fact that- Republicans and Democrats, they wanted us gone frankly. Sick, sick people, they ought to have some shame. We’ll be back soon, roaring back, trust me Bernie, it’ll happen.”

Trump was still in this race. He wasn’t about to give up. But Sanders? He couldn’t be nominated. He knew that. Trump knew that. It was a bittersweet moment.

`); e.pages.push(`

SPECULATIONS SWIRL OF WHO COULD BECOME PRESIDENT. BETTING MARKETS CHOOSE PRESIDENT CLINTON / VICE PRESIDENT BAKER

Terrifying times are ahead for the American people. More dysfunction in government. More infighting across party lines. It’s a mess. With the contingent election going ahead, no one is quite sure what to make of these results.

Rebirth, once considered to have all the momentum in American politics, was stopped by a tidal force of the Establishment parties crashing down on their parade. They had lost significant ground, and ultimately, cost them upwards of 50 House Seats along with 3 to 6 Senate Seats.

With this in mind, and a Democrat resurgence in the House and Republicans controlling a plurality within the Senate, the odds that there will be a D/R Executive has gone through the roof. This would be a reversal of what happened back in 2016, with a R/D Executive, however, it remains to be seen what might occur between the leaders of both parties.

They could either make a deal with each other, or reach out to Rebirth, who are currently pursuing legal challenges to the election result, as they question the legitimacy in some states.

The most likely option would be that Hillary Clinton becomes the first female President in American history, with Charlie Baker becoming Vice President. This would mark a significant shift in policy from Trump/Sanders, and ultimately, it remains to be seen if this outcome will even occur.

It is a long way until January, and a lot could still happen.

`); e.pages.push(`

How America switched from a two-party state to a four-party state, in just 8 short years

2028.
There now belongs a 4 Party system within the United States of America, all vying for control of the Presidency and the Legislature. There are the two ‘main’ parties, those being the Republican Party and the Democratic Party. The ‘new kid on the block’, Rebirth, was founded in 2018, just before the Midterm elections that same year. Its focus on populism and dismantling what it saw as an unjust and unfair system resonated with millions of Americans, and ultimately, it had planted itself on the political landscape, hoping to bring a new wave of populism right across the United States.

The newest party, formed from some Progressive Democrats and some of the Rebirth Party, is called the Justice Party. This is led by senior figures such as Alexandria Ocasio Cortez and Ilhan Omar, who were supposedly ‘tired’ of kowtowing to Former President Donald Trump on certain issues, and issued an ultimatum.

“Give us justice reform, give us more power within the party, otherwise we will revolt.”

The Former President did not take it so seriously, and asked the Rebirth Chair, Bernie Sanders, to talk to them. Ultimately, while they had made some headway, negotiations stalled, and as such, they formed their own party.

The political parties at play in this election:

Democratic - Centre-Leftism, Capitalism, Soft Hawkism/Dovism, Social Justice
Republican - Centre-Rightism, Free-Market Capitalism, Hawkism, Conservatism
Rebirth - Big Tent Populism, Mixed Economy, Dovism, Social Progressivism
Justice - Leftism, Socialism, Dovism, Progressivism

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20181006.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20181006.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20181006.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "What A Loser", "Jazzwave", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-27%20150131.png", "EqFQ61lX528" ); } } else if (e.final_overall_results[0].candidate === 301 && e.final_overall_results[0].electoral_votes == 269 && uniparty == 1 ) { e.pages.push(`

TRUMP/SANDERS ONE OFF FROM VICTORY - CLINTON PUSHES FRAUDULENT CLAIMS FURTHER, SCOTT CONCEDES DEFEAT

Livid. So close, and yet so far from victory. Donald was angry. Why didn’t all of Maine go his way? Why didn’t Nebraska? Why didn’t these swing states vote for him? Was he the issue facing Americans? They were so close to giving him an awesome mandate - one which would have transformed American politics. Scott and Clinton were fighting for the remaining scraps. Trump didn’t care. All he wanted was that one vote that could change American history forever.

Bernie sat beside him, looking at the map, then back at Trump, then back at the map again. It was unbelievable. How could this have happened? If a few hundred votes swung one way or another, they’d be comfortable enough to know that they could have had a mandate to carry on their vision for the country. Bernie didn’t want to face reality, just as much as Trump did. It was sort of a heartbreaking result for them.

First came Scott. He conceded gracefully, he knew he was at the end of the road. He said that while it was a tie, he would officially bow out of the race now, and free his votes to whomever desired them. It was a shambles for him. But Trump smelt an opportunity. But then…

Clinton went out and spoke to her crowd. What was about to be said would change the course of American history.

“This election, I won it, and I won it by a lot.”

Trump couldn’t believe his ears. What? Is she suicidal? Does she really want to end her political career here?

Bernie knew what Trump was thinking. It was obvious, but he didn’t speak up. He knew, in his lifetime, this happened maybe once? In a generation. He knew it went against his moral convictions. He knew it went against what he stood for, but he also saw the opportunity. He saw what could be done. He saw what Clinton had just committed. It was time to go on the offensive.

“Bernie, look, I think we have a real shot, I mean, we’re one off, right? We can very easily win this thing. Clearly, Clinton has lost her marbles, and who cares what she thinks? She’s just doomed her political career. She’s an idiot, a fraud, a crook. I think we can do it, right? People won’t complain, look- our people, patriots, the real winners, they’ll cheer us on no matter what, okay? We both know, frankly, what needs to be done. I, Donald J Trump, will be the one to fix this, and these losers and suckers in the crooked media, the establishment, they won’t stand a chance, trust me.”

In his heart, Bernie knew that Trump would probably have said the same thing if he had lost, but still, he stood up, walked up to Donald, and shook his hand. Right then, a silent deal was made. It was time to pull in a few favours. It was time to take the fight to Clinton.

`); e.pages.push(`

CLINTON CALLS FOR THE ABOLITION OF THE ELECTORAL COLLEGE, SAYS ‘TRUMP RIGGED IT’

“The Electoral College has significant flaws. It no longer works for the American people. It’s a disgrace of a system. It’s deadlocked time and time again, and now, we need to replace it. We need to fight this rigged system. We’re going to fight, and win. I have faith.”

Hillary Clinton, the Democratic nominee for President, called for the abolition of the Electoral College. She knew what she was doing. She had to do anything to win. She saw it as her destiny to assume the Presidency. She bided her time with Bill, she almost won against Obama, and then was betrayed by Sanders. It was disgraceful in her eyes. She devoted her life, her services, to America, and yet, they threw it back in her face. It was almost pitiful.

“Listen, Trump, his cronies, Sanders included in that, for the record, they rigged it against me. They rigged it against Scott, but he’s backed down. I WON’T. I will fight on for the American people and ensure that we get a fair, democratic election. It’s time for real democracy to take place. No more division, no more delay. I call on Trump to concede. I call Sanders to concede. They need to do it- for the country.”

It was going to be a long few months for Clinton and her legal challenges. But everyone knew this was going to happen, no matter the result. All she wanted, in their eyes, was revenge. Retribution. They almost pitied her for it. It was sad.

`); e.pages.push(`

Why the Democratic Party is obsessed with losing - and how it can turn the party around

The recent midterm elections prove why the Democratic Party is losing ground with many of its core voters. They had lost over 70 House Seats and another 10 within the Senate. It was a brutal night for them, while both Republicans and Rebirth managed to both gain massively, with many ‘safe’ Democratic seats falling in place for Rebirth especially.

Republicans: 190 Seats
Rebirth: 192 Seats
Democratic: 52 Seats

Still, they hold enough seats within the House to swing the vote for Speaker of the House, which can effectively make them kingmaker of the House. The same can be said for the Senate.

Republicans: 42 Seats
Rebirth: 36 Seats
Democrats: 22 Seats

Again, this would ensure they are able to become policy/king makers within the Senate. The Democratic Party may have fallen down a deep hole, but they can dig themselves out. By playing ball with both parties, they will be able to secure some concessions, and ultimately, appeal back to their voters.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20204830.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20204830.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20204830.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Night Of Fire", "Niko", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20193642.png", "SRbhLtjOiRc" ); } } //other endings if (quickstats[0] < 269 && trumpcruzdeath == 1) { e.pages.push(`

CRUZ LOSES THE 2020 PRESIDENTIAL ELECTION -- PRESIDENT SANDERS WINS FULL TERM

The crowd at the election watchparty was silent. Some of them eagerly awaited the results while others looked indifferent, and a few looked outright bored. Cruz started to watch the results trickle in. A few staffers looked excited. They knew that he had gained a sympathy vote, but would it be enough to carry him over the finish line? It was time to find out.

The states started to be called. Vermont went to Sanders, Kentucky went to Cruz. As the election went on, Cruz was starting to get unnerved. Some swing states started shifting left instead of right. It was worrying to him. The watchparty booed as some swing states were called for president Sanders. Eventually, the inevitable result was called.

"President Sanders has gained over 270 electoral votes, and has won a full term."

Rage was all Cruz felt. He sucked up to Trump, and this is what he got for it? As he was thinking about the campaign, Cruz heard the phone ring. Everything told him not to answer, that answering meant defeat. But he had no choice. What was he meant to do, say the election was rigged? No- by doing that, he would not only dishonour himself, but the entire Trump family, who had backed him up through to this very moment.

Cruz walked out and greeted the crowd. They clapped politely as he got ready to speak.

For a few seconds, the world that Cruz inhabited, stopped. He picked up the phone, swallowed what pride he had left, and starting speaking to now President Sanders. His daughters, once just small children a few moments ago, gave him nods of approval. He did the right thing.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-30%20203827.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Roshambo", "The Network", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/2/26/Thenetwork_moneymoney2020-cover.jpg", "4XOlN4YiHbw" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 269 && trumpcruzdeath == 1) { e.pages.push(`

CRUZ WINS THE 2020 PRESIDENTIAL ELECTION -- TRUMP'S LEGACY SAVED?

Silence. There was only silence as the election watch party started. Cruz waited for the results to come in. After Trump's death, he knew that he had gained a little sympathy vote, but would it be enough to get him across the finish line?

The usual states came in first. Kentucky to Cruz, Vermont to Sanders. Ted started to think about Trump. He thought about 2016 and how they both hated each other, of course, that was different now. They were on the same ticket and knew that defeating Bernie was more important than their petty squabbles.

Cruz knew that people were counting on him. He stared as more states were called. Texas, was obviously called for Cruz. He heard cheers as Bernie's lead in the Midwest and other areas started to shrink. Finally, the election was called:

"Ted Cruz has won over 270 electoral votes, and will now be our president-elect."

The people at the watch party roared. Their new savior, Trump's successor. They could continue to Make America Great Again.

Cruz greeted the crowd. He wondered what Trump would've thought.

"As your president-elect, I just want to thank you for...."

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-30%20203848.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Born in the U.S.A", "Bruce Springsteen", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/3/31/BruceBorn1984.JPG", "MUx5Vu2XRqA" ); } } if (quickstats[0] > 269 && trumpkempdeath == 1) { e.pages.push(`

KEMP WINS THE 2020 PRESIDENTIAL ELECTION -- A NATION RESTORED?

The watchparty didn't have a lot of energy. Most of Trump's supporters had either left out of grief or had ditched Kemp for some third party nutjob. Of course, the reasonable people--the moderates and even some conservative democrats disgusted by Bernie's socialism jumped to the ticket. Kemp and a few staffers were busy watching the results trickle in. He had gained some sympathy votes, but that was undercut by some Trump supporters voting for third parties.

The first few states were being called. Vermont to Sanders, Kentucky to Kemp, and so on. Georgia, and many other states in the South were obviously called for Kemp. Some states in the Midwest were looking pretty close, but finally, the networks called it.

"Brian Kemp has crossed 270 electoral votes, and is now our president-elect."

The crowd at the watchparty cheered. Now, in their eyes, it was time for the nation to heal and move away from insane populism.

Kemp went out to greet the crowd, he waved before he spoke. He wondered what Trump would've said. He quickly brushed those thoughts away. 'He's dead.' Kemp reminded himself. 'Plus, barely anyone here had a positive view of him...'

"I would like to thank the American people for..."

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-30%20203222.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "What's the Frequency, Kenneth?", "R.E.M", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/6/65/R.E.M._-_Monster.jpg", "bwT1U7d5-Vs" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] < 269 && trumpkempdeath == 1) { e.pages.push(`

KEMP LOSES THE 2020 PRESIDENTIAL ELECTION -- PRESIDENT SANDERS WINS FULL TERM

The crowd at the watchparty was silent. Some checked their phones, and others started to leave. Most of Trump's supporters had voted for right-wing third parties and didn't show up. Kemp knew that he could lose, but he tried to push that thought away. He did gain some sympathy votes from Trump's death, but was it enough to carry him over the finish line?

Soon, states were being called. Vermont to Bernie, Kentucky to Kemp. More and more states were getting called. Georgia to went Kemp, obviously, and New Mexico went to Bernie. He saw . Staffers could already tell that things weren't looking good. Kemp tried to convince himself that this was just a blue mirage. The numbers proved him wrong.

The networks called the race.

"Bernie Sanders has crossed 270 electoral votes, and has now won a full term."

Kemp sighed. Of course he lost. It had to have been those Trump supporting nutjobs who cost him the election. They couldn't stomach voting for someone they thought was a "RINO" and proudly split the vote in certain states.

He needed to call Sanders.

He waited for the new President to pick up.

Finally, he did.

"Um, hello, Governor Kemp."

"Hello, Mr. President. I just want to wish you luck over the next 4 years."

"Thank you, Governor. We may, er, disagree on a lot of things but I respect how you handled Trump's death. It greatly affected me as well."

"Sure. You know, you'll probably get into situations where you won't be able to get everything you want. You'll need to water things down. It's part of the political process. I hope you understand what I'm trying to say."

There was a pause on the other end.

"I understand."

Eventually, the line hung up.

Kemp got up and went to address the crowd.

"Look, I know things didn't go the way we wanted them to, but...."

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-30%20203407.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Bad Day", "R.E.M", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/7/79/R.E.M._-_In_Time.jpg", "LevEZqInttY" ); } } if (quickstats[0] > 269 && trumppalindeath == 1) { e.pages.push(`

PALIN VICTORIOUS AGAINST SANDERS - PROMISES ‘AMERICAN RENEWAL’ AMIDST CHAOS IN THE WHITE HOUSE

It was certainly surprising. Sarah Palin, the so-called ‘Darling of the Right’, or the loser of the 2008 election, had now come back 12 years later to fulfill what she saw as her destiny. She had joined Trump, knowing what it had entailed. She knew what she was getting into. She knew there was some risks, and concerns. She joined anyways. She saw her country go down a path that it could not go down without killing itself. America was in deep, deep trouble, and she saw that only her, and Donald, could save it.

“And we can now project that former Vice Presidential nominee, and now Presidential nominee Sarah Palin, has reached the required 270 Electoral College Votes to out-right win this election and become the first female President of the United States.”

Surreal. She broke the glass ceiling. Hillary was fuming. In fact, half of America was fuming, but now- this was her world, and she could shape it as she deemed fit. This was her moment in the sun. Everyone thought, the pundits, the public, they all thought she peaked in 2008, her pinnacle of success. How wrong they were.

A Victory for the ages. She would surely relish it. There was no need for a call with Sanders. He was irrelevant now. Sarah Palin, the 47th President of the United States.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-31%20190837.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Buddy Holly", "Weezer", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/7/70/Weezer_-_Blue_Album.png", "SiJie3Z7DG8" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] < 269 && trumppalindeath == 1) { e.pages.push(`

TRUMP SYMPATHY VOTE CUTS SHORT - PALIN LOSES OUT TO NOW PRESIDENT SANDERS

Distraught. Palin was distraught. She gave it her all for her beliefs, her passion for an America built on conservatism and it’s fundamental way of life. She had now lost that to what she saw as a ‘gun grabbing, hippy communist’ and someone who demeaned and embarrassed the best Republican President in a lifetime. It was shameful. She couldn’t believe her eyes. How could this have happened? It was unbelievable.

How could this have happened? Palin thought, at the very least, some sympathy vote would come in for the former deceased President. The numbers, they were there, but it wasn’t enough. It left her heartbroken. She kept questioning herself. Her internal doubt, it clouded her. It clouded her judgement. This isn’t what she wanted. She wanted a victory, a big victory, one that would silence the Left. But, alas, it wasn’t meant to be.

She would go out to her crowd, and concede yet another race. She was used to it by now, anyways. The people who were there for her, they weren’t her people. They were Donald Trump’s people. They stood by him ever since he rode down the escalator in 2015, but now, they had decided to stick with Palin. All for naught.

She started off with the words…

“I’m sorry.”

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-31%20190931.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Go Away", "Weezer", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/3/32/Cover_of_Weezer%27s_album_Everything_Will_Be_Alright_in_the_End.jpg", "quSh86h6uAk" ); } } if (quickstats[0] > 269 && trumprubiodeath == 1) { e.pages.push(`

FIRST HISPANIC AMERICAN TO BE PRESIDENT OF THE UNITED STATES - SANDERS RETREATS AS RUBIO LOOMS OVER ELECTION WIN.

Rubio had expected this, and it felt so sweet to him. He deserved it, more than anyone. He wasn't going to lose to some socialist who defended the actions of Cuba and the former USSR. He felt relieved, knowing he'd be able to now ascend to the top job of the United States. He was looking forward to continuing his work for America. At last, he could put down the campaign and relax for a moment. The cheers, the champagne bottles, it all felt so surreal. Victory had never been so good for Rubio, and it finally dawned upon him.

It was a shimmering moment. While he did not personally want to gloat about it, he had become the first Latino man to assume the Presidency. He felt proud at that very moment, why wouldn’t he be? The doubters, the people who punched down, the frauds within each party. Even Trump himself cast doubt on Rubio, but he proved his doubters wrong. He could truly aspire to be a President for all Americans.

“We can now project that Senator Marco Rubio, from Florida, has officially won the 270 Electoral Votes needed to become President of the United States, and will assume office January 21st, 2021. This has been a surreal moment for both campaigns, as with Bernie Sanders becoming President after Donald Trump’s death due to COVID-19, many thought that this election was simply too close to call, but we do indeed have a winner: Marco Rubio.”

It felt unreal. But it was very much a reality. He now had to address the crowd that awaited him. This was now his movement.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-31%20202235.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Stylo", "Gorillaz", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/d/d1/Plasticbeach452.jpg", "OmwZtFwFj94" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] < 269 && trumprubiodeath == 1) { e.pages.push(`

CUBAN-AMERICAN LOSES TO REAL SOCIALIST SANDERS - REPUBLICANS DESPONDENT.

Saddening. All the hard work that he had put in, it was gone. He had debated him plenty of times within the Senate, he had fought against Sanders at every turn, and now, he had lost to him. He didn’t know what to do. The Trump family, who were with Rubio at the time, they had that look. Disappointment. Dread filled the room. No one was happy with this result, it was shattering.

Donald Trump broke the political establishment 4 years ago. Rubio couldn’t even replicate that. He WAS the Establishment. It was a travesty. Bernie Sanders, the Democratic-Socialist, had managed to climb back through the ranks and topple what he saw as an ‘Oligarchy running the most mighty and just nation of all’. Rubio couldn’t stand it. But what could he do now? All he could now was call him. But something told him, he couldn’t do it. He just couldn’t. What would his parents think? Or his grandparents? He felt ashamed.

He went out to the crowd in Mar-A-Lago. He couldn’t look them in the eye, face to face, but he began his speech. He didn’t call Sanders.

“After looking at the recent votes, there is no way forward for our campaign. We, uh, we fought very hard and for President Trump, but I couldn’t make up the difference. I want, to uh, congratulate President Sanders on his win…”

It was going to be a long, long humiliation ritual for Marco Rubio. This was just the start.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-31%20202301.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Broken", "Gorillaz", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/d/d1/Plasticbeach452.jpg", "ZfSiNh2zkRM" ); } } // bernie endings if (e.final_overall_results[0].candidate === 50000 && e.final_overall_results[0].electoral_votes == 538 && bern == 1) { e.pages.push(`

Bliss

Your eyes are glued to the screen, with the clocks ticking louder and louder, heavier and heavier, waiting for the News Stations to just call it, the wait is heavier than the results itself. You pray, and pray, and pray for the people to choose the right path, for a new era, you don’t really understand why you want this so much, you just sit there, idle as the thoughts grow louder and louder, do you deserve this position of power, whilst so many more suffer? But you know it deep down, you deserve this, through your hard work yourself, you passed through, and now you wait.

Tick Tock Tick Tock Tick Tock Tick Tock Tick Tock Tick Tock

And then it fades out, as you slowly fall to the land of sleep, but you didn’t, as something has happened, something you thought would be impossible, something that never could have been, you eyes latch on to that little screen, with the states being called. It’s too close to call in Republican strongholds throughout, with Democratic strongholds called early, then those Republican Strongholds are called

Blue, Blue, Blue.

Each and every state falls down your aisle, picking you, whether it be DC progressive or those Wyoming Country Gals, they went out for you. God knows how, but they did. It seems like they finally chose the people’s man rather than an elitist. You shed a tear from the results. You're just so happy, you laugh at the Republican collapse, you just laugh, seeing how the nation sided for the right side, and by a mandate so large it can’t be ignored, you bask in it.

they need you, you must fix what has gone wrong, you must build back, bigger and better than before. The senate and house results were better and better for you, 61 Senate seats, 252 House seats, and 28 Democratic Governors, you look up into the Heavens and thank God, as such a dominating victory hasn’t happened since LBJ. May the nation prosper under your arm.

`); e.pages.push(`

The Revival Of The Progressive Era

For every election that happens, a new era will come and go, whether it be the Era of Hope and Change with Barack Obama, the New Deal Era of FDR and so on and so forth, but this election has revived an old era, that of Progressives, their standard bearer? Bernie Sanders, with his firebrand style for what's right, he has pulled the Dems to the left, with them actually having a spine eh? Now let’s actually talk about his policy.

During the campaign Sanders stuck to his guns, without moderating at all, and surprisingly, it was very successful, with the landslide we got this election. These campaign policies include:

1. Universal Healthcare including Dental and Visual coverage

2. $17 minimum wage

3. Union empowering

4. A $3 Trillion stimulus package in the form of public works to build public transit, housing, road repairs and much much more

And lastly, a crackdown on gerrymandering

It seems like the people did feel the Bern with this agenda as the coattails he brought have started working day and night on the ‘American Revival Act’ which include policies 1, 2 and 4 as seen, and this bill being passed is an inevitable. However, Republican filibusters (however few are left) are trying to stop this passage, but really, think about it, theres barely any Republicans left after the Blue Storm, so they can’t hold out for long

Now let’s pull up Trumps concession speech, did he even have one? All he ever said was that he conceded, in that feeble voice that Orange man did, With Bernie not caring all too much about that. Mitch McConnell probably got a heart attack and fucking died from hearing the results, wait one second

Huh, oh what, he actually did?

Well it seems like after Bernie’s inauguration, he did as Bernie Sanders certified himself. So uh yeah, I didn’t really want to end on that note, but that’s all I have let to talk about so I guess go live on with your day.

`); e.pages.push(`

FROM TRUMAN TO SANDERS, UNIVERSAL HEALTHCARE HAS BEEN PASSED

It seems like what was once promised by Truman long long ago has finally be put into action, almost 70 years later, but it has come. Public response to this has been universally popular, with Congress (apart from the Progressives that came with Sanders coattails) are mute on this, with Hillary Democrats in a slight revolt. President Sanders has made a comment on this in our most recent interview with him; ‘ I do believe that we have made monumental progress against the 1% with this new bill, but the fight is not over, as Gerrymandering, the Electoral College and Big Business are all still enemies for us and the nation as a whole

And it seems like the Bernie machine can’t stop and won’t stop, as new bills are being rallied up, with free university being prepared as the next target, it seems like nothing will stop him from his policy, even with the chaos in his party, seems like his coattails have kept his plan alive.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20195145.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20195222.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20201418.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Song of the United Front", "Jamie O'Reilly & Michael Smith", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20195716.png", "ufYu8ocTeLo" ); } } else if (e.final_overall_results[0].candidate === 50000 && e.final_overall_results[0].electoral_votes > 269 && e.final_overall_results[0].electoral_votes < 290 && bern == 1) { e.pages.push(`

Victory?

It wasn’t meant to take this long. It was already 1 AM in the East Coast and yet still CNN couldn’t project a winner, Hell they couldn’t even call New Hampshire or Minnesota, Minnesota! But thankfully, ever so slowly, you saw the Blue Tide trickling in, though quite narrowly in the Key Races, which stretched through all of the Midwest (bar Missouri and Indiana), the so called “Sun-Belt” of Georgia, North Carolina and Florida and the South-West, and ever so narrowly, you won. It sure as hell wasn’t clean, and just a few 10, 20 or even 30 thousand could have thrown these swings to Trump, giving him a win, or worse yet, a deadlock.

That ever so unfamiliar sense of relief flooded down on you, with your vindication from 2016 shown in full here, in fac-

*ring ring* *ring ring*

It seems Trump will actually acknowledge your victory here Bernie (why are you referencing yourself in the third person?) You pick up your office’s faded white phone as you listen in to what Mr Trump has to say.

Trump: “I’m not sure how you did it yet but I know that YOU rigged this election, because after all how on earth could I LOSE, L-O-S-E, I’m the best president this nation has had in a century, my approval is sky high and the economy is booming under me! Your snaky little friends down at the DNC obviously rigg-”

You don’t have time to deal with this coward anymore, he knows you won, but he would never say that, so instead, he tries to scream election fraud all the way. Now, let’s get to work!

`); e.pages.push(`

MCCONNELL DEAD

November 18th, 2021

ABC News

Earlier this evening we have received reports that Mitch McConnell, Senate Majority Leader has been confirmed dead as of 1:53 PM EST in his home in Louisville, Kentucky. New reports coming in have reported his death was due to a heart attack, with a special election on the way, with Amy McGrath in a solid lead compared to her 2020 run, with Kenneth Lowndes trailing 5 points behind. This is already quite a shock to pundits around the nation with McConnell’s sudden death.

It seems like the commonwealth will get competitive this season, even without a major Sander victory. Now with the death of McConnell, the Republicans have to do some restructuring in the South, with old folks like Mitch in charge since the ‘80s, when they die, it will be chaos, with the ‘Solid South’ shifting left, little by little, and lets see if McGrath’s shocking lead holds up until the election.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20150435.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-21%20152946.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "The Touch", "Stan Bush", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/3/38/Transformers-them-movie-soundtrack.jpg", "HSh73d3TZcA" ); } } else if (e.final_overall_results[0].candidate === 50000 && e.final_overall_results[0].electoral_votes > 339 && e.final_overall_results[0].electoral_votes < 535 && bern == 1) { e.pages.push(`

The Height of the Mountains

You sit there, aloof as the results slowly tick in. They’re quite close in Republican strongholds, but it seems like the Republicans aren’t going down without a fight, but when the states are called, you feel a sense of warmth, it’s a landslide win. They cheer you on as you just think. A sense of joy fills right up to your nose, you just know it was the right thing, but was it? You just sit there and ponder whilst you regain your resolve.

You deserve this.

You get up from your lousy office couch as you start the victory tour, from Portland to To Miami and the cheers, oh the cheers are deafening. They love you, they need **you**. You’ve seen it all, from the Highest of highs to the Lowest of lows, you understand how they feel, why they feel like this and that jazz, so you shut your mouth. And that was what they would do, they shut up for you.

You know what this is, your no Jack Kennedy, but you sure feel like one, as it seems like your David, versus the Goliath, a man you helped establish power, was the man you slaughtered. Can you think straight. Yes you can, can you talk straight. Yes you can. You feel it in your bones, the desire, it slowly kicks in, for those who suffer, for it all.

`); e.pages.push(`

SANDERS SUPREME

ABC - November 10th, 2020

With the latest data as collected, have seen a Landslide win for Bernie Sanders, a left-wing populist and the former Vice-President of Donald J Trump, and with his electoral victory secured, Sanders has no need to stress out about shenanigans from the Republicans as they have gave their concession speech earlier this day;

“It seems like this election has firmly picked Bernard Sanders as the president, whilst I am not satisfied with this outcome, I will not be a sore loser, as I know when I have lost, so I will concede this election and wish the best on Sanders”

With this Concession, it seems like the Overton Window has shifted, with the States of Ohio and Iowa moving firmly Blue, with the Blue wall stronger than ever whilst the leftward tilt of the south has stopped.

`); e.pages.push(`

THE FALL OF KABUL

Fox News

Febuary 12th, 2021

It seems like Sanders folly has finally caught up to him, with Kabul’s fall being a real wake-up call to the silent majority as a massive blow to the presidents prestige, with the Rational Afghanis conquered by barbarians who don’t know what humans rights are, it’s quite sad to see. In under 5 hours the Taliban took control of Kabul. In ***5 Hours***, how so? This is such travesty to America and her military. And with his unchecked power in Congress, he can pass his radical agenda whilst destroying America’s prestige.

Now with the Midterms looming, I would say that this Democratic Trifecta is long gone, with Republican gains ready up with polls shifts toward Moderate Republican, it seems like these special elections will show how the Midterms are, with the Republican Party shifting left.

Along with this it seems like America has accepted the socialised option, with the Public Option a true form of socialism from the government, what are we? Are we the USSR? Hell no we aren’t, so why are we trying to become some communists and do this, business knows what’s best for all.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20150511.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/bernportrait.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20172850.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Soak Up The Sun", "Sheryl Crow", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/7/71/Sheryl_Crow_-_C%27mon%2C_C%27mon.png", "KIYiGA_rIls" ); } } else if (e.final_overall_results[0].candidate === 50000 && e.final_overall_results[0].electoral_votes > 309 && e.final_overall_results[0].electoral_votes < 340 && bern == 1) { e.pages.push(`

A New Dawn

You’ve seen elections come and go lots before, but not, this election is the most important yet, as it is about you now, no longer are you some offset asset, that ‘radical socialist’ who’s put there to placate the left of the party, your at the Helm of the party, with this election deciding wether they follow in the path of progressivism or down the dark and twisted road of moderation, so the states tick in, slowly and steadily you see the victory coming.

It was always going to happen, that was for sure.

With the results in and done, you see it all, the states that fell your way, they fell down your aisle, and the win was respectable, it was strong, it was everything you wanted and a little more.

You get up, it’s a long drive to Madison Square, and the vehicle lumbers on down the road, with the speech ready in your mind, just without the words to form it, you expected this outcome but didn’t think it would really happen. It’s a nice feeling, victory. Donald Trump might give a concession speech, but Donald is not the man to do it, so you need to sit back and take a break, you’ve earned it.

You are content with the results, and you are just sitting down, watching, listening, and understanding. It seems like these next 4 years will be free for you to run, with a decent sized trifecta and a large series of bills to pass, the skies are blue, and all will be true. Now let us hope for a new dawn, a dawn better than our last.

`); e.pages.push(`

SANDERS PRESIDENT-ELECT

As we can now conclude, Vice President Bernard Sanders will become the next president of the United States of America, with quite a sizeable margin of Victory, giving a narrow trifecta to the President-Elect, with Donald J Trump giving his concession speech earlier this morning in Baltimore, Maryland to a crowd of 18 Thousand:

“Good morning America! This beautiful nation has every right to choose who leads her, which is why I will humbly concede to my opponent, Bernard Sanders. You see, despite our differences, Bernard is a good man, quite a good man, and I will congratulate him on his win, another populist fighting the machine”

Audiences noted that it is a sweet and simple concession, with Trump not making any claims that the election was rigged or calling for any sort of violence, so for the relative future, the sky is looking blue, the grass is greener than before, and a ***Dawn of a New Era*** is here, so let us move towards this time. With Bernie on the helm, and the future bright, he has given a new address in the Pulsing Mecca of America, New York City, with his address yet to happen.

`); e.pages.push(`

COLLAPSE OF THE SANDERS REGIME

Newsmax - November 8th 2022

For long a while the silent majority were quiet on such treachery of Bernard Sanders, but with his incompetent governance on full display with this debacle. As seen earlier this year with the Russo-Ukrainian war, Sanders is unable to manage foreign policy, whether it be Ukraine or now, with China flexing its military muscle.

For anyone unbeknownst of this current situation, the People’s Republic of China has grown more violent with skirmishes with the Philippines and Taiwan increasing over, and it seems like Xi won’t back down as these skirmishes have not stopped since Sanders weak attempt at resolving this issue. Along with that inflation is at its highest yet at 8.7%, showing the incompetency of the sanders regime.

And it seems like there is one last thing we have yet to cover, it seems Yemeni pirates have been capturing and looting American ships, whilst Sanders is just sitting in DC with other coastal elites there, whilst we lose our stuff we paid for to barbarians.
