Deadlock

You peer into the screen, another world really, and you look in, peering at what those little reporters say, and you open your eyes a little more, with the states being counted one by one, as you see them, closer than expected, with ‘solid’ states on both sides having the margins run up, and then slowly, but surely, you see no one has won, it’s a deadlock, 4 years later. “Victory doesn’t repeat itself, but it sure does rhyme” - Mark Twain, and Twain was quite right about that, as in such different conditions, the same result came once more.

You look at the results, and you see that a lot of negotiations will have to be done, with this election more chaotic than ever, Trump has gone feral, screaming how the deep state is rigging this election to tear down populists like him. I think at this rate Schumer will have a stroke if theres another deadlock. And then you just take a good look, with the Margins so very close through the swings, you can’t help but wonder how this came to fruition? You can’t help but wonder, what if they truly were the things that never were.

You’re sitting there, just shocked by how you could deadlock twice in a row, and then you just stare out to the abyss, just thinking, and then the world goes to dark…

`); e.pages.push(`

Sanders Sworn In

January 20th, 2021

With the deadlock that came from the 2020 election, and a contingent split right in the middle with the few votes from Independents carrying Bernie over the finish line, but the senate was different in each and every way, with a bombshell revelation of Trump vying for the vice-presidency, and winning by a narrow margin of 51-49, now with Sanders the boss of Trump, we see a reversal of 2016, which is quite a comical turn of fate, but Trump will be staying in the White House, with the cost of destroying the Republican Party as a whole.

“We might slowly be shifting towards a multi-party America” from Phillip Arvon, member of the New York City council and a journalist for CNN “As with 2 deadlocks in a row, people are slowly seeing how the Electoral College really screws over small parties and favors the smaller states more.

Along with that we can see a Republican collapse in all forms and sizes, with the MAGA movement dead in the water now, with the Republican Party in the same crossroads as in 2016, but this time without the Trump Style in to give it an identity.

`); e.pages.push(`

*PRESIDENT-ELECT PHILLIP ARVON

NBC News - November 4th, 2032

For the first ever Parliamentary election of America, Phillip Arvon, of the Democratic-Progressive party has won a majority of the 847 seats, with each seat ranging from around 300,000 to 600,000 people in them. The Results are as Follows:

Independents with 3 seats

Democratic-Progressive with 312 seats

Labour with 124 seats

The Centrist Party of America with 62 seats

Libertarian Democrats with 14 seats

The Christian Democratic Party with 244 seats

And Liberty with 88 seats

It seems like the presidency of Nikki Haley will not continue on as Phillip Arvon, New York Governor since 2026, with them winning a plurality of seats. A coalition of DP-L has been formed, with Andy Beshear as Vice-President.

Now the reason for this new system dates back to 2024, with Sanders re-election attempt ending in deadlock, with him president once more, but with this deadlock, Sanders would create a bill to create a parliamentary America, to rid of this deadlock chaos, but with an amendment added by moderates that it would take place from 2032 onwards, no earlier. But up until then, the old system would stay, it’s quite a miracle how Bernie passed it, maybe just the second deadlock, the democratic tack left and whatnot. But in 2028, there was another deadlock, leading to 4 deadlocks in a row, which really sold America on the idea of a parliament, leading to general support of this, with some trying to push this towards the 2030 midterms.

Now under this new system, we will see how Arvon's presidency plays out.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20150435.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20150435.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20173656.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "The Winner Takes It All", "ABBA", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/1/1c/ABBA_-_Super_Trouper_%28Polar%29.jpg", "92cwKCU8Z5c" ); } } else if (e.final_overall_results[1].candidate === 50000 && e.final_overall_results[1].electoral_votes >= 259 && e.final_overall_results[1].electoral_votes < 269 && bern == 1) { e.pages.push(`

The American Rot

As the election results come ticking out, you see razor thin margins in the key swings states, it seems you have the lead, then Trump, then it stays Trump, you… lost, but just barely, and you just can’t help but feel annoyed, after all the chaos of the Trump Administration, they went with the Republican himself, a man who has no care for anyone but his lackeys in Wall Street, you just sit down, not knowing what to think.

The concession speech was a day later, who conceded honourably, but you can’t help but feel somethings wrong, with the Republicans just laughing at your concession, like the prick they are, and your not upset with the results, your just filled, and filled with a resolve, you see how small the margins were, how it all came crashing down, maybe it was a lack of campaigning in the key swings, maybe a gaffe or two, maybe just how you treated your enemy.

But the fight isn’t over, with the blocking of a Republican Trifecta with the senate at 51- 48 for the Democrats, if the Republicans want to want to pass anything, they’re going to have to fight tooth and nail for every inch of regression, and you know that, so your to fight, and win.

`); e.pages.push(`

American Fallout

www.politico.com - Last updated 5 minutes ago, 3rd of May, 2021

With the most recent shutdown having its end slowly being negotiated, people seem to ask, how did we get here? Well, it all stems from the Republican narrow victory in 2020, and with the radically different budget pushed by the Republican Tea Party, with this causing a shutdown which has lasted over 60 days before Republican back-down, and this seems like a real blow to the gut, so what did this Republican agenda include? Well, it includes the following:

1. The full removal of the Affordable Care Act, also known as as Obamacare

2. A sweeping tax decrease, but in the the effect of a negative progressive tax rate, decreasing taxes on the rich the most whilst little on the poor

3. Large military and ICE spending to as quote “Remove all those robbers and rapists back to Mexico”

4. Shutdowns of “Democratic Run” departments such as USAID to fund

Along with other, more minor issues, but in all reality, this is a big blow to MAGA and its core, with the left invigorated by this win, however, this has come with the pain of the centre, with Schumer voting to end the shutdown with figures such as Catherine Cortez Masto voting for the earlier compromise bill, but this may overall have a negative effect on the democrats, with a potential loss of the house, but this will most likely cause a leftward shift for the Democratic Party as a whole, with Schumer approval in the gutter with democratic voters, as 15%

What does this mean for America? Well who knows really, all we know is that this is a negative spiral for the Republican Party as a whole, with approval of Donald Trump falling from 52% to a low of 46%. And with inflammatory speeches from Trump and those of MAGA Republicans, America will become more polarised than before.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20175210.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20181006.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Washing Machine Heart", "Mitski", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/f/f3/Be_the_Cowboy.jpg", "12rsVkwSsXU" ); } } else if (e.final_overall_results[1].candidate === 50000 && e.final_overall_results[1].electoral_votes >= 229 && e.final_overall_results[1].electoral_votes < 259 && bern == 1) { e.pages.push(`

`); e.pages.push(`

Well, it all stems from the Republican narrow victory in 2020, and with the radically different budget pushed by the Republican Tea Party, with this causing a shutdown which has lasted over 60 days before Republican back-down, and this seems like a real blow to the gut, so what did this Republican agenda include? Well, it includes the following:

1. The full removal of the Affordable Care Act, also known as as Obamacare

2. A sweeping tax decrease, but in the the effect of a negative progressive tax rate, decreasing taxes on the rich the most whilst little on the poor

3. Large military and ICE spending to as quote “Remove all those robbers and rapists back to Mexico”

4. Shutdowns of “Democratic Run” departments such as USAID to fund

Along with other, more minor issues, but in all reality, this is a big blow to MAGA and its core, with the left invigorated by this win, however, this has come with the pain of the centre, with Schumer voting to end the shutdown with figures such as Catherine Cortez Masto voting for the earlier compromise bill, but this may overall have a negative effect on the democrats, with a potential loss of the house, but this will most likely cause a leftward shift for the Democratic Party as a whole, with Schumer approval in the gutter with democratic voters, as 15%

What does this mean for America? Well who knows really, all we know is that this is a negative spiral for the Republican Party as a whole, with approval of Donald Trump falling from 52% to a low of 46%. And with inflammatory speeches from Trump and those of MAGA Republicans, America will become more polarised than before.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20175210.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20181006.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Washing Machine Heart", "Mitski", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/f/f3/Be_the_Cowboy.jpg", "12rsVkwSsXU" ); } } else if (e.final_overall_results[1].candidate === 50000 && e.final_overall_results[1].electoral_votes >= 191 && e.final_overall_results[1].electoral_votes < 229 && bern == 1) { e.pages.push(`

PRESIDENT-ELECT DONALD J. TRUMP

With the results in, we can confirm to you that Donald J. Trump has won , much to the shock of polls all around the nation, with polling errors of up to 8% in states

And you may ask how this could happen really. Well it might be that the momentum that Bernie had throughout his campaign was a farce, non-existent really, or that the polling was skewed by a large margin, maybe it was the lack of campaigning in key states, maybe just an issue of funding really, the reasons why Bernie lost isn’t the clearest, but we know he lost, and lost bad.

Now we see the Republicans stay in the White House, but this time, without a Democratic in the office of the Veep, along with strong margins in the house and senate, but this does not at all mean it will be easy for the Republicans to hold on to their seats as the fervour that is the campaign of Bernie is not lost upon many, with the Cities of New York, Los Angeles, Seattle and so many more up in flames from this loss of Sanders, when reaching out to Sanders, we have not got a comment

Further on we see that the Democratic Party as a whole has shifted leftwards, with a democratic “Tea Party” taking shape, with Sanders as it metaphorical head, as we see that the survivors of this Red Massacre are more akin to the likes of Sanders, whilst Republicans whom win democratic seats were in the more Moderate side, however there were a couple of exceptions to this spree of Democratic losses, with a few notable gains in Georgia.

`); e.pages.push(`

The Brink of Nation: How America Almost lost its Commander in Chief

VNA News

As Donald J Trump, the sitting president of America comes out on a rally in Syracuse, New York State, gunshots ring out. 5 times such echo would ring, and 5 times they would hit. Secret Service scrambled through to protect the President. Trump would be taken to hospital via stretcher, all by 9:02 AM EST.

Who was behind this? Well all reports Lead to it being a 23 Year old Woman named Marie Anderson, quite oddly enough a fervent Trump supporter and Far-Rightist, whom was not caught until 7 hours later, at 4:07 PM EST in Buffalo, New York State, where she would be arrested.

President Trump is stable from what we know, with no bullets hitting major arteries or organs

Anderson has been detained and put in holding prior to trial, with a manifesto found left behind. This Manifesto contains a large array of widely debunked conspiracy theories, ranging from ‘Lizard People’ to a shadow oligarchy of ethnic minorities, circling such ideas through far-right communities on X, Reddit and Discord, with this person mostly using the Sub-Reddit of r/thecampaigntrail

*This is a live article, and as such we don’t have all the details right now, check in a day or two if you want to see more of the full story

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20171655.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20182658.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Washing Machine Heart", "Mitski", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/f/f3/Be_the_Cowboy.jpg", "12rsVkwSsXU" ); } } else if (e.final_overall_results[1].candidate === 50000 && e.final_overall_results[1].electoral_votes > 135 && e.final_overall_results[1].electoral_votes < 191 && bern == 1) { e.pages.push(`

America the Idiotic.

As the results start pouring in you saw the red wave, you thought it surely wouldn’t last, but it did, and you suffered through it all, with not just every swing states, but states that are meant to be reliably Blue flipping Red, the vote gushing in red, and States such as New York and California having their margins whittled down to that of the Margins of a swing state.

You put your head in your hands, how could this have all gone so wrong? The polls, every poll, even the most conservative of the conservative where predicting a Sanders win, however narrow it may be, and that was your downfall, you were too complacent, too ineffective, too little of that, too much of this, your campaign was no well oiled machine, it was a clunky, slow machine,one that could not work fast enough compared to Trumps

You just can’t believe it, you were so close, yet so far, from Burlington to the Second-In-Command, you reached the sky, up so high,larger than life gave, and yet you fell. Fell harder than ever. You worked for this nation, for something you dreamed of, something to help the many, not the few, yet they called you names, so many horrible, horrible names and they twisted the knife right into your back.

But unlike him, you will give up graciously and honourably, as you know when to give up the fight, no matter how painful. You get in the car to D.C, getting your concession speech ready, it’s going to be a long trip ahead, and hopefully you’ll see the light at the end of the tunnel.

`); e.pages.push(`

Trumpmania!

Andsodat Times - November 5th, 2020

It really seems like people swung Trump this election, despite the polls indicating a strong Sanders victory, flipping the states of Ohio, Iowa, Florida, the Rust Belt and the Sun Belt, which would give sanders 374 EVs, instead, it seems like Sanders has lost in a landslide, losing key swings throughout the nation and even Safe Democratic states sliding down the Republican Aisle, with states we predicted blue flipping red, with only Democratic Strongholds staying blue, but with thin margins for usually such blue states.

Now this is the biggest Republican Landslide since Dukakis now, with this being a horrible, horrible night for the Democrats, with them losing dozens of seats in the house, with more than 30 lost to the Republicans, with pundits scratching their heads on how this could happen, despite the general disapproval of Trump by most conventional sources, maybe it was due to Sanders’s perceived radicalism, maybe it was due to something else, but theories have started to sprout up, claiming the election was rigged, but this theory is largely discredited and only circulated in small communities which the median voter definitely isn't in.

With Trumps electoral victory we have seen a surge in the stock market, with the DOW rising by 10% on average, with companies such as Tesla and Apple rising by as much as 15%, with a cryptocurrency called Bitcoin at its highest ever, reaching a value of $52,540.

`); e.pages.push(`

Trump Begone!

@Obaminion_19736536 - March 5th, 2021

Well, it really seems like Trump would do anything to win now, doesn’t he? Well, if you’re living in a hole on the side of a ditch, let me catch you up to speed, well, it’s simple, Trump Lied and People died. Trump rigged the election, causing whatever abomination this electoral map is, and with the Senate voting on this, it seems like Trump might be out of office relatively soon.

Update 1: Oh my god it’s actually close, rn it’s 27 Ayes on this impeachment and 23 Nays, like, there is actual, fucking proof of Trump rigging this election, but Senators are still voting to save Trump? Is this their way of committing political suicide?

Update 2: Nvm, it seems like that was just the small red tide, with 69 (yes haha funny number) Senators voting Aye and 31 Nays, and it seems like Donald Trump will be impeached from office, Democratic Landslide in 2022?

Update 3: Oh my fucking days it actually happened, Trump is no longer President, now it will be his vice president who takes over, I think they’re gonna lose worse than Ford in 2024, I’m telling you now, Texas, Florida, Ohio, Iowa, Georgia, North Carolina and maybe even states like Montana and Kansas will all go blue, it’s over, it’s actually over for them

Something New.

As the results pour in, you can’t help but notice the trickle of red over the coverage, with states considered “safe” were still too close to call, but with the shakeup to the foundation of American politics, this was expected to be closer, but slowly, well, not really slowly but more rather quite rapidly you see the red wave rush in, which state after state being called for Trump

And then New York was called.

CNN: “We can now officially call the state of New York to Donald Trump, and with that, securing another 4 years in the White House for him.”

That wasn’t meant to happen. It wasn’t meant to be such a blowout loss, both you and Trump know this, but it happened. Every single state was called for Trump, and even DC?! You can’t help but wonder how this could have happened, did you not campaign hard enough, was it your style, your policies or something else? As you start dialing Trump, you can’t help but notice the tremble in your hands, the slight pale tint to your skin whilst you wait for him to pick up.

Trump: “Well Bernard, I’ve been waiting for you to call me! No hard feeling about this election, am I right?”

You hold back for a bit, unsure what to say as Trump thinks you can’t hear him, finally, you find the words to say.

“Well- uh, congratulations on your victory, Donnie, I’m not too sure how you won so bigly, but its been an honour to work with you.”

You weren’t intending to say the last part of your sentence, but you don’t correct yourself, seeing the cheer in Donald’s voice as he rambles on about some stupid shit, but you see that he’s not too different from you, and not just some vile, loud-mouthed madman but just another person, with many flaws but still just another person.

`); e.pages.push(`

Mod Announcement : A New World Order

March 17th, 2026

One Explosion was all it took. Just One. One to shatter the American Overton Window. Both Bernard Sanders, Donald J. Trump, Ted Cruz, Sherrod Brown and many more dead in such a horrible act of violence. And out of everyone there ready for the Oval Office, it would be Nancy Pelosi to step up, the lamest, least uniting of them all to lead, with a country ripping itself by its seams and American hegemony toppled how will Pelosi survive A NEW WORLD ORDER…

(if you’ve been living under a rock for about 5 years or so on January 6th 2021 whilst Bernie Sanders was certifying Donald J. Trump’s when a bomb (which was later found to be planted by Ali Khamenei which resulted in the American invasion of Iran from 2023 - Current) exploded, killing Trump, Sanders, 3 Dem Senators and 4 Republican senators, resulting in House Majority Leader Nancy Pelosi to take charge of the nation)

`); e.pages.push(`
Archive of Our Own
A Billionaire, A Revolutionary
by Big Toni
Rating:General Audiences Pairing:Sanders/Trump Category:Romance

After a long and hard campaign trail victory has been achieved, and the knight in shining armor, Donald, and helpless princess, Bernie go back to the castle, the White House, to celebrate winning the election together. It wasn’t an easy victory, no, some would say it was a pyrrhic victory, for the pair had realized many things about each other, and truth be told, they were happy as ever to have learned so much about themselves in the process.

As they’re both driven to the White House, Bernie and Donald sit side by side from each other. They were laughing and chatting about all the funny hijinks the two had gotten up to with each other, Covid had brought them both down, it hit the economy so hard, and a lot of lives were lost from it, so the laughter the two shared with each other served as a good respite from all that mess

As the two were laughing, Bernie sat his hand down on the arm rest, and Donald, while not looking where he was going to put his hand down to rest, accidently touched Bernie’s hand. The car was dead silent, and the two looked at each other for a fleeting moment, and looked away, and the two blushed hard. “S-sorry” Donald said, sheepishly, not even managing to look at Bernie. The blush on two faces remained, stagnant, and everlasting.

The driver in the front of the car asked if everything was alright, and two quickly said yes, and the rest of the ride was ridden in silence. When the two reached the White House, they quickly exited the vehicle, and the rest of the night was a blur. The two slept in separate rooms, in separate halls, and the two had separate hearts, and they both woke up to a peaceful morning, a peaceful sky.

As the sun was rising in the sky, Donald walked up to the balcony, and faced the sun. While looking at the beautiful sun, he got a cigar and lit it and started smoking it. He enjoyed the amazing view of the Washington monument, and watched people on their morning jogs in D.C.

He remembers visiting it on the campaign trail, he giggles to himself about that wasted day. As he was enjoying the view, Donald heard a familiar voice, Bernie’s voice

Donald almost drops his cigar, he was scared, he didn’t want to face that man since the incident in the car, but now he has to face him, now he has to face the biggest challenge he has ever faced in his political career. “Donald.” That voice, it came from a Vermonter, from a man raised thousands of miles away from where the two are now today. “Donald”. The voice is getting louder, his heart is pounding. Bernie’s heart is also pounding, the two hearts are at risk of ripping from both of the men’s chest and embracing into one. “Donald!”. He can’t do it, he can’t face him. “DONALD!”. “I LOVE YOU!” Donald says, shouting with all of his might, the power of which shocked the two. “I love you two, Donald.” Donald drops his cigar, and the two embrace, the two hearts embrace, the billionaire and a revolutionary, at last, unite.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20173805.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20185030.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/bro.gif'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Y.M.C.A", "Village People", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/4/4c/Cruisin1978.jpg", "khOFB1Erzdg" ); } } //trump endings if (quickstats[0] > 535 && donald == 1) { // 538 e.pages.push(`

TOTAL TRUMP VICTORY

The mood in Mar-A-Lago was one of intense celebration. Trump had done what nobody thought possible. He had won in a landslide, but not just a regular landslide. He had won every single vote in the electoral college. How the hell did he do it? Polls showed him beating Sanders, but this? It was inevitable that the Democrats would cry foul, but he would deal with that later. For now, at an abnormally long table, the President clinked glasses with his closest donors and friends. They planned to fundamentally reshape the nation with the mandate given to them. The President excused himself from the table, and strolled into a utility closet, before locking the door. He put his hand to his ear, pressed a button, and began to speak.

“Are you alone?”

“Yeah Chit, I'm alone. Frankly, I think it's safe enough for you to part in the festivities! People won’t even notice how we won in such a landslide!”

“Look, that is incredibly stupid. Now moving on, we need to talk about how you will begin removing protections on Uk-”

Trump was barely paying attention, instead fixating on a crack in one of the marble tiles on the ground.

“Are you even paying attention?”

“Yes I am, look Chit, I understand you want concessions, but we need to ensure the Democrats don’t try to steal back the election first.”

“I have a plan for that, you see, the idea is-

Suddenly, a cracking sound echoed throughout the closet. The President turned around, and even through his cheap spray tan, his skin turned pale.

“Donald, what’s going on-”

Trump quickly ended the call.

“Bernard! What is my good friend Bernard doing in the floor, with a pistol no less!

“The fact that even a single person thinks this result is legitimate is a serious problem.”

“What are you talking about Bernard? We won bigly, and fairly against you!”

“Oh shut the fuck up Donald! I heard your little conversation with an agent named “Chitmenyu over there, the US is built on sacred institutions of fair elections, I won’t let you take even that from us.”

“Let’s just calm down…”

“Absolutely not! We are done here, say goodbye Donald!”

“Just hold on one goddamn second!”

“What’s left to talk about?!”

The President quickly reached for his ankle, but as his finger was on the trigger, a shot rang out.

And It was over.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/tuff.jpg'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "YMCA", "Village People", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/4/4c/Cruisin1978.jpg", "khOFB1Erzdg" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 339 && quickstats[0] < 535 && donald == 1) { // Massive victory e.pages.push(`

TRUMP ELECTED DECISIVELY

You sat silently on the couch, half-eaten McDonalds on the table. It wouldn't be finished - the televisions occupied your attention. There were the liberal coastal elites, morose, Bernie was too close to Trump, they said, too radical, maybe the truth is he was too far away from you, just a little ant compared to this American Colossus. FOX said it was a blood-red map, Florida went within minutes, his People had made it too big to rig, had flooded Michigan and Pennsylvania then Minnesota and New Jersey, an army that had finally broken through the gates, all for you. A whole nation gift-wrapped as the present for your greatness, for your incredible triumph over the Losers, the Liberal Establishment that always had a vendetta against you.

The bastards on CNN were silent, couldn't acknowledge your victory, the second death of the Soviet Union, You could barely hear Jared telling you it was a party fractured in a million places, the Left had died, the Moderates lost, and the Establishment had been shattered. They would never recover. Not fast enough, at least.

There were so many people talking, but you were too caught up in your mind, the spectre of final victory within your second term, ready to become a reality. You couldn't hear the calls for you to deliver the victory speech, the call from whoever your opponent was, Jared said he had a call from Mitch McConnell, you didn't care about a call from yout new puppet, it was suffocating in here anyways, you went off to the bathroom without any explanation and stared yourself in the mirror.

In the mirror, you saw the greatest president in American history. Obama couldn't have done this, could never have a world reshaping itself to his every whim, could never crush an opposition so brutally fractured and inept, rolling out the red carpet all for him. You saw it practically as a divine vision, your future, the future of your world. It would all be yours in a second term. There was no need to negotiate with RINOs or roll over the Dismal Democrats, it had all been done tonight, you could finally end Obamacare, end the immigrant crisis, end term-limits, pack the Court, something not even the oh-so great Roosevelt could do.

A knock on the door.

"Sir?"

In the light of the mirror, you saw how great you were, the outlines of the world you would come to define. It was blinding.

`); e.pages.push(`

ANALYSIS: CAN THE DEMOCRATIC PARTY RECOVER?

January 18th, 2021

Ever since Donald Trump's absurd triumph over Bernie Sanders, the Democratic Party has been split into a million pieces, with the terrifying possibility that recovering in time might be impossible. It wasn't supposed to end like this, and nobody thought it would - except perhaps the staunchest Trump supporters - but now the image of a defeated crowd watching silently as Sanders conceded the election hangs like a spectre over the intraparty squabbling. Much has been written about how we got here, I plan to focus on what's next.

First, the Moderates. With the Left's main figure, Sanders initially discredited by moderates as far too close to Trump on some issues, it served as a burden on his campaign even if some were split between opening the possibility of bipartisan work or shutting out Bernie entirely for shaking hands with the Devil. None of it matters now, all replaced by a traumatizing election night that saw vast amounts of their faction wiped out, even if many believe they were vindicated by the results. With a wiped-out bench (a bench that was first depleted under Obama), the amount of moderates able to take the fight into 2024 are low. Names range from Michael Bloomberg to Cory Booker and strangely enough Hillary Clinton of all people.

Now, the Left, a faction that will perhaps forever be unable to shake off the 2020 election. Even as the nation seemed to move strongly in favor of right-wing populism, it also seems to have rejected left-wing populism. Bernie has forever been burnt by this election, and it's unknown which progressive can carry his torch from now on. Unless a reversal of fortunes happens within the next four years, they likely won't make it far. They must also grapple with exisential questions about their lack of popularity compared to their policies.

That's all for now, my final message is please don't nominate Hillary Rodham Clinton in 2024.

`); e.pages.push(`

Trump policies are unpopular, but can Dems take advantage?

April 11, 2024

Every poll shows the same thing - Trump's interventions in Greenland and Venezuela are disliked by a majority of people. Americans don't support his escalation against Cuba, and many are starting to turn against his aid for Israel. It's not just a general disdain for hawkishness - Trump's dovish actions in Ukraine are also unpopular, most especially his meeting with then President Zelenskyy.

Domestically, it's a mixed bag. His more populist actions have garnered popular support, but the hefty military budget and large tax cuts are putting a strain on the budget, as the economy itself begins to enter a downturn. Trump still holds the advantage on this issue, but it's starting to wane. More than that, his pet issue - immigration - is starting to lose popularity with Trump's heavy, brutal enforcement. Other issues like abortion are far less favorable to the President.

So why do the polls also say that the Republicans are favoured to win in November? Let's narrow things down.It could be that Trumponomics really is working, and us Liberal Media people are wrong for questioning it, spreading misinformation about gas prices that fail to mislead the People. It probably isn't down to anything Trump's vice-president has done. More likely, it's because the Democratic candidate is Hillary Clinton. She's spent half of her time calling herself the legitimate winner in 2016, practically calling Bernie a traitorous Judas who colluded with Trump in the White House (and her supporters say, perhaps threw the election to Trump), and also turning off the left faction further by being almost as hawkish as Trump is, just smarter. Some left-wing third party will probably win a decent amount of votes, which is to say 4%, since Bernie's not running. This is possibly the worst election in American history.

Conclusion? May god save America. Because none of these candidates can.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-03%20190817.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-03%20190955.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-03%20190729.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "America, Fuck Yeah!", "Team America", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-04%20092721.png", "5uPoDNEn3I0" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 309 && quickstats[0] < 340 && donald == 1) { // Large victory e.pages.push(`

“I never really liked him anyway, to be honest.”

The mood in Mar-A-Lago was exquisite. In the gold plastered halls of the Grand Ballroom, the President clinked glasses with donors, while he awaited his inevitable victory. He knew it’d be a landslide, and right he was. While the Democrats had an initial edge, the red wave would finally crest. By 11PM , it was all over. One by one, television networks would crown Donald Trump the winner of the election. Soon after, he appeared on stage, at the head of rapturous applause.

“Sorry to keep you waiting folks! Complicated business!”

The crowd roared, knowing that their savior, the man who would take them from the brink, had won. Chants of “USA” could be heard from miles away.

“This was, and still is, one of the greatest political movements in this country. MAGA they call it, Making America Great Again, and we’re going to do amazing things in this country, believe me. I wanna thank you, the people, and to my amazing, truly tremendous staff on making this happen, I mean, y’know, I did most of the work, but I don’t mind sharing some of the credit! Now, we need to heal the divide. Frankly, I mean, Bernie is a crazy guy, he wanted to make my whole administration a socialist hellhole! I see the people have rejected that, that is why we won BIG LEAGUE.”

“Every single day, every single hour, I will fight for you, I will not rest until America becomes the most prosperous and well-respected nation, and we’re going to continue our amazing work, just without Sanders! Thank you everybody, thank you, may God bless you and may God bless the UNITED STATES OF AMERICA.”

Soon after taking his leave from the stage, the call came.

“Hello, Mr. President?”

“Well, if it isn’t crazy Bernie himself!”

A deep exhale is heard through the phone.

“I congratulate you on your victory in this election. I hope that we can move o-

“You didn’t think you would beat me, did you, Bernard? I don’t get why you ran this campaign in the first place!”

“I hope that we can move on from any division lingering and enter a more peaceful era in politic-

“Come on Bernard, give me something here, I feel like I’m talking to a wall here!”

“Fine then, I’d suggest you remember the people who got you to where you were.

The phone hangs up, and Bernie was gone.

He was finally gone.

The President stood up, and rejoined the celebration.

`); e.pages.push(`

TRUMP’S VICTORY SPEECH: HOW IT CAME TO BE!

The people were cheering, chanting, yelling his name. His victory, his struggle was theirs. As President Trump stepped up on the stage to address his crowd, he couldn’t conceal a smug grin, his hands pressing the podium deeply. Come to think of it, they looked a little off. But he’d cross that bridge when he got there.

As the mass chanting died down, the President spoke up:

“I want all of you to look back to the past. They said: Oh, Trump is toast. Bernie took the Wind out of his sails. He's done for.”

A general grumbling filled the arena as the people in it remembered the pencil-neck pundits doubting their champion.

“They counted me out. They thought it couldn't be done. Let me ask you something. Where are they now!?”

The crowd cheered.

“I'll tell you where they are! They're hiding scared, with a dark stain over their pants, because not only did we win, we won bigly.”

There was further celebration.

“Congress, it's… it's totally aligned. We've got a mandate, in both the House and Senate. Let this sink deep within you: WE did that! This is our total, supreme victory over the doubters, the losers, and…”

A moment of hesitation. The memory of the first meeting he had with Bernard. The brutality in which he handled a campaign against someone he would have gladly called his friend not too long ago.

That was all in the past. He won and that's all that mattered. He swatted those thoughts away as he focused back on his prize, his fired up base.

“... and especially over the marxist communists who hate America!”

The decibels increased dramatically, before being hushed for one last victory lap.

“Thank you America. Thank you for believing in the values of patriotism and common sense conservative leadership. I really don’t like to say thank you. I don't think it’s good, but it's truly terrific what we've achieved tonight, and I intend to fulfill your wishes to the fullest in these next four years.”

The crowd, already cheering, was further whipped up into a frenzy, hooting and hollering deep into the night. The country was theirs, at last.

`); e.pages.push(`

POLITICO - November 14th, 2022

Fresh off a midterm session that defied all polling, the Democrats find themselves at a crossroads.

The party is currently split between an older, more centrist leadership and a progressive caucus that looks to be growing by the day trying to “bring the party over” to their fold, using biting attacks on the Trump administration to rally voters onto their side. Ever since the midterms, this rift has only grown wider and pundits say this might jeopardize Democratic chances heading into 2023 and 2024.

House minority leader Pelosi stressed a renewed commitment to bipartisanship in the wake of the results, while party chair Clinton pledged yesterday to continue the current “big tent coalition” approach, promising her constituents that the results of the long term strategy would manifest “soon enough”.

Despite flipping enough seats to win the House and successfully oust speaker Paul Ryan, the GOP held control over the senate, with the only two seats going to democrats, Ohio and North Carolina, not being enough to challenge Republican control. On the matter, Trump mocked the Democrats on MetaWeb (formerly known as Twitter), saying that “Bad Brown and [Beasley] have wasted effort for nothing”, and promising in the same Webpost to “continue the Golden Age the country finds itself in regardless of what the LOONY DEMS do!!”

Of course, the discussion around the elections is still very vivid and hotly contested, seeing as, despite flipping the House, turnout actually dipped in traditionally Democratic areas such as New York, Illinois and Washington.

Emmanuel Rahm, for one, points to the republican margin of victory in the last election as the reason for such a poor democrat performance. “It's not about the party platform nor its strategy” - he said in an interview with POLITICO this week - “simply put, the red wave of 2020 allowed the republicans to coast through the midterms without needing to worry about Congress at all.”

Rahm also disapproved of pundits using the results to try and push the party left, saying: “If the Democrats took such a hardline approach against a beloved incumbent, it would have certainly put voters off. By simply pointing out what went wrong with the Trump administration and running a big tent coalition, Democrats were able to reclaim the House’s gavel” - something that he remarked as a “considerable achievement.”

Meanwhile, others point fingers at a possible conspiracy due to the massive discrepancy between the pre-election polling and the actual results. While the democratic party apparatus has not dignified these rumours, they appear to be slowly gaining a foothold in left-wing dominated social media.

Other news stories:

>Trumpcare: the GOP’s proposed sol…

Nuclear standoff in middle east ente…

>How inflation could be beneficial to …

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/landslide.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20215203.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20173656.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Seven Nation Army", "White Stripes", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/a/a8/Elephant%2C_The_White_Stripes_Original.jpg", "RDuzszjrdcc" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 289 && quickstats[0] < 310 && donald == 1) { // Large victory e.pages.push(`

“Sorry to keep you waiting folks! Complicated business!”

The crowd roared, knowing that their savior, the man who would take them from the brink, had won. Chants of “USA” could be heard from miles away.

“This was, and still is, one of the greatest political movements in this country. MAGA they call it, Making America Great Again, and we’re going to do amazing things in this country, believe me. I wanna thank you, the people, and to my amazing, truly tremendous staff on making this happen, I mean, y’know, I did most of the work, but I don’t mind sharing some of the credit! Now, we need to heal the divide. Frankly, I mean, Bernie is a crazy guy, he wanted to make my whole administration a socialist hellhole! I see the people have rejected that, that is why we won BIG LEAGUE.”

“Every single day, every single hour, I will fight for you, I will not rest until America becomes the most prosperous and well-respected nation, and we’re going to continue our amazing work, just without Sanders! Thank you everybody, thank you, may God bless you and may God bless the UNITED STATES OF AMERICA.”

Soon after taking his leave from the stage, the call came.

“Hello, Mr. President?”

“Well, if it isn’t crazy Bernie himself!”

A deep exhale is heard through the phone.

“I congratulate you on your victory in this election. I hope that we can move o-

“You didn’t think you would beat me, did you, Bernard? I don’t get why you ran this campaign in the first place!”

“I hope that we can move on from any division lingering and enter a more peaceful era in politic-

“Come on Bernard, give me something here, I feel like I’m talking to a wall here!”

“Fine then, I’d suggest you remember the people who got you to where you were.

The phone hangs up, and Bernie was gone.

He was finally gone.

The President stood up, and rejoined the celebration.

POLITICO - November 14th, 2022

Fresh off a midterm session that defied all polling, the Democrats find themselves at a crossroads.

The party is currently split between an older, more centrist leadership and a progressive caucus that looks to be growing by the day trying to “bring the party over” to their fold, using biting attacks on the Trump administration to rally voters onto their side. Ever since the midterms, this rift has only grown wider and pundits say this might jeopardize Democratic chances heading into 2023 and 2024.

House minority leader Pelosi stressed a renewed commitment to bipartisanship in the wake of the results, while party chair Clinton pledged yesterday to continue the current “big tent coalition” approach, promising her constituents that the results of the long term strategy would manifest “soon enough”.

Despite flipping enough seats to win the House and successfully oust speaker Paul Ryan, the GOP held control over the senate, with the only two seats going to democrats, Ohio and North Carolina, not being enough to challenge Republican control. On the matter, Trump mocked the Democrats on MetaWeb (formerly known as Twitter), saying that “Bad Brown and [Beasley] have wasted effort for nothing”, and promising in the same Webpost to “continue the Golden Age the country finds itself in regardless of what the LOONY DEMS do!!”

Of course, the discussion around the elections is still very vivid and hotly contested, seeing as, despite flipping the House, turnout actually dipped in traditionally Democratic areas such as New York, Illinois and Washington.

Emmanuel Rahm, for one, points to the republican margin of victory in the last election as the reason for such a poor democrat performance. “It's not about the party platform nor its strategy” - he said in an interview with POLITICO this week - “simply put, the red wave of 2020 allowed the republicans to coast through the midterms without needing to worry about Congress at all.”

Rahm also disapproved of pundits using the results to try and push the party left, saying: “If the Democrats took such a hardline approach against a beloved incumbent, it would have certainly put voters off. By simply pointing out what went wrong with the Trump administration and running a big tent coalition, Democrats were able to reclaim the House’s gavel” - something that he remarked as a “considerable achievement.”

Meanwhile, others point fingers at a possible conspiracy due to the massive discrepancy between the pre-election polling and the actual results. While the democratic party apparatus has not dignified these rumours, they appear to be slowly gaining a foothold in left-wing dominated social media.

Other news stories:

>Trumpcare: the GOP’s proposed sol…

Nuclear standoff in middle east ente…

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/landslide.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20215203.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20173656.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Seven Nation Army", "White Stripes", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/a/a8/Elephant%2C_The_White_Stripes_Original.jpg", "RDuzszjrdcc" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 269 && quickstats[0] < 290 && donald == 1) { // Very close victory e.pages.push(`

TRUMP EDGES OUT WIN IN THE ELECTORAL COLLEGE - PRESIDENT SAYS HE IS VINDICATED

It didn’t matter how much of the Popular Vote Sanders won by. It didn’t matter how much the left would scream and wail like spoilt brats. This was his moment, his victory. He was going to savour it as much as possible. All the trials and tribulations. All the struggles he had to endure. All the fights he had with VP Sanders over policy. It didn’t matter anymore. Finally, the executive was under full Republican control. No longer would his agenda be stalled by some communist hippie.

The drinks flowed through Mar-A-Lago, not that Trump had any. His staffers, his campaign personnel, they needed this. It was a stressful few months, to say the least. The back and forth, the barbs, the attacks, the defenses. They finally had to wind down.

Then the call.

Trump stood up, and was presented the phone. It was Bernie calling. He smirked. Finally, he had that socialist right where he wanted him.

He answers. Calm. Cool. Collected.

“Hello, uh, Mr President, I just want to, uh, congratulate you on your win. The system has spoken and it has decided that you should be the President of the United States. I wish you the best.”

The bastard hung up. On the President? He knew what he was doing. Saying the ‘system has spoken’. Trump will remember that.

In his moment of victory and triumph, the President couldn’t feel anything but anger.

`); e.pages.push(`

TRUMP MOVES FORWARD WITH CABINET POSITIONS - SANDERS CONCEDES DEFEAT AMIDST DEMOCRAT BETRAYAL

“I have just called President Donald Trump to congratulate him on his victory and this very difficult campaign. This isn’t the outcome any of us wanted, but it is the outcome we have to respect. We cannot go down the path of denialism and delay. We have to accept the facts. It’s been a tough year, especially for ordinary Americans, but I will continue to serve as Vice President until January 21st, working for the American people and their needs. Thank you, God bless you, and goodnight.”

Trump sat down, watching Bernie concede. It was beautiful. He had prepared his speech now. He was going to be the conciliator. The ‘nice guy’, not how the crooked media painted him. In his own eyes, he was a saint, a man to be protected against the evil, wicked establishment. He was the man who saved the Republican Party from the brink of neo-conservatism, he was the man who had prevented socialism from infecting the very core values of Americanism. Millions more would cheer this on. Millions would despise him because of it. But he won. It was as simple as that.

Trump went onto the White House Lawn. He saw reporters, supporters, all these different people from unique backgrounds, their history all coming together for this one moment. Sweet Victory.

“I had just received a call from Vice President Sanders. He had called, very gracefully actually, to concede the race to me, Donald J Trump. It was very nice, very sincere of him, and I truly wish him the best as we move forward as a nation. Americans have had enough of division, and frankly, deadlock within our electoral system. That’s why we won, by the way, because the public wanted a real MANDATE that only I could deliver. Look, there’s no two ways of looking about it. We’ve been elected on a win, and we’re going to use that to ensure our elections are secure, our democracy is safe and that America continues to be the best nation on Earth.”

With Trump now having the full executive behind him, only time will tell now whether or not he actually gets a tri-fecta within government, as many of the House and Senate results are still pending their final vote tally.

`); e.pages.push(`

TRUMP APPROVAL RATINGS SLIP AFTER BOTCHED AFGHANISTAN WITHDRAWAL

Following the usual inaugural honeymoon, President Trump was probably hoping for a better start, even with a divided congress. Instead, his approval ratings have dropped to 40%, a new low in his second term, even if it doesn't yet match the lows of his first. More concerningly, public opinion in other areas is turning against the President, with only 42% trusting him on foreign policy, and only 40% of people seeing him as a strong, capable leader.

The polling largely shows that Trump's credibility as an able leader is shot, with rambling press conferences failing to convince Americans, and inspiring concerns about his age. The White House is expected to shift gears after this failure, focusing on passing crucial domestic priorities to move on from this failure.

Asked for comment, the White House stated that Trump is more than up for the job, blaming falling approval ratings on "biased polling" and the "liberal media hegemony", deriding reporters as "panicans".

The White House also stated there was no concern about Trump's mental fitness or age, citing private anecdotes where he perfectly drew a map of the Middle East, including bases and troop numbers while discussing the withdrawal in Afghanistan, and another where he ably discussed the economy, managing to "remember graphs and statistics" in a way that "impressed" seasoned economists like Jerome Powell and Steve Mnuchin. These anecdotes have not been independently confirmed.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20171655.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20173805.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20172850.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "All I Need", "Radiohead", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/1/14/Inrainbowscover.png", "FM7ALFsOH4g" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] == 269 && donald == 1) { // Deadlocked election e.pages.push(`

How have we ended up here AGAIN?

A Billionaire and A Revolutionary

A Tale of two Prophets...

Donald Trump, Darling of the- wait, what? I swear we've done this before, haven't we? Let me see here.......yeah.....2016, deadlocked with President Trump and Vice President Sanders. How have we managed to get here again? I mean this is unprecedented, I can't even- just why? I don't have the faintest idea how this will play out.......well, only one way to find out I suppose. Anyway....

You stare, unblinking at the phone, waiting, begging even, for a call that you know will never come. You also know that not so far east in Vermont, Sanders will also be staring at the phone, going through the exact same thought process. You both know what will happen now, that being absolutely nothing. Despite all of the very public arguments you both have had since 2016, you are once again pushed together by an electorate that can't make up its bloody mind. However unlike 2016, you both know perfectly well how this is going to go, and if it's anything like the first term, it won't last long either. What happens then is known by fate only and will most likely throw America into the biggest crisis since WW2.

Finally, after what feels like forever, the phone finally begins to ring. You leap up out of the seat, almost stumbling as you lift the receiver.

"Donald."

"Bernie."

"Well, I uh....want to congratulate you on re-election, if you can call it that."

"Thank you, Vice President Sanders."

You smile after these words, it's nice to know you still have a small ledge up on the guy, even if the situation calls for you to be equals. You smile more as you sense the agitation in his voice, and wish for all the world you could be in Burlington to see his face.

"Look, we both know we didn't get on the first time round, but we have to work together. For America."

He begins to drone on about respecting democracy, but just as you think the anecdote is approaching a lecture, he finishes with some garbage about balanced authority.

Yeah right. You can barely keep the glee from your voice, but you steady yourself.

"I'll be seeing you in the White House soon Bernie. Bye for now."

After the call ends, you sink back into the chair with a sigh. You resign yourself to the fact that it's better than losing....probably. For now though, you decide to relish the last few hours of freedom before being shackled to the rock for another for years.

`); e.pages.push(`

Fear and Loathing in America '21

Against all logic and reason, it's happening again. This time, almost everybody is just stunned, tired, angered. Few are happy. Just look at Pelosi's face when a reporter asks her about abolishing the electoral college (which is far more popular now), or Schumer sleep-walking through questions about the impending contingent vote with a barrage of "no comments".

Everyone knows how this will probably go down. Faced with Trump or Bernie, every Republican will go for Trump, even if some wackos make noise about forcing some concessions from him. In the Senate, things get complicated. The vote operates as normal, which means a filibuster could leave things open to practically a congressional coup d'etat, as it is agreed the Republican Speaker would take the vice-presidency. To win the war, Democrats have settled on an unusual strategy for this time - continuity. There are reports the Dems plan on electors voting for Bernie as veep, pushing him into the contingent election, where the Senate could vote him in. Dem leadership see this as their best strategy, for an even stranger reason - Trump is fine with Bernie, telling a reporter he'd be "okay" with letting Sanders back on the ship, and stating he believed they should vote on it, therefore undercutting a filibuster.

This absurd crisis has motivated an attempt to abolish the electoral college (no comments from the GOP leadership, although Trump said he was tired of the EC), and now it's reached a new low where the most likely outcome is Trump / Sanders, again. A billionaire, and a revolutionary for a full eight years.

`); e.pages.push(`

A House Divided In Half: The Story of Trump and Bernie's Second Term

Sanders was tired. He had no particular desire to run in 2024, so him remaining in the spot was motivated only out of a desire to stop Trump. It became clear this wouldn't happen. He had been ignored almost fully in debates over policies and the final straw came in Ukraine.

In his view, Trump's aides had poisoned the relationship, had cut off his access to Trump in-order to better manipulate the President. It had started with the failure of electoral reforms, the sheer disunity of the two's effort, He was no longer invited, no longer informed, and it wasn't long until Trump flailed around in Ukraine, ping-ponging from severe military operations that would possibly start World War 3, to horrifying one-sided negotiations that would humiliate Ukraine. When Sanders spoke out, Trump raged, and promptly had his office removed from the White House. He had angered the president over his public shaming of Trump's policies, telling reporters he was firmly opposed to Trump's conduct, and ever since he had been seen as a "burden" to the administration. The President had toyed around with sending Jared Kushner to "fire" Sanders, plotting some way to humiliate him and remove him from office. Bernie was tired of it all. He wanted to go home, and he wanted to potentially land a death-blow on the Trump administration, especially with the new Democratic senate having to confirm his successor.

Weaver was convinced Sanders had to go to keep his principles, Shakir was sure Sanders had to resign to slay Trump. The decision was debated, but it was inevitable.

The book will be released nation-wide in stores October 21st.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20204830.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20150435.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20171000.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "I Can't Decide", "Scissor Sisters", "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/1/19/Scissor_sisters_-_ta_dah_cover.jpg", "buYrBbwyCGE" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] >= 259 && quickstats[0] < 269 && donald == 1) { // Close loss e.pages.push(`

"We're gonna fight like hell."

It was tense in Mar-A-Lago. Unnaturally so. While you and your team had been internally preparing for this possibility; a race to the wire with your former Vice President which would be decided by thousands, not millions of votes. More than ever, large swathes of the nation left largely out of the conversation, with efforts focused onto a handful of states. You should've been prepared for this feeling. And yet it still grips you, growing tighter and tighter as the night goes on, calling the safe states....then the leans.....the tilts.....and then......

betrayal.

The sickly grin on the supposedly 'unbiased' CNN reporter's face says it all. Not even Caesar faced such a tragic betrayal by those around him. The greatest conspiracy since Obama's 'landslide', perhaps ever. The room goes totally silent. You could hear a pin drop, a heart stop, or even the silent resolution to fight on. You know you won. We know you won. The people gave you the mandate to lead them to greatness. But **they** conspired to bring you down. You slowly rise from the chair you were slumped in, face totally pale. You can barely talk, but you force the words out, a stuttering, spit-fuelled torrent, pure hatred directed at anyone willing to listen.

A small, totally irrelevant advisor steps forward, seemingly recoiling before he even starts to speak. "Mr....uh...President."

You turn ever so slightly toward him, beady eyes locked on. You go perfectly still as he stutters on, save for a single, blonde eyebrow slowly rising.

"Shall I put...Vice President Sanders on the li-?"

He doesn't even finish, the presented phone smacked from his hand in a flash, his stunned expression quickly replaced by total fear, as you advance toward him.

"HOW DARE YOU?"

He tries to backpeddle, stammering, trying to use any excuse he can think of. A joke! No, satire! Or perhaps a cruel attempt at manipulation? All are easily dismissed as you close the final steps, the rage now solely focused on him.

"Listen to me you worthless runt. We are going to fight, alright? We are going to go as far as we have to, for as long as we to. And we will not stop. We're going to win. For the good of America. DO YOU UNDERSTAND ME?"

He seems to melt in front of you, the very embodiment of the weakness that lead to this treasonous plot against you, the rightful claimant of the Oval Office.

With a roar, you dismiss the irrelevancy in front of you, and storm toward the stage. This wasn't over, not by a long shot.

`); e.pages.push(`

"STOP THE STEAL"

Trump surveyed the crowd. The turnout, stunning, really incredible, they were chanting, stomping, screaming,

"STOP THE STEAL! STOP THE STEAL! STOP THE STEAL!"

It was right, they stole it from me, very horrible people, you know who they are, he had to give the orders, had to give them the power to stop this fraud, save America.

"This election, frankly was not free, was not fair, many people tell me it was the most fraudulent election, ever. Can't let it happen, we have to Stop the Steal, because they stole it from us, and we know who they are, we have to fight back, get at them."

These were his people, real patriots, like the ones in the Revolution, overthrowing the monarchy, now they had a bigger task, not to create America, but to make it great again, they had to overthrow the modern day kings, frankly.

"We have to really, throw these votes out, because we can't let Judas be president. We won't let it happen, folks, won't. Get in there, and fight like hell, or we're not gonna have a country anymore. Very fine patriots, all of you, have to spread out everywhere, like the patriots in the Revolutionary War, and fight, to take back control."

In the corner of his eye, he saw a man with some picture of Bernie, right in the crosshairs, didn't condone it, didn't condemn it, sometimes these are just the things that happen when you betray a great movement, sad but it happens.

"They're gonna try to certify this fraud, make it real, can't happen, it's in Washington D.C they tell me, January 6th, we have to fight this, because we are a free country, we don't want Stalin to be president."

The shot heard around the world.

`); e.pages.push(`

DONALD TRUMP JR. NOMINATED BY REPUBLICAN PARTY

Following a contentious primary against Nikki Haley and Ron DeSantis (who eventually endorsed him), Trump Jr. has won the Republican nomination.

Trump Jr. gave a victory speech, focusing on the "need" for "justice and retribution" to save America, pledging to free his father from prison, and to lock up immigrants, drug dealers and liberal billionaires like George Soros instead, who he said had poisoned the nation, and had stolen the 2020 election for "Judas" (President Bernie Sanders).

Beyond that, he pledged to "end wokeness" by ending affirmative action programs, encouraging state-wide bans on abortions (tailored to the needs of every state, as he said), and create a "stronger America" by getting aggressive with adversaries such as China and Cuba, pledging further tariffs and sanctions, while also pressuring European allies to fall in line, and providing aid to Israel to act as a "bulwark" in the Middle East.

President Sanders declined to comment, stating he was focused on the economy and providing better conditions for all Americans.

While the Republican base is largely enthusiastic about Trump Jr, polling shows that his policies are widely unpopular, and he currently has a 7% deficit on average. His campaign stated these were the results of fake polls, and insisted a "silent majority" would come into play over the course of the campaign.

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20203813.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-04%20180451.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-04%20095839.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Revolution", "The Beatles", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-04%20192130.png", "6MbqzDm1uCo" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] >= 229 && quickstats[0] < 259 && donald == 1) { // Very close defeat e.pages.push(`

"We're gonna fight like hell."

It was tense in Mar-A-Lago. Unnaturally so. While you and your team had been internally preparing for this possibility; a race to the wire with your former Vice President which would be decided by thousands, not millions of votes. More than ever, large swathes of the nation left largely out of the conversation, with efforts focused onto a handful of states. You should've been prepared for this feeling. And yet it still grips you, growing tighter and tighter as the night goes on, calling the safe states....then the leans.....the tilts.....and then......

betrayal.

The sickly grin on the supposedly 'unbiased' CNN reporter's face says it all. Not even Caesar faced such a tragic betrayal by those around him. The greatest conspiracy since Obama's 'landslide', perhaps ever. The room goes totally silent. You could hear a pin drop, a heart stop, or even the silent resolution to fight on. You know you won. We know you won. The people gave you the mandate to lead them to greatness. But **they** conspired to bring you down. You slowly rise from the chair you were slumped in, face totally pale. You can barely talk, but you force the words out, a stuttering, spit-fuelled torrent, pure hatred directed at anyone willing to listen.

A small, totally irrelevant advisor steps forward, seemingly recoiling before he even starts to speak. "Mr....uh...President."

You turn ever so slightly toward him, beady eyes locked on. You go perfectly still as he stutters on, save for a single, blonde eyebrow slowly rising.

"Shall I put...Vice President Sanders on the li-?"

He doesn't even finish, the presented phone smacked from his hand in a flash, his stunned expression quickly replaced by total fear, as you advance toward him.

"HOW DARE YOU?"

He tries to backpeddle, stammering, trying to use any excuse he can think of. A joke! No, satire! Or perhaps a cruel attempt at manipulation? All are easily dismissed as you close the final steps, the rage now solely focused on him.

"Listen to me you worthless runt. We are going to fight, alright? We are going to go as far as we have to, for as long as we to. And we will not stop. We're going to win. For the good of America. DO YOU UNDERSTAND ME?"

He seems to melt in front of you, the very embodiment of the weakness that lead to this treasonous plot against you, the rightful claimant of the Oval Office.

With a roar, you dismiss the irrelevancy in front of you, and storm toward the stage. This wasn't over, not by a long shot.

`); e.pages.push(`

"This election, frankly was not free, was not fair, many people tell me it was the most fraudulent election, ever. Can't let it happen, we have to Stop the Steal, because they stole it from us, and we know who they are, we have to fight back, get at them."

These were his people, real patriots, like the ones in the Revolution, overthrowing the monarchy, now they had a bigger task, not to create America, but to make it great again, they had to overthrow the modern day kings, frankly.

"We have to really, throw these votes out, because we can't let Judas be president. We won't let it happen, folks, won't. Get in there, and fight like hell, or we're not gonna have a country anymore. DONALD TRUMP JR. NOMINATED BY REPUBLICAN PARTY

Following a contentious primary against Nikki Haley and Ron DeSantis (who eventually endorsed him), Trump Jr. has won the Republican nomination.

Trump Jr. gave a victory speech, focusing on the "need" for "justice and retribution" to save America, pledging to free his father from prison, and to lock up immigrants, drug dealers and liberal billionaires like George Soros instead, who he said had poisoned the nation, and had stolen the 2020 election for "Judas" (President Bernie Sanders).

Beyond that, he pledged to "end wokeness" by ending affirmative action programs, encouraging state-wide bans on abortions (tailored to the needs of every state, as he said), and create a "stronger America" by getting aggressive with adversaries such as China and Cuba, pledging further tariffs and sanctions, while also pressuring European allies to fall in line, and providing aid to Israel to act as a "bulwark" in the Middle East.

President Sanders declined to comment, stating he was focused on the economy and providing better conditions for all Americans.
