`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-03-26%20203813.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-04%20180451.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-04%20095839.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Revolution", "The Beatles", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-04%20192130.png", "6MbqzDm1uCo" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] >= 191 && quickstats[0] < 229 && donald == 1) { // Realistic loss e.pages.push(`

Sanders Emerges victorious amid the “National Divorce.”

The mood in Mar-A-Lago was one of boiling resentment. As the margin grew tighter and tighter with each passing hour. The red wave was coming, he was assured.

But it never arrived.

By 1 AM, the election was finally called for Sanders. The vast majority fury in Mar-A-Lago was emanating from one man. Staffers rushed through the halls of the grand building, now seeming ever-larger to Donald J. Trump. The pundits, the networks who doubted you against Clinton four years ago, felt vindicated. They doubted you before and they did until the very end. Your supporters waited at the gates for your victory, but it didn’t come to pass. Across social media they cried foul, claiming all kinds of reasons that Sanders isn’t on the phone conceding right now. It was so clear now.

Those fucking Democrat governors must’ve stuffed the ballots! A fraud on the national scale throughout all the swing states! Christ, now Bernie’s communistic agenda could be passed… The President orders his advisors and staffers out, before stumbling into a utility closet. He talks to himself, muttering about the “greatest fraud the nation has ever seen.” Yes, he can get the governors to throw the results out, and get the votes removed, another contingent, one he is sure to win, yes, yes! He was the real winner, and everyone would know it!

This isn’t the end.

He is going to fight like hell, to save the nation.

`); e.pages.push(`
January 6th, 2021

It was inevitable. Trump stood in front of his army, as a great general, assembling them for a great purpose, he just had to give the speech like Patton did.

"We didn't lose, frankly we did not lose, because the Democrats, their governors, it was horrible, they stole it. They took everything away from me, from you, we can't let it happen."

Roars came up from the crowd, they were angry, angry for him, for his victory that was stolen. He knew timing, very well, just had to deliver the blow.

"It was disgusting, very disgusting. Really, the greatest fraud, in the history of this country, unbelievable... And they say, I'm still the President, not going, lots of power."

His troops, ready for the fight, but so were they, had to deliver something Big. With power. The power that had been invested in him.

"As your President, I am ordering the governors, of Nevada, Arizona, Georgia, North Carolina, Florida, Ohio, Pennsylvania, Wisconsin and Michigan to throw out these horrible results, the fraudulent votes, we have to protect our democracy, save America, Make America Great Again, at any cost."

They were scared, he could tell, the Republican governors, they'd fight for him if they knew what was good for them, the Democrats, very weak, his people would force their hand if they didn't. It'd be great, another contingent election, another victory, for Donald J. Trump.

As the crowd raged and stomped, he knew he'd never lose again. Never.

`); e.pages.push(`
Wall Street Journal - June 7th, 2026

Donald Trump pleads guilty to crimes committed on Epstein’s island.

Around 3 hours ago, former President Donald John Trump pled guilty to conspiracy to commit sex trafficking, money laundering, along with other miscellaneous misdemeanors before a state court. He awaits his sentencing in South Bay Correctional center, in isolation.

After the verdict, Trump was taken to a temporary holding cell to have his mugshot taken and to allow for state authorities to sign off on his transfer to federal rangers. His mugshot, which has already begun circulating the internet to great acclaim, shows a defeated Trump looking just to the side of the camera lens.

In the wake of the announcement, Former President Bernie Sanders denied any knowledge or involvement in Trump’s illicit dealings during his time as Vice President; however, that hasn't stopped some from drawing parallels between him and Former President George H. W. Bush.

As the country reels from the series of high-profile arrests towards Epstein Island visitors, the White House released this press statement regarding the situation:

“Donald Trump’s actions are completely morally reprehensible. We stand fully by the law, and remain certain that justice will prevail. We also want to take the time to say that Donald Trump’s past allegiances do not represent, in any way, shape, or form, the current trajectory of the Republican Party. We are committed to working overtime with Congress, as well as the Attorney Generals of the various states, to issue arrest warrants for all those criminally implicated in the 2024 release of the Epstein files, regardless of political affiliations.

We thank you for the trust you've given us. God bless you, and God bless the United States of America.

OFFICE OF THE PRESIDENT;

-President Youngkin and first lady Schulze”

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/carnage.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-04%20180451.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-04%20180009.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "Perfect Situation", "Weezer", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-04%20192601.png", "Mpp3vUZKuzc" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 134 && quickstats[0] < 191 && donald == 1) { // Landslide loss e.pages.push(`

Incumbent decisively repudiated amid “National Divorce

The mood in Mar-A-Lago was tense. Staffers rushed through the halls of the grand building, as beads of sweat rolled down the faces of Miller and Bannon, they all saw it coming. But there was one man who knew better. Donald J. Trump awaited the results of his surefire re-election. Entire hours came and went, as the color slowly drained from the faces of his advisors. No no, this isn’t right, surely. By 10 PM, it was all over. One by one, the networks called the election for Sanders. In a decisive manner, too.

A loss could be chalked up to the Democrat governors stuffing ballots, but this? A fraud on the national scale, even in red states, they had embedded their footsoldiers in the highest levels of state government. Ensuring not only an unfair victory for Sanders, but an unfair mandate for him to pass all his communist policies. Trump stumbles into a utility closet, he talks to himself, muttering about the “greatest fraud the nation has ever seen.” Yes, he can get the governors to throw the results out, and get the votes removed, another contingent, one he is sure to win, yes, yes! He was the real winner, and everyone would know it!

A staffer opens the door, holding a phone.

“Mr. President?”

The President composes himself.

“It’s… Him.”

“Put him on hold. I need to talk to the people.”

`); e.pages.push(`
Death of the MAGA movement

The shriveled man hurried on stage, dampened up by the pouring of rain. He knew his people needed him, he knew that the country wasn't done with him yet, he knew that there was still time to salvage this.

He knew there was still time to win.

Grabbing a hold of the podium, he clamped onto it with such force to turn his knuckles white. He felt a redness in his face, but he didn't pay it any mind. He forced himself not to.

“My great patriots!” - he blurted out - “this is our great test! We've been given such a bad, horrible hand by the cooked democrats. They- they've actually stuffed ballot boxes with, with stolen ballots! And- and they've… They've thrown out…”

This wasn't what Donald was used to. By now, he should have been interrupted by the cheer of his people, giving him time to think his speech through. This time there was no break and no reprieve.

And so he turned to face them: bleak, dull, grey faces wore hats whose color had long ran out. There was no more room left for anger, nor fight, nor cheer. It was then that he saw the one emotion he most feared.

Disappointment.

His base, his people, his world had become completely disappointed in him. Just like Father. In each of their eyes, he saw Father's gaze.

Why did you do that, Donnie? Team up with some wacko hippie? How didja think it was gonna end up?

Donald held the podium firmly, not to project strength, but to prevent him from swooning.

You had a helluva chance, son. A helluva chance and you blew it.

It was not his fault! It was not his fault, damnit! Those kids… those assholes threw the goddamn fastballs too fucking fast! He tried his best! It's not his fault… please, it's not his fault.

More and more water pooled up on Donald’s face, more water than it was falling from the sky, as a matter of fact.

But I wouldn't expect anything less from you. Whiny bastard you turned out to be. You got that from your half-broad of a mother, not me.

But he-

Shut your trap and concede already. You've embarrassed me enough.

In the pouring rain, Donald John Trump stepped away from the podium and awkwardly got off the stage, accompanied only by mild chattering of the crowd and the beating of the sky’s years.

In the pouring rain, Donnie died standing.

`); e.pages.push(`
WILL SANDERS RUN AGAIN?

The past four years have been incredibly successful for Sanders - following a landslide victory in 2020, he used his new-found political capital to pass universal healthcare, make a wide variety of COVID-era policies permanent (most contained within the CARES act, although some state-wide policies have inspired new nation-wide ones), pass the PRO act, and pushing for another white whale accomplishment as the Green New Deal slowly goes through Congress. His accomplishments have meant that he maintains a 52% approval rating even as war continues in Ukraine and inflation heats up, although it has decreased from his 58% approval rating in November 2022.

But President Sanders has declined to comment on whether he's running in 2024, a question growing due to his denials and his age, as he is already the oldest president in U.S history. If he declines to run, his vice-president would be the frontrunner, with most experts predicting a coronation and no serious prinary challenge.

Nomination Tracker

Democratic Party

Bernie Sanders - Uncommitted, Lock (if running)

Republican Party

Marco Rubio - Entered, Frontrunner

Ted Cruz - Entered, Close Behind

Ron DeSantis - Entered, Slipping Trumpist

Nikki Haley - Entered, Gasping For Air

Doug Burgum - Entered, Obscure

Donald Trump Jr. - Not Entered, Tied To His Father

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/carnage.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/carnage.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20195222.png'; }]); if (typeof window.playEndingSong === "function") { window.playEndingSong( "It Wasn't Me (Trump Cover)", "PoliticsJOE", "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-04%20190132.png", "epvLrK6Mhd4" ); } } else if (quickstats[0] > 2 && quickstats[0] <= 134 && donald == 1) { // Ridiculous loss e.pages.push(`

The mood in Mar-A-Lago was tense. Staffers rushed through the halls of the grand building, as beads of sweat rolled down the faces of Miller and Bannon, they all saw it coming. A loss could be chalked up to the Democrat governors stuffing ballots, but this? A fraud on the national scale, even in red states, they had embedded their footsoldiers in the highest levels of state government. Ensuring not only an unfair victory for Sanders, but an unfair mandate for him to pass all his communist policies. Trump stumbles into a utility closet, he talks to himself, muttering about the “greatest fraud the nation has ever seen.” Yes, he can get the governors to throw the results out, and get the votes removed, another contingent, one he is sure to win, yes, yes! He was the real winner, and everyone would know it!

A staffer opens the door, holding a phone.

“Mr. President?”

The President composes himself.

“It’s… Him.”

“Put him on hold. I need to talk to the people.”

“My great patriots!” - he blurted out - “this is our great test! We've been given such a bad, horrible hand by the cooked democrats. They- they've actually stuffed ballot boxes with, with stolen ballots! And- and they've… They've thrown out…”

This wasn't what Donald was used to. By now, he should have been interrupted by the cheer of his people, giving him time to think his speech through. This time there was no break and no reprieve.

And so he turned to face them: bleak, dull, grey faces wore hats whose color had long ran out. There was no more room left for anger, nor fight, nor cheer. It was then that he saw the one emotion he most feared.

Disappointment.

His base, his people, his world had become completely disappointed in him. Just like Father. In each of their eyes, he saw Father's gaze.

Why did you do that, Donnie? Team up with some wacko hippie? How didja think it was gonna end up?

Donald held the podium firmly, not to project strength, but to prevent him from swooning.

You had a helluva chance, son. A helluva chance and you blew it.

It was not his fault! It was not his fault, damnit! Those kids… those assholes threw the goddamn fastballs too fucking fast! He tried his best! It's not his fault… please, it's not his fault.

More and more water pooled up on Donald’s face, more water than it was falling from the sky, as a matter of fact.

But I wouldn't expect anything less from you. Whiny bastard you turned out to be. You got that from your half-broad of a mother, not me.

But he-

Shut your trap and concede already. You've embarrassed me enough.

In the pouring rain, Donald John Trump stepped away from the podium and awkwardly got off the stage, accompanied only by mild chattering of the crowd and the beating of the sky’s years.

In the pouring rain, Donnie died standing.

`); e.pages.push(`
WILL SANDERS RUN AGAIN?

The past four years have been incredibly successful for Sanders - following a landslide victory in 2020, he used his new-found political capital to pass universal healthcare, make a wide variety of COVID-era policies permanent (most contained within the CARES act, although some state-wide policies have inspired new nation-wide ones), pass the PRO act, and pushing for another white whale accomplishment as the Green New Deal slowly goes through Congress. His accomplishments have meant that he maintains a 52% approval rating even as war continues in Ukraine and inflation heats up, although it has decreased from his 58% approval rating in November 2022.

But President Sanders has declined to comment on whether he's running in 2024, a question growing due to his denials and his age, as he is already the oldest president in U.S history. If he declines to run, his vice-president would be the frontrunner, with most experts predicting a coronation and no serious prinary challenge.

Nomination Tracker

Democratic Party

Bernie Sanders - Uncommitted, Lock (if running)

Republican Party

Marco Rubio - Entered, Frontrunner

Ted Cruz - Entered, Close Behind

Ron DeSantis - Entered, Slipping Trumpist

Nikki Haley - Entered, Gasping For Air

Doug Burgum - Entered, Obscure

Donald Trump Jr. - Not Entered, Tied To His Father

`); e.executable.push([0, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/carnage.png'; }]); e.executable.push([1, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/carnage.png'; }]); e.executable.push([2, () => { e.image = 'https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/Screenshot%202026-04-02%20195222.png'; } if (split == 1) { unlockAchievement("Well, That Didn't Last Long") } return construct(0) } // Tooltips - KEEP THIS AT THE BOTTOM OR THE TOOLTIPS WON'T WORK - tmc function getTooltips(str) { let matches = []; tooltipList.forEach((tooltip, index) => { // Adjust the regex to match searchString potentially surrounded by “ and followed by optional punctuation let regex = new RegExp(`(?<=\\b|\\s|^|“)${tooltip.searchString}(?=[.,;!?]?\\b|\\s|”|$)`, 'g'); let match; while ((match = regex.exec(str)) !== null) { matches.push({ start: match.index + (match[0].startsWith('“') ? 1 : 0), // Adjust for potential starting “ end: match.index + match[0].length - (match[0].endsWith('”') ? 1 : 0) - (match[2] ? 1 : 0), tooltipIndex: index }); } }); // Sort by starting position; if two start at the same position, longer match comes first matches.sort((a, b) => a.start - b.start || b.end - b.start - (a.end - a.start)); // Filter out overlaps for (let i = 0; i < matches.length - 1; ) { if (matches[i + 1].start < matches[i].end) { matches.splice(i + 1, 1); // Remove the next match since it overlaps } else { i++; // Move to next match } } return matches; } function applyTooltips(str) { const matches = getTooltips(str); let result = ''; let lastIndex = 0; matches.forEach(match => { const tooltip = tooltipList[match.tooltipIndex]; result += str.slice(lastIndex, match.start); result += `${tooltip.searchString}${tooltip.explanationText}`; lastIndex = match.end; }); result += str.slice(lastIndex); // Add the remainder of the original string return result; } function applyTooltipsToObject(obj) { for (let key in obj) { if (typeof obj[key] === 'string') { obj[key] = applyTooltips(obj[key]); } else if (typeof obj[key] === 'object') { applyTooltipsToObject(obj[key]); // Recursive call } } } applyTooltipsToObject(campaignTrail_temp.questions_json); applyTooltipsToObject(campaignTrail_temp.answers_json); applyTooltipsToObject(campaignTrail_temp.answer_feedback_json); class ChartEnhancer { constructor() { this.isProcessing = false; this.initializeStyles(); this.initializeReplacements(); this.init(); } initializeStyles() { if (document.getElementById('ce-tooltip-styles')) return; const style = document.createElement('style'); style.id = 'ce-tooltip-styles'; style.textContent = ` .ce-tooltip-container { position: relative; cursor: help; display: inline-block; opacity: 1; transition-delay: 0.5s; border-bottom: 1px dotted blue; } .ce-tooltip { visibility: hidden; opacity: 0; pointer-events: none; transition: opacity 0.3s ease, visibility 0s 0.3s; position: absolute; top: 50%; right: 100%; transform: translateY(-50%); margin-right: 10px; z-index: 1; background-color: #e07645; color: #fff; font-family: Segoe UI, sans-serif !important; padding: 10px; border-radius: 6px; box-sizing: border-box; width: 200px; text-align: center; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 8px; } .ce-tooltip-container:hover .ce-tooltip { /* Make visible on hover */ visibility: visible; opacity: 1; /* Hover-specific styles */ pointer-events: auto; transition-delay: 0s; background-color: #141f38; background-size: cover; font-family: Segoe UI, sans-serif !important; font-style: normal !important; box-shadow: 0px 4px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1); } .ce-tooltip img { display: block; max-width: 85%; height: auto; margin: 0; } `; document.head.appendChild(style); } createTooltip(text, { img, title, name, desc }) { const imgHtml = img ? `` : ''; const titleHtml = title ? `${title}
` : ''; const nameHtml = name ? `${name}
` : ''; const descHtml = desc ? `${desc}` : ''; return `${text}` + `${imgHtml}` + `${titleHtml}${nameHtml}${descHtml}` + ``; } initializeReplacements() { this.replacements = new Map([ ["Trump", this.createTooltip("Trump", { img: "https://file.garden/aPF6sdg4jk-629YB/TrumpCaricature.jpg", title: "President", name: "Donald J. => { this.isProcessing = false; }, 100); } init() { const checkAndProcess = () => { this.processChart(); }; this.intervalId = setInterval(() => { requestAnimationFrame(checkAndProcess); }, 200); }
